Batch triage works on a fixture set. xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(triage)' green.
notes:
2026-07-06 dedup: absorbed sinex-7z0.2 (same deliverable, earlier framing). Unique design points merged from 7z0.2: (a) queue ordering = evidence strength x staleness (both measured, surfaced in the row per standing rule 1) — reconcile with this bead's confidence x volume into one measured ordering; (b) defer is a first-class verdict so the queue can actually reach zero; (c) batch judgment = ONE judgment event with N proposal parents (legal via NonEmptyVec); (d) when the semantic plane activates (5v6/2nd), an unworkable judgment UX means L4 silently stops mattering — the urgency argument. 7z0.2 superseded by this bead.
2026-07-06 code targets: the server half already exists — handle_curation_list_proposals + handle_curation_record_judgment (crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/curation.rs:30,:48; wire types sinex_primitives::rpc::curation). The bead's real scope is the OPERATOR surface: sinexctl triage command / TUI pane / MCP write tool driving those two RPCs, plus queue ordering + batch judgment. Start by reading the existing RPC contract before adding any new server method.
2026-07-06 adjudication-under-depletion design addendum (analysis #5 — the doctrine/north-star collision): adjudication is the ONLY path to truth, but the AuDHD constraint forbids daily ritual and the surface must work when depleted, else L4 silently dies (the Perkeep consumption-death pattern). Design consequences, binding: (1) NO standing queue-zero expectation — the queue is allowed to grow; staleness is rendered honestly, never as guilt (no counts-as-nag, no red badges); (2) judgment sessions are OPT-IN MOODS — the operator opens the seat when they have energy; batch-by-homogeneity makes one good session clear hundreds (accept-all-of-kind with spot-check sampling); (3) DEFER is first-class and FREE — deferring costs one keystroke and never resurfaces as pressure; (4) delegated-policy scope (DeterministicPolicy actor) handles the mechanical tail so the human only sees genuinely ambiguous items — the queue ORDERING optimizes for judgment-per-keystroke, not FIFO; (5) depletion-mode rendering: a low-energy view showing 3 items max with large targets, no dense tables; (6) the seat measures its own cost (time-per-judgment as an event) so the burden claim is falsifiable. If judged-proposal volume stays ~zero for weeks, that is a signal to gate proposal-producing lanes down (vfy/pq5), not to nag the operator.

title: Derivation control plane: product-classed lanes, epochs, declarations, and promotion
description: Sinex already built a derivation control plane and wired it to ONE lane. crate/sinex-primitives/src/semantic.rs: SemanticEpochRecord (versioned regime: config_hash, components, prompt_set_hash, model_config_hash), SemanticLaneRecord with SemanticLaneKind {Canonical, Shadow, Experiment} + SemanticLaneStatus lifecycle {Planned..Promoted/Discarded/Expired}, EntityRelationDiffReport (diff two lanes). curation.rs + authority.rs: CurationProposalPayload -> CurationJudgmentPayload -> CurationFinalizedPayload over the shared authority seam (Proposal<T>/Judgment, FinalizerRegistration::human_required), with the doctrine in code: confidence cannot become authority. All of it serves only the entity/relation lane. Generalizing it to every automaton SUBSUMES four otherwise-separate needs (evidence-tier carriage, judgment workflow, blue-green derivation regimes, promotion gate) into proven machinery. CORRECTION vs draft: CurationJudgmentActorKind is {User, Operator, DeterministicPolicy, TestFixture} — there is NO agent actor kind today; agent-recorded judgments need an explicit actor-kind extension (or agents act as Operator with actor_id attribution) — that is a design point of this epic, not an existing fact.
design: Lift SemanticLaneRecord from entity-scoped to derivation-generic: (1) audit SemanticScope kinds (source_material/event_set/document_chunk_set are batch-shaped; continuous automata need a stream/checkpoint scope kind — record what does not lift); (2) every AutomatonSpec binds a lane + epoch (semantics_version becomes the epoch's component version); (3) generalize EntityRelationDiffReport to a LaneDiffReport over any output kind (keep the entity/relation one as its first instance); (4) promotion path: candidate lane -> curation proposal -> judgment -> finalized flips SemanticLaneStatus to Promoted. (5) Actor-kind decision: extend CurationJudgmentActorKind with Agent (attributed) or formalize agents-as-Operator-with-actor_id; pick ONE and record it. Prove generality by lifting a SECOND lane (sessionization) before claiming done. PITFALL: entity/relation diff semantics (EntityRelationDiffCounts/Examples) may not generalize — LaneDiffReport must be output-kind-generic with typed per-kind examples.
acceptance_criteria: A generic derivation schema exists for product declarations, epochs, lanes, lane outputs, and lane diffs; semantic lanes are ported or wrapped without becoming an authority path. Every registered automaton has a static DerivationOutputDeclaration with DerivedProductClass, semantics_version, write surface, input eligibility, default support, and verification command. Sessionization runs as a registered lane/epoch; a shadow session lane diffs against live via generic LaneDiffReport and promotes through curation judgment/finalizer. DB/runtime gates reject undeclared derived-event writes and finalizer bypasses.
notes:
2026-07-06 symbol map: the control plane to generalize = SemanticEpochRecord/SemanticLaneRecord/SemanticLaneKind{Canonical,Shadow,Experiment}/SemanticLaneStatus (primitives/semantic.rs:27-87) + repo create_epoch/create_lane/list/set_lane_status/discard_lane_outputs (sinex-db/repositories/semantic.rs:52-292). Current wiring is entity/relation-shaped only: write_entity_relation_outputs/seed_*/diff (:351-:575) — generalization = make lane output storage + diff pluggable per derivation kind (the interval-lift/attention/summarizer families are the next lanes). Fable correction stands: SemanticLaneKind is lane LIFECYCLE class, NOT evidence tier (8cr is separate vocabulary). CurationJudgmentActorKind has NO Agent variant — adding agent-actor judgments is a design point of THIS bead, gated on the authority seam.
2026-07-06 fold (U9, 016/02-uncovered-designs.md:142-155): the LLM-automaton failure taxonomy routes 1-4 are lane-robustness design points of THIS bead — (1) schema-invalid output -> DLQ with effect-key, never partial-parse; (2) valid-but-hallucinated -> uncatchable mechanically, hence judgment-required promotion + golden-set precision per lane; (3) nondeterminism -> effect ledger alarm on same-key-different-output, pin model+version in effect key, local via LiteLLM for replay-critical; (4) within-version drift -> monthly lane re-run on a frozen evaluation epoch, diff vs expected. Route 5 (cost blowout) has its own bead (LLM budget policy automaton).
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §20): 'derived' is too broad — adopt DerivedProductClass as a control-plane axis: canonical_derived_event | projection_row | analysis_claim | report_artifact | semantic_candidate | operator_judgment. Only canonical events + accepted claims are default-eligible inputs to further canonical derivation. Anti-handwave rule for every new derivation (audit): declare inputs, scope bound, write class, refusal states, evidence refs, invalidation triggers, why-cheaper-than-ledger, smallest demo. Storage/authority split is now doctrine in CLAUDE.md §Architecture.
2026-07-07 blueprint decision (new-gpt-pro 04-interpretation-plane-blueprint.report.md — full DDL sketches, Rust type shapes, enforcement-seam placement, migration order): do NOT turn semantic.* into a universal authority path; create derivation.* for generic control-plane mechanics; authority promotion stays in curation/authority. DerivedProductClass is a required declaration axis. Execution split into children 0vx.*/8cr.*/68c.* created from the blueprint.
2026-07-08 (post-ratification): the shadow lane now has its FIRST NAMED CLIENT — the ratified pq5-B entity-chain retirement makes 0vx the sole re-entry path (candidate lane, ClaimSupport-labeled proposals, promotion only via the authority seam). Two consequences for this beads design: (a) the "prove generality by lifting a SECOND lane" requirement is satisfiable with a real pair — sessionization (batch-shaped, already named) plus the entity chain (continuous, stream/checkpoint scope) — which is a stronger generality proof than two batch lanes, and it forces the stream/checkpoint SemanticScope kind the design already flags as missing; (b) the re-entry gate gives promotion its first end-to-end exercise: entity lane runs shadow -> LaneDiffReport vs nothing (candidate-only) -> a4w.1 golden set scores the extractor -> operator judgment promotes or leaves it discarded. The actor-kind decision (extend CurationJudgmentActorKind with Agent vs agents-as-Operator-with-actor_id) is still OPEN and still owned here — it is NOT covered by the 2026-07-08 ratification batch.

title: Interactive SLO tier: named latency budgets, continuously measured, regression-gated
description: Snappy needs numbers or it regresses silently. sinex-g3k (CLOSED, PR #2271) already fixed the diagnosed recall/event-query latency WITHOUT a projection: singleton source-equality + occurrence-order event-card listings use existing ts_orig indexes and meet interactive budget on the 69.3M-row dev store. What remains unowned is the STANDING tier: named budgets for the interactive surfaces (recall window render, event-card listing, MCP query round-trip, sinexctl cold start, ingest-to-queryable lag), continuously measured and regression-gated, so the g3k win cannot silently rot. CORRECTION vs draft: the ts_orig projection is NOT a standing need — it is the conditional escalation g3k's closure defined.
design: (1) Budgets (tune with evidence): recall window p95 interactive on the live store; event-card listing per g3k's proven numbers; MCP query round-trip; ingest-to-queryable lag for the live pipeline (source event -> queryable) reflecting the 2-5s design envelope. (2) Measurement: xtask test bench --contracts gains an interactive tier run against the dev store; regressions fail the gate. (3) ESCALATION PATH (conditional, from g3k closure notes): if a ts_orig-WINDOW workload (rollups/aggregates over occurrence time) exceeds index-served budgets, build the read model per the recorded decision — a PLAIN day-partitioned postgres table (U5 note), NOT a hypertable unless ts_orig-bucketed CAs are wanted; NEVER repartition core.events, NEVER mint UUIDv7 from ts_orig (gh#2182). PITFALL: budgets against the live-size store, not a toy fixture — g3k's evidence was 69.3M rows.
acceptance_criteria: An interactive tier exists in the perf-contracts config with named budgets. xtask test bench --contracts runs it green against the dev store and a deliberate regression trips it. The conditional projection escalation is documented in the bead/design docs with its trigger condition. Ref: g3k closure + PR #2271.
notes: 2026-07-06 folds: (a) freshness classes (lens L-iii) live on the read-model primitive (68c) — the SLO tier consumes them, budgets differ per class; (b) 019 perf lens: a result cache keyed on cursor/epoch (recall/pack answers cached, invalidated when new events pass the cursor) belongs to this bead's conditional-escalation family alongside the ts_orig projection — consider it BEFORE the projection; it is cheaper and reversible.

title: Enforce the ViewEnvelope/CaveatView contract on every read surface (no bare totals)
description: The honesty contract type ALREADY EXISTS: ViewEnvelope<T> with caveats+actions (authority.rs:178, relations.rs EvidenceWindow::into_view, views/desktop.rs) and SinexQueryResultRow.with_caveats(Vec<CaveatView>) (query_units.rs). recall emits caveats (recall.sessions.absent etc). What is MISSING is coverage and enforcement: other read surfaces (MCP counts, CLI aggregates, API handlers) can still return bare zeros/totals a reader interprets as 'nothing happened'. The truth-substrate guarantee is that an empty or partial result states WHY. CORRECTION vs draft: do not invent a ReadinessEnvelope — extend the existing ViewEnvelope/CaveatView vocabulary to a standardized readiness caveat set and enforce it everywhere.
design: (1) Standardize a readiness caveat vocabulary as CaveatView ids: source.absent, readmodel.stale_by, window.partial, coverage.unmeasurable, derivation.lane_not_promoted. (2) Sweep read surfaces (MCP tools in sinexctl mcp.rs, api handlers, sinexctl commands) so every aggregate/list response goes through ViewEnvelope or carries CaveatViews — no handler returns a bare count. (3) An enforcement check: a test (or forbidden-pattern gate) asserting read handlers construct envelopes. Source materialization state from the read-model primitive (sinex-68c) where it exists. PITFALL: one contract across ALL surfaces or it is theater — partial adoption recreates the silent-zero problem one surface over.
acceptance_criteria: The readiness caveat id vocabulary is defined in primitives. Every MCP read tool and sinexctl query surface returns envelope+caveats; a test enumerates handlers and fails on bare-total returns. An empty recall window names its cause via standard caveat ids. xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(readiness)' green.

2026-07-03 second slice: standardized sinexctl recall empty-window/source-gap caveats on the shared ReadinessCaveatId vocabulary. recall missing session detector now emits derivation.lane_not_promoted; active expected sources with coverage but no events in the recall window emit window.partial; absent/degraded expected source coverage emits source.absent; source coverage gaps emit coverage.unmeasurable while preserving source-specific detail in messages/refs. Verification: xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(readiness)' job 2001428 passed; xtask check -p sinexctl job 2001429 passed. Remaining sinex-42n scope: MCP/read-surface sweep, handler enumeration/no-bare-total enforcement, and envelope/caveat coverage across other aggregate/list surfaces.

Verification: CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(llm)' job 2001432 passed; CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(llm_budget_report_empty_rows_explain_missing_producer) | test(llm_budget_report_aggregates_ledger_events)' job 2001433 passed; CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_DEV_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl -p sinexd job 2001434 passed. title: ClaimSupport vector on derived outputs, replacing scalar EvidenceTier
description: Derived events carry automaton_model/temporal_policy/semantics_version but no epistemic grade. entity.related emits co-occurrence confidences (~0.5) that nothing validates; curation.rs already states the doctrine in code: 'consumers must not treat confidence as authority' (CurationProposalPayload.confidence doc). A tier vocabulary (mechanical-certain | heuristic | llm-judgment | adjudicated) makes every derived output self-declare its grade, and gates promotion: only adjudicated tier is truth. Retro-fix: relabel the dark entity chain's output tier:heuristic so its non-consumption becomes CORRECT (candidates awaiting judgment) rather than false knowledge. CORRECTION vs draft: SemanticLaneKind is {Canonical,Shadow,Experiment} — a lane LIFECYCLE class, orthogonal to epistemic grade (a canonical lane can emit heuristic outputs). EvidenceTier is its own vocabulary; do NOT conflate.
design: New enum EvidenceTier in sinex-primitives (mechanical_certain | heuristic | llm_judgment | adjudicated), carried in the derived-event metadata alongside automaton_model/semantics_version. Promotion to adjudicated happens ONLY via the existing authority seam (authority.rs Proposal<T>/Judgment; curation.proposal -> curation.judgment -> curation.finalized) — no parallel gate. Each automaton declares its tier in AutomatonSpec; read surfaces render tier via the established ViewEnvelope/CaveatView pattern (a heuristic row carries a visible tier, never presented as fact). PITFALL: tier is per-OUTPUT-KIND per lane epoch, not per-lane-kind. PITFALL: adjudicated tier requires a referenced curation.judgment event id — tier claims without judgment provenance are invalid by construction.
acceptance_criteria: ClaimSupport exists in primitives with support_level, source_coverage, temporal_quality, adjudication, and count fields. DerivedOutput carries ClaimSupport and DerivedProductClass; every registered automaton stamps both. Adjudicated support states are constructible only through the authority seam and require a judgment event id at validation and DB levels. No read surface renders semantic_candidate or analysis_claim rows without support-vector caveats; UI badges derive from the vector, never stored instead of it.
notes:
2026-07-06 symbol map: EvidenceTier does not exist anywhere yet (rg = 0 hits) — greenfield vocabulary. Insertion point: DerivedOutput<T> (runtime/automaton/output.rs:34) is the carrier every automaton already returns; it flows into the synthesized-event metadata columns (automaton_model/temporal_policy/semantics_version live on core.events as the 6 nullable synthesized columns, defs/events.rs:44-57 rationale comment). Adding a tier = new enum in primitives (domain or semantic module), a field + builder method on DerivedOutput (pattern: with_semantics_version output.rs:163), a nullable column on core.events + reflection.events via schema apply, threading through the derived-insert paths in sinex-db persistence, and stamping by each automaton (mechanical-certain for canonicalizer/rollups/interval-lift; heuristic for entity/relation co-occurrence). Fable corrections binding: tier is NOT SemanticLaneKind; promotion between tiers happens ONLY via the authority.rs Proposal/Judgment seam — the enum should make adjudicated unrepresentable except through that path (e.g. constructor privacy).
2026-07-07 audit integration (adversarial audit §3): scalar tier is too weak — two 'medium' claims can differ wildly (direct shell/git evidence vs LLM guess from a vague title). Adopt a claim_support VECTOR: support_level {direct|convergent|heuristic|self_report|model_inferred|unsupported} × source_coverage {covered|partial|unknown|unavailable|redacted} × temporal_quality (ladder rung) × adjudication {unreviewed|accepted|rejected|superseded} + counterevidence_count + support_family_count. UI may collapse to a badge; storage must not. First slice: ClaimSupportView DTO in context-pack output before any schema change. Temporal-quality propagation (a4w.3.12) feeds the temporal axis.
2026-07-07 blueprint decision: scalar EvidenceTier is obsolete — store the ClaimSupport VECTOR (support_level x source_coverage x temporal_quality x adjudication + evidence counts); payload-local confidence stays informational only. Consistent with the standing doctrine that confidence cannot become authority (authority.rs seam).
2026-07-08 (post-ratification): the ClaimSupport vector now has its FIRST concrete labeling target and its first refusal case. Target: the co-occurrence relation rows (0.5 confidence, empty source refs — cmy) become DerivedProductClass=semantic_candidate with an explicit ClaimSupport (support_level=heuristic, source_coverage from the actual referenced events, temporal_quality from ts_orig provenance, adjudication=none). Refusal case: PRE-EXISTING rows written before the vector exists have NO support vector — they must render as unknown-support, never be backfilled with a synthesized value (that would fabricate exactly the authority cmy identifies). Consumers: k6s (query surface, reframed as the pq5 re-entry evidence half) renders the vector; 0vx shadow lane carries it. Ordering: this bead makes the pq5-B retirement CORRECT rather than merely quiet — retired-but-unlabeled rows are still dishonest if a read surface ever touches them.

title: Interval-lifting primitive: one transition-to-interval automaton over N sources
description: attention.stream (a4w.3.1) and machine-context overlays need time-joins over intervals, but capture is points/transitions and only sessionization lifts intervals, bespoke. Evidence: wm.hyprland emits 16 transition kinds (window.opened..window.closed) with no lifted window-life or focus-span; systemd unit.started/stopped, dbus power.state_changed, activitywatch afk.changed are all uncaptured intervals. One reusable lifter unlocks the entire time-join family as a single mechanism (algebra in mechanisms, not instances).
notes:
2026-07-04 follow-up sinex-f8b closed: live bridge delivered later focus rows; missing interval was false duplicate suppression from sequence-based interval_id/equivalence_key. Interval identity now uses state_kind + subject + start/end parent ids, matching SourceContract occurrence_identity. Fixed live proof artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/interval-lift-live-bridge-fixed-20260704T0721Z. Remaining sinex-8y8 scope: generalize beyond Hyprland focus into the N-source transition-to-interval primitive.

2026-07-04 ActivityWatch interval-lift slice: interval-lift now handles two source rules through one generic StateObservation/StateIntervalPayload path. Hyprland focus remains adjacent-transition based; ActivityWatch window.active now lifts observed duration_ms directly into desktop.activitywatch.window intervals with single-parent evidence and parent-id equivalence keys. Legacy focus checkpoint state now migrates into the new generic observation shape instead of restart-looping (job 2001552 exposed missing state_kind; regression added). Verification: xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(interval_lift)' job 2001556 passed; xtask check -p sinexd job 2001557 passed. Live proof: runtime job 2001559 bound sinex-interval-lift-confirmed-events-material-filter-window_d_focused_or_window_d_active and, after a focus-switch interaction, produced 6 ActivityWatch-derived intervals since proof start; max source duration_ms=45714, max lifted duration_secs=1. Demo artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/activitywatch-interval-lift-live-20260704T0755Z. Remaining sinex-8y8 scope: add more transition/observed-duration rules (AFK/systemd/power/workspace) and extract any further generic rule table if duplication appears.

2026-07-04 ActivityWatch AFK interval-lift slice: interval-lift now consumes activitywatch/afk.changed and emits desktop.activitywatch.afk through the shared StateObservation/StateIntervalPayload path. Live runtime proof exposed a real open-row defect: latest AFK parent 019f2c2d-9933-7a00-a9bb-ecc6b4f54717 had duration_ms=919451; bounding by parent ts_coided produced a zero-duration derived row 019f2c37-7c84-7092-b743-fb4973d28eaa and duplicate suppression then masked corrected replays. Fixed by bounding observed-duration end_time at processing time (Timestamp::now()) and adding regression for open ActivityWatch AFK rows. Verification: forced focused test job 2001568 passed; package check job 2001569 passed. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/activitywatch-afk-interval-lift-proof-20260704T0829Z. Remaining sinex-8y8 proof gap: capture a clean fresh AFK transition after this fix, or add a first-class replay/proof command that bypasses checkpoint/equivalence-key ambiguity honestly.

The automaton now consumes systemd/unit.started and matching systemd/unit.stopped, keeps open per-subject transition state in generic active_subject_states, and emits derived.interval-lift/state.interval with state_kind=system.systemd.unit and parent-id equivalence keys. Catalog updated with the rule row. Verification: forced focused interval-lift tests job 2001572 passed; package check job 2001573 passed. title: Capture-losslessness as a standing coverage-error measure
description: r6d proves no silent loss WITHIN the pipeline; this makes CAPTURE completeness a continuous measure - per source, observable-real-world-events vs events Sinex actually holds, with the gap's evidence (journald sequence gaps, inotify overflow, cursor lag). sinex_source_coverage exists as a triage view; promote it to a standing metric so the instrument reports its own coverage error rather than assuming completeness (the losslessness doctrine).
acceptance_criteria: Formal source-coverage metric exists and is visible in sinexctl sources status/API; metric separates missing-material, missing-events, runtime-binding-unobserved, bridge-unobserved, and stale-source cases; denominator/basis-points semantics documented per source contract; at least one fixture injects each class and fails if absence is rendered as nothing-happened; live dev closing packet records current coverage_error_sources and top error reasons.
notes:
2026-07-04 first implementation slice: promoted capture-losslessness into the shared SourceCoverageSummaryView as coverage_error_sources, coverage_error_basis_points, and coverage_error_kinds computed from existing readiness/continuity/gap evidence. sinexctl sources status now renders the count/rate in the human summary; JSON/YAML envelope output carries the machine fields. Live patched dev runtime proof: sinexd.sources.status.view count=64, coverage_error_sources=58, coverage_error_basis_points=9062, reasons include gap.missing_material=58, readiness.missing_material=50, gap.missing_events=43, continuity.gapped=43. Demo artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/capture-losslessness-live-20260704T0137Z. Verification: xtask test -p sinex-primitives -E 'test(source_coverage)' job 2001468 passed; xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(source_status)' job 2001469 passed; xtask check -p sinex-primitives -p sinexctl job 2001471 passed after rerun; xtask check -p sinexd job 2001472 passed. Remaining sinex-jdp scope: source-specific external denominators such as journald sequence gaps, inotify overflow, provider cursor lag, and expected-rate models.

2026-07-04 denominator slice: promoted existing source-specific evidence into shared CoverageGapView so coverage_error_kinds now includes runtime denominator debt and test-proven provider/session/projection debt. Live patched dev runtime proof from sinexd.sources.status.view count=64: coverage_error_sources=58, coverage_error_basis_points=9062, gap.runtime_binding_unobserved=36, gap.runtime_bridge_unobserved=3, plus existing missing_material/missing_events gaps. Demo artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/capture-losslessness-denominators-live-20260704T0159Z. Verification: job 2001476 passed exact sinexd lib tests for provider failure, mailbox projection debt, ready source no-gap regression, and session control gap; job 2001477 passed xtask check -p sinexd. Operational finding: broad `xtask test -p sinexd -E test(source_status)` compiled unrelated integration binaries and was cancelled as job 2001474 after peaking near 19 GiB; rerun with --lib was correct. Remaining scope: real external denominators such as journald sequence gaps, inotify overflow, provider cursor lag, expected-rate models, plus fixing xtask target inference to prevent broad filtered test fan-out.
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §5+§6): coverage-state vocabulary: covered_zero | covered_nonzero | source_silent_but_expected | export_out_of_window | stale_export | partial_parse | privacy_redacted | unsupported_source | unknown. Denominator basis enum: ContractOnly | RuntimeBinding | UpstreamProbe | ImportedMaterialBounds | OperatorDisabled. External denominator probes for the first slice: ActivityWatch DB path/row freshness, Hyprland socket presence, Atuin DB freshness, git/Raindrop timer status. Also generate the source-capability matrix (contract_declared × runtime_binding × parser × live_collector × static_import_cmd × coverage_report × status native/partial/bridge/staged_only/schema_only/deferred) so no surface calls a source 'missing' that was never expected — catalog breadth ≠ runtime closure.

title: Top-down ETA compression: sequence the graph for a 2-3 week critical path
description: The program's wall-clock is its critical path: confirmed-delivery merge (egs) to convergence-contract to interval-lift to attention.stream (a4w.3.1) to recall-v2. Top-down bead-spec changes that cut ETA disproportionately (the polylogue quarter-to-2-3-weeks move): (1) consolidate machinery - deriv-control-plane folds four beads into one; (2) minimum-spanning demo first - ~4 beads to an undeniable artifact (min-span-demo); (3) keystones unblock many - interval-lift, deriv-control-plane, evidence-tier=lane-kind, egs merge, tsorig-projection; (4) parallelize independent lanes; (5) execution-grade specs so agents do not burn discovery time.
design:
STATUS RECONCILIATION (2026-07-06): the first compression round EXECUTED — wave:0 (egs, r6d.6) closed, wave:1 (60r) in flight, wave:2 keystones (8y8 interval-lift, 42n envelope, a4w.3.1 attention.stream, uw6 min-span demo, g3k latency) ALL CLOSED. This bead is the STANDING sequencing instrument, not a one-shot: each round picks the next 2-3-week critical path and applies the five compression moves (consolidate machinery / demo-first / keystones-unblock-many / parallelize lanes / execution-grade specs).

NEXT ROUND (current graph state): the visible critical path is 5v6 (embedding worker — unblocks cem.4, cem.5, and with 2nd+a4w.1 also cem.6) -> 7z0.4 (judgment seat — the adjudication bottleneck by operator ruling) -> 0vx/8cr (control-plane + evidence-tier pair). Independent parallel lanes: 2bk (cheap deploy proof), r6d.9 harness (closes vxu + proves r6d.4/.5/.7), x9i completion (unblocks lpz). Decision beads pq5/vfy gate automata cost.
acceptance_criteria: A round is complete when: (1) wave:N labels are stamped on the chosen critical path; (2) every wave bead is execution-grade (design+AC+verify present — one-read startable); (3) the sequencing rationale is recorded in this bead's notes with the date; (4) blocked/ready state matches the dependency graph (bd blocked output is accurate). The bead itself stays open as the standing instrument; close only if the operator retires wave-based sequencing.
notes:
2026-07-07 audit integration: graph grooming applied this session (PR pending): ready queue compressed to the 11-bead wave:0 slice + r6d.11; 139 packet edges + 13 related→blocks upgrades applied; 5 inverted blockers removed; waves 0-9 restamped; 27 ACs formalized; new beads r6d.11 (emission receipt — THE Batch-A head), h2x/2k2 (5v6 preflights), cmy (entity defaults), rkv.13 (git.file.changed). Judgment deviations from the packet recorded in the PR body: r6d.5/r6d.3 not gated on r6d.1; 7z0.4 not gated on 8cr/0vx; 2nd gated only on 7z0.4+a4w.4. Milestone cut lines M0-M8 adopted from the packet (see new/sinex_bead_adjustment_packet.md §Milestone cut lines).
2026-07-07 (post-#2403): the critical-path inputs changed materially — re-derive the ETA sequence before executing. Deltas: r6d.11 now gated on r6d.5+r6d.9 (spool fix and killpoint harness precede the receipt primitive); new wave:1 P1s exist (r6d.12 multi-event settlement, z8p pre-redaction confirm, bor topology spec, ive xtask stale-binary); 0vx/8cr/68c decomposed into 14 wired children (wave:4 spine is now walkable in small PRs); capture fan-out is ungated from 5j8; nbi.11 promoted to wave:2 carrying prod-visible Nix fixes.
2026-07-07 round (post #2403/#2404) — the five moves applied to the current graph. LANES: (A) correctness core, serial: r6d.9 + r6d.5 (parallel) -> r6d.11 receipt -> {vxu, r6d.4, r6d.7, w4i} in parallel; r6d.12 and z8p are independent event-engine fixes startable immediately. (B) prod truth, parallel: 2bk proof (git safe.directory leg expected to fail -> feeds nbi.11) -> nbi.11 manifest fixes -> bor topology spec (per-stream numbers shared with n23.3). (C) devloop gate integrity, small+early: ive first — a stale verifier binary undermines every other lane's proofs — then 9es substrate seal. (D) consumption spine, wave:4: 8cr.1 -> {0vx.1->0vx.2->0vx.3 | 0vx.4 -> 8cr.2 -> 0vx.5 | 68c.1 -> 68c.2/.3/.4} — 14 one-PR-sized children. KEYSTONES (unblock counts): r6d.11 (4), 0vx.4 (7 transitively), pq2 decision (5 — promoted to wave:3), a4w.1 fixture (cem.5, cem.7, pq5 input), 7z0.4 (2nd). STAMPS CHECKED: one true wave inversion found and fixed (vxu wave:0->1; it is gated by r6d.11). NOTE: the earlier '0 inversions' lint result was a false pass — bd list --json uses type/depends_on_id while bd show --json uses dependency_type/id; any graph lint must key on the list shape.
2026-07-08 ROUND (post-ratification, PR #2414). The decision-brief batch removed the deliberation gates that were sequencing constraints; re-derive from this state, not the 2026-07-07 round.

WHAT THE RATIFICATION UNBLOCKED (five closed decision beads = five removed gates): pq2 -> dcs is now pure encoding and un-gates k6s/2xy/a4w.3.2/a4w.3.3/a4w.3.12; pq5+vfy+nbi.4 -> one cheap implementation carrier (ijz6) replaces three deliberations; y8v -> the interval chain (ecy -> n9a -> h3g -> 5s6) has zero open semantics; r6d.11 backend (A) -> Batch A is designable end-to-end; h2x -> 5v6 may start.

LANES FOR THE NEXT 2-3 WEEKS (four, genuinely parallel — different subsystems, no shared hotspot files):
(A) CORRECTNESS CORE, serial and long: r6d.5 + r6d.9 (parallel, both wave:0) -> r6d.11 receipt (backend A, design final) + 4as3 CommitFrontier (design written 2026-07-08) as ONE PR-chain -> p5ou lifecycle core (design written) -> {vxu, r6d.4, r6d.7, w4i} become route-through-the-core rather than four patches. 87hu (model-based tests) can be written against the reference model DURING r6d.11 implementation, not after. r6d.12 and z8p are independent event-engine fixes startable immediately by anyone idle.
(B) CONSUMPTION SPINE: dcs (execution-grade, cheap) -> the five un-gated composites. a4w.1 golden fixture is the parallel instrument (it also feeds the pq5 re-entry verdict).
(C) PROD TRUTH, cheap and parallel: ijz6 (registry + Nix defaults; one PR, prod-visible), 2bk (deploy proof), bor (topology spec), 60r (attention sources), ive (stale-verifier P0 — a false-pass in the ONLY gate).
(D) LLM PLANE, fully independent: 5v6 (allowlist contract fixed by h2x) -> its measurements close 4j2.8 -> cem.4/cem.5, 2k2, a4w.5.

THE FIVE COMPRESSION MOVES APPLIED: consolidate machinery = p5ou (kills a 4x cross-product) and 32p2 (kills the drift class); demo-first = cem.2 fault-injection is now nearly free once uz9d lands its injection layer; keystones = r6d.11+4as3+p5ou (one chain unblocks the whole r6d epic), dcs (unblocks five), ijz6 (unblocks the vfy/pq5/nbi.4 residue); parallelize = the four lanes above; execution-grade specs = p5ou/4as3/dcs/k6s/ijz6 all got designs written 2026-07-08 so agents do not burn discovery time.

RECOMMENDED START ORDER for a single agent pipelining (per the global CLAUDE.md batch protocol — disjoint footprints, pipeline PRs, never idle-wait CI): ijz6 (cheap, prod-visible, disjoint) -> dcs (docs-only, disjoint) -> n9a (interval keystone) -> then commit to lane A serially. 5v6 is the one lane a second agent could take with zero hotspot overlap.

STANDING: this bead remains the sequencing instrument; next round re-derives after lane A reaches r6d.11.

title: Decision: consume-or-retire the dark entity substrate
description: The 4-stage entity chain (extract to resolve to enrich to relate) runs and entity.related is read by nothing (verified: no consumer in source; it is terminal). By the construct-validity doctrine this is worse than not existing - it burns the wildcard fan-in and emits unvalidated 0.5 confidences that look like a knowledge graph. It cannot stay write-only.
design:
DECISION FRAME (verified state 2026-07-06): the 4-stage chain (entity-extractor -> entity-resolver -> entity-enricher -> relation-extractor) runs default-enabled in every deployment; entity.related has zero consumers (terminal); extractor is 4 regexes first-match-only (a4w.4 tracks multi-entity outputs); relations are co-occurrence @0.5 confidence, unvalidated (8cr territory). OPTIONS: (A) CONSUME — wire entities/relations into query units (lv8) + evidence-graph reads (k6s) + project.attribution (a4w.3.3), keeping the chain and paying its wildcard fan-in; (B) RETIRE-UNTIL-NEEDED — disable the four automata by default (SINEX_AUTOMATA_ENABLED), keep code, re-enable behind the a4w.3.3 entity-join demand; (C) SHADOW-LANE — move the chain to a semantic lane (0vx machinery) so its outputs are proposals, not canonical-looking derived events. INTERACTS: vfy (wildcard fan-in bound — the chain is a large share of the * readers), a4w.1 (golden set would evaluate the extractor), 8cr (tier vocabulary would label the 0.5s honestly). Construct-validity doctrine says write-only is worse than absent.

PROPOSED RULING (2026-07-08, encoded for operator ratification): (B) NOW, (C) AS THE RE-ENTRY SHAPE. Disable the four automata by default in registry/NixOS defaults immediately — this stops the fabricated-authority emissions (cmy: confidence 1.0/0.5 defaults, empty source refs) and removes 4 of the 5 wildcard readers (largest single relief for vfy) at zero code cost. Code stays; existing rows stay (archive lifecycle applies if later pruned). Re-enablement is gated on BOTH: a named consumer demand (a4w.3.3 entity-join or lv8 query units) AND the 0vx shadow-lane machinery, so the chain comes back as evidence-carrying candidate claims (ClaimSupport vector per 8cr), never as canonical-looking derived events. Straight (A) is rejected today: wiring consumers to unvalidated 0.5-confidence co-occurrence rows launders noise into the consumption plane — the golden fixture (a4w.1) must exist first to evaluate the extractor before its outputs earn consumers. NOTE: cmy (defaults fabricate authority) remains valid independent work — the DB-level defaults are wrong regardless of which automata run.
acceptance_criteria: A recorded decision (A/B/C or hybrid) in this bead with rationale; follow-up beads created for the chosen path and dep-linked; if B: automata disabled by default in registry/NixOS defaults with a re-enable note; if A: at least one real consumer (query unit, read model, or composite) dep-linked; entity.related is no longer a terminal write-only kind OR no longer emitted by default. Operator sign-off (this is an operator-authority call per the adjudication doctrine).
notes: RATIFIED 2026-07-08 (operator, decision-brief batch): (B) retire-until-needed NOW with (C) shadow-lane as the sole re-entry shape is FINAL. Implementation carrier filed this session (apply ratified automaton portfolio). Re-entry gate: named consumer + 0vx shadow lane + a4w.1 extractor evaluation. Closing this decision bead.
close_reason: Decision FINAL and operator-ratified 2026-07-08: (B) retire-until-needed NOW — the 4-stage entity chain goes default-off — with (C) shadow-lane as the sole re-entry shape (0vx machinery, ClaimSupport-labeled proposals, named consumer + a4w.1 extractor evaluation required). Straight consume rejected: unvalidated 0.5-confidence rows must not earn consumers before evaluation. Implementation carrier = sinex-ijz6 (filed, dep-linked). cmy remains valid independent work. Verify: bd show sinex-pq5 (ruling + ratification); bd show sinex-ijz6 (implementation AC).

title: Land the Sinex doctrines: nine falsifiable load-bearing statements
description: Cheap to write, expensive to lack. Nine load-bearing doctrines, each naming the observation that would DISPROVE it, so the system is auditable against its own claims (the Gwern/LessWrong falsifiability stance): provenance (occurrence in columns not PK), three-clocks (never falsify a clock), layer (value = height of the CONSUMED layer; a kind earns existence by naming its consumer), join (four operators; a third ad-hoc key = missing entity), evidence-tier (confidence carries provenance or is absent; only adjudicated = truth), degraded-state (no silent zeros), losslessness (coverage-error is a standing measure), agent-posture (attributability over restriction), non-goals (the negative space is load-bearing).
design: SHAPE: one doctrine page (crate/sinexd/docs/architecture/doctrines.md or .agent/includes/architecture/doctrines.md — pick the transcluded location so agents load it) listing the nine load-bearing statements, EACH with: the claim, the falsifier (the concrete observation that would disprove it), the enforcement point (file:line or gate), and the owning bead/epic if currently unproven. The nine (from epic set + final-shape): (1) provenance XOR / occurrence-in-columns-not-PK; (2) three clocks, never falsify any (ts_orig=source-domain time, ts_coided=f(id), ts_persisted=write time); (3) replay is re-interpretation, economics scale with affected subgraph; (4) events are a cache over materials (L0 forever); (5) value = height of the consumed layer — a kind earns existence by naming its derivation (derivation_catalog.md); (6) confidence cannot become authority — promotion only via Proposal/Judgment (authority.rs); (7) no silent loss — every loss window enumerated + closed/accepted (r6d matrix); (8) honest gaps — every read surface carries ViewEnvelope/CaveatView; (9) uniform event semantics, physically separated lanes where policy diverges (reflection lane). Cross-link each to existing docs rather than duplicating prose.
acceptance_criteria: Doctrine page exists at a transcluded location; all nine have claim+falsifier+enforcement-point; xtask docs check passes; provenance.md / final-shape / derivation_catalog cross-reference it instead of restating; the page is cited from CLAUDE.md architecture index. No doctrine restates something code cannot support (each enforcement point verified by grep/test at write time).
notes:
2026-07-06 rawlog mining: the operator has RECURRING doubt about the core derived-events concept (rawlog 06-18 22:07 'worry whether chains of derived events is actually all that relevant/useful'; 06-18 22:54 'imperative solution... maybe declaratively/functionally'; 06-28 21:35 'was append-only JSONB the right model... bespoke event-specific tables might have been better'). Final-shape doctrine (07-03, later) settled it FOR derived events — but the doctrine page should carry this as doctrine 3/5's honest counter-position with its falsifier: if the composition audit (nbi composition bead) finds the algebra doesn't close in practice, or L3 consumption stays empty after the a4w wave, the worry was right. Doctrines that record their own strongest objection are stronger.
2026-07-08 (post-ratification): three of the nine doctrines now have RATIFIED enforcement points to cite instead of open questions — write them with these: (4) JOIN doctrine = the pq2 4-operator algebra (time/entity/provenance/containment; a third ad-hoc key = a missing entity kind); enforcement point = derivation_catalog.md formal core (dcs); falsifier = a shipped composite that needs a fifth key. (5) EVIDENCE-TIER doctrine = ClaimSupport vector (8cr), adjudicated only via the authority seam with a judgment event id; enforcement = validation + DB level; falsifier = an adjudicated support state constructible without a judgment id. NEW DOCTRINE CANDIDATE (from y8v, ratified): SINGLE LIVE INTERPRETATION PER OCCURRENCE — supersession archives, never mutates; identity is start-anchored; ends move, starts do not. Falsifier = two live rows sharing an occurrence key. Its enforcement point is n9a admission RevisionPolicy. This is arguably load-bearing enough to be doctrine 10, or to be folded into doctrine (1) provenance as its revision clause — decide when writing the page. Also: doctrine (7) no-silent-loss now has a concrete closure instrument chain (r6d.1 matrix + r6d.9 killpoints + uz9d fault matrix + 87hu interleavings) — cite them as the falsification apparatus.

title: Add ORDER BY sort syntax to Sinex query DSL
description:
Fresh-agent MCP postfix proof sinex-egc showed the agent naturally tried:

    events where ts_orig >= '2026-07-02T12:00:00Z' and ts_orig < '2026-07-02T13:00:00Z' order by ts_orig asc limit 100

The query parser rejected `order by`, even though sorted bounded event windows are a natural agent/operator query shape and the ViewEnvelope query model already carries `sort: []`. Implement first-class order/sort syntax in the query-unit DSL, wire it through MCP/sinexctl query surfaces, add focused parser/CLI tests, and update orientation/examples so fresh agents discover the supported shape without failure.
notes:
Implemented first-class ORDER BY support for query-unit expressions:
- Parser accepts `order by <key> [asc|desc]` as an alias for the existing `sort <key> [asc|desc]` clause.
- Connector parsing now requires token boundaries, so `order` is no longer misread as `or`.
- Event query units expose `ts_orig` and `ts_coided` sort keys.
- `event_query_from_sinex_query` lowers event sort keys into explicit `EventOrdering::{TsOrig,TsCoided}` plus `SortDirection` so the DB listing order matches the DSL.
- `sinex_query` rows expose `ts_orig`/`ts_coided` fields, and agent orientation/tool descriptions show the fresh-agent bounded-window shape.

Verification:
- xtask test -p sinex-primitives -E 'test(parser_lowers_order_by_ts_orig_for_event_windows)' -- job 2001413 passed.
- xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(mcp_query_accepts_order_by_ts_orig_event_windows)' -- job 2001414 passed.
- xtask check -p sinex-primitives -- job 2001415 passed.
- xtask check -p sinex-db -- job 2001416 passed.
- xtask check -p sinexctl -- job 2001417 passed.
Additional verification: xtask check -p sinexd -- job 2001418 passed after EventQuery ordering field was added.
close_reason: Implemented ORDER BY query DSL support and verified parser/MCP/package gates.

title: Operator & agent UX: make Sinex tangible — the daily surface, the judgment seat, the agent hookup
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03): UX revamp making Sinex tangible for the operator AND for agents. This epic also owns, by operator ruling, the two strategic risks previously unowned: the judgment bottleneck (adjudication is the only path to truth and has no workflow) and the consumption-habit loop (tower complete but unused is the failure mode). Diagnosis: every capability currently terminates in a JSON-emitting CLI invocation the operator must remember to type and an agent must be separately taught. The system has no place you LOOK, no place you JUDGE, and no default way agents arrive already-connected. Three children: (1) the operator daily surface, (2) the adjudication seat, (3) agent arrival. Latency is a prerequisite handled elsewhere (sinex-0tj/g3k); demo polish is cem's; this epic is the standing product surfaces.
design: PRINCIPLES (settled): tangible = zero-recall-cost entry points (a keybind, a pane, a morning artifact, an ambient hook) — not more commands; every surface renders through the shared card/ref substrate (no UI-only queries — algebra rule); every number shown carries provenance or is absent; gaps render as gaps. Agents are first-class users: the same surfaces must be MCP/JSON-addressable, and agent-facing descriptions teach the data model (materials/derived/refs) so a cold agent uses refs correctly without repo docs.
acceptance_criteria: Operator reaches for a Sinex surface unprompted on an ordinary day (the habit test — measurable via consumption telemetry, cf sinex-nbi.2); proposals get judged within days not never (queue age visible); new agent sessions arrive with Sinex context without per-session setup.
notes: 2026-07-06 UX constraint stamp (vision.md north star — binding on every child): (1) AuDHD constraint — capture automatic/invisible, query precise, engagement only by choice; a feature that requires energy to use fails when it matters most (no daily-ritual surfaces). (2) The four refusals — no hidden AI (LLM output is always provenance-carrying events), notes are input not ontology, NO streaks/scores/gamification in any default surface, no prompts/nudges (ambient surfaces like 9vn deliver information, never directives — 'push, not pull' means delivery mode, not behavioral pushing). Judgment seat + daily surface + briefs must all pass these four filters.

title: Recover zero-event orphaned source materials without raw DLQ
description: After deploying the zero-event slice_arrival_timeout remediation, production health still degraded from PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ because reconcile_orphaned_sensing_materials routed an ordinary zero-event desktop.window-manager orphan as orphaned_sensing_material. That is the same empty source-material residue class under the restart/orphan path: status failed, parsed_event_count=0, total_bytes NULL, no useful raw event to retry. Make zero-event ordinary orphaned sensing materials recovered_partial with explicit metadata/remediation instead of raw DLQ pressure.
acceptance_criteria: Orphaned ordinary source material with parsed_event_count=0 is marked recovered_partial and does not publish events.dlq.event_engine; remediation_plan shows a distinct inspect_empty_orphaned_source_material decision for the recovery reason; eventful ordinary orphans still route as explicit failures; targeted sinexd tests and check pass.
close_reason: Implemented in working tree and verified before commit: zero-event ordinary orphaned sensing materials now mark recovered_partial with recovery_reason orphaned_zero_event_source_material_recovered_partial and dlq_policy suppressed_zero_event_source_material_restart_orphan, while eventful ordinary orphans keep explicit failure routing. API remediation_plan maps that recovery reason to inspect_empty_orphaned_source_material. Verification: targeted sinexd test job 2001365 passed, including orphan_reconcile_recovers_globally_stale_zero_event_source_material_without_dlq and remediation_decision_inspects_empty_orphaned_source_material; xtask check -p sinexd passed as job 2001366.

title: Fix sources archive to scope lifecycle archival to the requested material
description: While implementing sinex-17w, source-material remediation needed a safe operator clear path. Existing sinexctl sources archive <material_id> is not safe to advertise for that purpose: handle_sources_archive verifies the requested source material exists and counts events for it, but for non-dry-run it builds LifecycleArchiveRequest with before/source/event_ids all None and limit=10000, so the underlying lifecycle archive is not scoped to the requested material. Fix the RPC to archive exactly the requested material cascade or remove/guard the non-dry-run path until it is precise.
acceptance_criteria: sources.archive non-dry-run archives only the requested material and its derived cascade; dry-run preview and execution use the same scoped cascade; regression proves another material's events are not archived; remediation guidance may safely point at sources archive only after this is true.
close_reason: Fixed sources.archive to collect root event ids for the requested source material and feed those ids into lifecycle archive for both dry-run and execution. Regression proves the requested material root plus derived cascade archive while another material remains live. Verified with xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(sources_archive_scopes_to_requested_material_and_derived_cascade)' (job 2001367) and xtask check -p sinexd (job 2001368).

title: Make material-assembler slice timeouts health-actionable instead of DLQ noise
description: During production restore on 2026-07-03, nine zero-event self-observation materials created around restart/purge cleanup timed out with slice_arrival_timeout and were published to PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ. Raw ingestion was caught up, but health degraded solely because these assembly-failure records sat in the raw-ingest DLQ until manually purged. The system needs a clearer distinction between event-ingest DLQ pressure and material-assembly cleanup residue, plus an operator action/remediation path that does not require ad hoc NATS stream purges.
acceptance_criteria: A slice_arrival_timeout for a stale zero-event material is surfaced through a material-assembly health/debt/remediation channel with material_id/source/status/count context; raw-ingest DLQ health no longer conflates these records with event ingestion failures; operator can clear or acknowledge resolved assembly residues through sinexctl/xtask without direct nats stream purge; tests cover stale zero-event self-observation material timeout classification.
notes: Evidence: after sinex-mhk restore proof, health degraded with raw-ingest DLQ pressure: 9 pending. PROD_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ seq 837..845 decoded to {material_id, error:slice_arrival_timeout, elapsed_seconds:359, timeout_seconds:3600}; corresponding raw.source_material_registry rows were failed, parsed_event_count=0, total_bytes NULL, sources were sinex.self-observation.* materials started around 2026-07-03T09:38 and failed at 09:50. Raw event consumer remained caught up. Manual purge restored healthy runtime.
close_reason: Completed by c37b533b9. Zero-event slice_arrival_timeout materials now bypass raw-ingest DLQ and land as recovered_partial source-material remediation with material/source/count metadata; self-observation residue is low-severity acknowledgement, ordinary source-material residue is medium-severity inspection. Operator acknowledgement is through the existing safe per-material command sinexctl sources annotate <material_id> --tag acknowledged-empty-timeout. Verification: material-assembler focused job 2001359 passed 2 tests; remediation handler job 2001361 passed 4 tests; xtask check -p sinexd passed job 2001362 with no current sinexd diagnostics.

comments.author: Sinity
comments.text:
Research packet written: `.agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-17w-assembler-dlq-health.md`.

Exact current DLQ path:
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance.rs:250-288` — `MaterialAssembler::find_stale_materials` identifies stale in-memory assemblies by `slice_arrival_timeout`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance.rs:332-375` — `MaterialAssembler::process_stale_material` calls `mark_timeout_material_recovered_partial_if_eventful(..., "slice_arrival_timeout")`; if that returns false/error it calls `route_material_error(material_id, "slice_arrival_timeout", {timeout_seconds, elapsed_seconds})` and then `finalize_failed_material(material_id, "slice_arrival_timeout")`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/finalize.rs:132-196` — `MaterialAssembler::route_material_error` builds `MaterialDlqPayload { material_id, error, context, failed_at }`, tags it `NatsTrafficClass::RawIngestDlq` plus semantic `transport::Class::SourceMaterial`, and publishes to `self.dlq_subject`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/finalize.rs:220-271` — `mark_timeout_material_recovered_partial_if_eventful` only suppresses the raw-DLQ path for `slice_arrival_timeout` when `parsed_event_count > 0`; zero-event materials return `Ok(false)` and fall through to DLQ.
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/mod.rs:337-339` — `self.dlq_subject` is `events.dlq.event_engine` under the runtime namespace, covered by the raw-ingest DLQ stream.

Exact health classification path:
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:53-59` — `RawIngestDlqHealth` is the gateway component.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:405-510` — `ServiceContainer::probe_raw_ingest_dlq_pressure` inspects `SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DLQ`; `state.messages == 0` is Healthy, any non-zero message count is Degraded with detail `raw-ingest DLQ pressure: ...`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:656-678` — `health_report` pushes that detail into `degradation_reasons` when raw-ingest DLQ is Degraded.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/service_container.rs:688-693` — top-level `healthy` is false when raw-ingest DLQ is Degraded.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/system.rs:33-77` — `system_health_response` exposes the component status/detail as `components.raw_ingest_dlq`.

Existing adjacent surfaces:
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance.rs:377-467` already suppresses DLQ for globally stale orphaned self-observation registry rows by marking them `recovered_partial` with `dlq_policy = "suppressed_self_observation_restart_orphan"`.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/sources.rs:371-448` implements `sources.remediation_plan`; `sources.rs:399-400` excludes empty failed materials unless `include_empty` is true.
- `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/sources.rs:413-484` exposes `sinexctl sources remediation-plan --include-empty`.
- `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/ops/debt.rs:171-184` and `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/ops/catchup.rs:90-99` call `sources.remediation_plan` with `include_empty: false`, so zero-event residue is not shown in those dashboards today.

Small first implementation slice:
1. In `MaterialAssembler::process_stale_material`, before `route_material_error`, classify zero-event self-observation timeouts as material remediation debt: read `source_identifier`/`parsed_event_count`; if source is `sinex.self-observation.*` (including `#material=` suffix) and count is 0, mark `recovered_partial` instead of publishing to raw DLQ.
2. Use a recovery reason like `slice_arrival_timeout_zero_event_self_observation_recovered_partial` and metadata with `material_id`, `source_identifier`, `elapsed_seconds`, `timeout_seconds`, `parsed_event_count: 0`, `dlq_policy: "suppressed_zero_event_self_observation_timeout"`.
3. Reuse source-material remediation surfaces: update `remediation_decision` for that recovery reason to low severity review/acknowledge, and optionally include empty recovered-partial material residue in `ops debt` / `ops catchup` or expose a separate count.
4. Defer raw-DLQ health filtering by semantic transport class for a later slice. `probe_raw_ingest_dlq_pressure` currently does only cheap stream-state inspection; peeking/classifying messages in the health path is a larger latency-sensitive change.

Suggested tests:
- `crate/sinexd/src/event_engine/material_assembler/maintenance_test.rs`: add `stale_zero_event_self_observation_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq`, mirroring existing `stale_eventful_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq` but with `parsed_event_count = 0` and a `sinex.self-observation.*#material=<id>` source; assert `RecoveredPartial`, metadata, and no `events.dlq.event_engine` message.
- Same file: optional counter-test that zero-event non-self-observation timeout still routes to DLQ and marks failed.
- `crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/sources_test.rs`: add remediation-decision test for the new recovery reason mapping to low severity review/acknowledge.
- `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/ops_test.rs` or `crate/sinexctl/src/commands/sources_test.rs`: if debt/catchup visibility changes, assert the row appears with the new reason.

Verification commands for implementation:
```bash
xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(stale_zero_event_self_observation_timeout_recovers_partial_without_dlq)'
xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(remediation_decision)'
xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(source_remediation_plan)'
xtask check -p sinexd
xtask check -p sinexctl
```
Do not use bare cargo. Reserve `xtask check --full` for final implementation confidence, not research.

title: Harden recall projection latency on large production stores
description: Production restore proof on 2026-07-03 showed the narrow recall path works, but an initial sinexctl recall --window 30m --limit 40 JSON probe against prod timed out at the gateway after 30s and left DB statement-timeout / slow-pool evidence in sinexd logs. A later tight 5m recall succeeded in 140ms after raw backlog cleanup, and events query/sources status were fast. This issue should make recall/context projection robust for broader windows on an ~80M-row production store without relying on manual retry timing. Start by decomposing the recall path: base event-card query, diversity top-up source queries, self-observation filtering, and source coverage caveat query. Add bounded timing/telemetry and remove sequential query amplification where possible.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl recall --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --window 30m --limit 40 --format json completes reliably under a healthy production runtime; implementation records per-stage recall timings or equivalent evidence; tests cover the projection path without adding a silo; broad recall failure produces an actionable caveat/error instead of tying up DB pool silently.
notes: Evidence packet: .agent/demos/sinex/prod-restore-recall-proof-20260703T095311Z. Failure evidence: initial 30m recall command was stopped after exceeding the useful wait; journal showed RPC 504 at 30s, DB statement timeout, and slow sqlx pool acquire. Successful counter-evidence: 5m recall with --include-self-observation returned 12 events across 5 sources in ~140ms; events query 5m returned in ~389ms; sources status returned in ~1041ms.
close_reason: Implemented first hardening slice: recall/context now records per-stage timings in machine-readable query_echo.stage_timings_ms and bounds base event-card query, diversity top-up, and source-status caveat stages with explicit recall.* timeout caveats. Verification: focused sinexctl tests passed as job 2001371; xtask check -p sinexctl passed as job 2001372; xtask build -p sinexctl passed as job 2001373; live prod /var/cache/sinex/.../target/debug/sinexctl recall --window 30m --limit 40 --format json returned exit 0 in 9.1s with stage timings and recall.diversity_top_up_timeout instead of hanging. Follow-up created for replacing sequential diversity top-up.

comments.author: Sinity
comments.text:
Executable issue packet added after read-only code/evidence pass.

Research packet: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-0tj-recall-latency.md. Related Bead linked: sinex-g3k owns the broader source-filtered latency / ts_orig read-model branch decision; this Bead should harden the recall command path and feed measurements into sinex-g3k if the shared EventQuery listing remains the bottleneck.

Likely root causes:
- CLI query amplification: crate/sinexctl/src/commands/context.rs:263 builds the base recall EventQuery, then :289 calls events.cards, :290 runs top_up_context_diversity, and :292 runs source caveats. top_up_context_diversity at :456 issues sequential events.cards LIMIT 1 RPCs for every missing CONTEXT_DIVERSITY_SOURCES entry.
- EventQuery listing filters original time but orders/cursors by interpretation id: crate/sinex-db/src/repositories/events/composable_query.rs:128 builds the listing, push_filters at :577 applies ts_orig range predicates, and :157 orders by relevance/id or id. Existing indexes in crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/events.rs:387 include ix_events_ts_orig, ix_events_source_ts_orig, and ix_events_source_type_ts_orig, but the generic listing does not order on ts_orig.
- Recall excludes self-observation after fetch: context.rs:291 calls apply_self_observation_mode after the DB/RPC work, so small limits can be consumed by telemetry rows that are later dropped.
- Source caveats are useful but non-essential: context_source_caveats at context.rs:500 calls sources_status_view_filtered(None, None, false); server side source status still performs material aggregation, event presence probes, provider/session-state reads, and view assembly in crate/sinexd/src/api/handlers/source_status.rs:123 and :256.

Implementation slices:
1. Instrument and bound recall stages in context.rs: base cards, diversity top-up, self-observation filter, source caveats, render. Emit timings in JSON/YAML metadata or caveats. Add stage-specific timeout caveats such as recall.diversity_top_up_timeout and recall.source_status_timeout instead of letting the operator see only gateway 504/slow-pool symptoms.
2. Remove sequential top-up amplification. First acceptable slice: run top-up queries concurrently under a small cap and shared budget. Better shared-substrate slice: add a server-side source-diverse event-card query under the general events/context substrate, returning base latest cards plus latest card per requested source/family with one disclosure pass. Avoid a recall-only silo.
3. Push self-observation exclusion into EventQuery/composable_query. Preserve --include-self-observation by not applying the predicate. Add tests so recall limits cannot be exhausted by rows that are immediately dropped.
4. Measure/fix the ts_orig listing shape. Run bounded EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) for the generated base recall query and source-filtered top-up query. If an index/order fix suffices, consider ts_orig DESC, id DESC listing/cursor semantics for time-window queries plus any missing required indexes. If it still touches too much data because core.events partitions by id/ts_coided, defer the projection/read-model branch to sinex-g3k.

Acceptance-criteria refinements:
- 30m production recall returns either full output or finite recall output with stage-specific caveats; opaque gateway 504 without stage attribution is not acceptable.
- Recall uses a bounded number of RPCs independent of how many diversity sources are absent; target shape is one event-card RPC plus one bounded/optional source-status RPC.
- Stage timings or equivalent evidence are present in machine-readable recall output.
- Self-observation exclusion is applied before DB result limiting unless --include-self-observation is set.
- If broad latency is still dominated by core.events ts_orig scans after slices 1-3, this Bead records the measured plan and hands the read-model decision to sinex-g3k rather than silently expanding scope.

Verification commands:
- Static/read-only: rg -n "execute_context_request|top_up_context_diversity|context_source_caveats|is_self_observation_card" crate/sinexctl/src/commands/context.rs; rg -n "execute_event_listing|push_filters|ORDER BY id|ts_orig" crate/sinex-db/src/repositories/events/composable_query.rs; rg -n "ix_events_ts_orig|ix_events_source_ts_orig|ix_events_source_type_ts_orig" crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/events.rs crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs.
- Focused tests after implementation: xtask test -p sinexctl -E 'test(recall_)'; xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(composable_query)'; xtask check -p sinexctl; xtask check -p sinex-db.
- Live/prod proof only when operator-approved and runtime is healthy: sinexctl recall --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --window 30m --limit 40 --format json; compare with --include-self-observation and sinexctl events query --rpc-url https://127.0.0.1:9999 --since 30m --limit 40 --format json.
- DB plan proof only with explicit read-only session and statement_timeout: EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT * FROM core.events WHERE ts_orig >= now() - interval '30 minutes' AND ts_orig <= now() ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 41; repeat with source='webhistory' LIMIT 2 for the top-up shape.

title: Make schema backfills explicit, resumable, and observable
description: Production restore on 2026-07-03 showed backfill_source_material_event_counts scanning ~80.8M core.events rows during sinexd startup. The work is legitimate first-run convergence, but it is opaque, timeout-sensitive, non-resumable, and guarded by a weak sentinel (any parsed_event_count > 0). Design and implement a migration/backfill substrate for one-time schema data repairs: versioned sentinel, progress/observability, resumability/idempotency, cancellation behavior, and operator-facing proof. Immediate seed case is parsed_event_count backfill on raw.source_material_registry.
design: Executable packet integrated from research subagent Carson; full local packet: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-exc-schema-backfills.md. Shape: schema apply creates objects and registers backfill specs; unbounded data repairs move to an explicit sinex-schema backfill runner surfaced as xtask schema backfill. Add sinex_schemas.schema_backfill_runs keyed by (backfill_key, version) with status, phase, cursor, target, progress, operation_id, timestamps; use pg_try_advisory_lock per key/version; use core.operations_log for operator-visible lifecycle after adding a schema-backfill operation type, but keep checkpoint truth in schema_backfill_runs. First slice parsed-event-count-v1: install existing count triggers first, snapshot target.max_event_id, scan material events by id chunks into an accumulator table, apply accumulated counts to raw.source_material_registry in material-id chunks, persist progress after every chunk, no-op successful reruns unless --restart. Initial implementation may require quiescence; online exactness is a child issue.
acceptance_criteria: apply_schema_for_url no longer runs backfill_source_material_event_counts or any unbounded data repair; xtask schema backfill status --format json reports registered backfills and run state; xtask schema backfill run parsed-event-count-v1 resumes after interruption from persisted cursor; progress is visible in DB and CLI (phase, rows scanned, target max id, materials accumulated/applied, updated timestamp); successful rerun no-ops and partial rerun resumes without double-counting; fixture proof covers zero-event, one-event, multi-event, and derived-only source materials; command refuses or warns unless run in supported quiescent mode.
notes: Code references: crate/sinexd/src/main.rs startup schema apply; crate/sinex-schema/src/apply.rs backfill_source_material_event_counts weak sentinel/full aggregate; crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/events.rs future count triggers; crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/source_materials.rs parsed_event_count; xtask/src/commands/schema.rs operator entrypoint. Suggested child Beads: online-safe delta capture; measured source_material_id index evaluation; sinexctl ops backfill status; xtask schema backfill verify parsed-event-count-v1.
close_reason: Implemented explicit schema backfill substrate and parsed-event-count-v1 runner. Schema apply now only registers backfill metadata and no longer runs the unbounded source-material event-count repair. Added persisted run/progress tables, resumable chunked scan with quiescence acknowledgement, status/run xtask commands, generated docs, and DB-backed resume/count/idempotence tests. Verified: xtask test -p sinex-schema -E 'test(parsed_event_count_backfill)' job 2001377 passed; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(command_catalog_exposes_core_public_surface)' job 2001378 passed; xtask check -p sinex-schema job 2001379 passed; xtask check -p xtask job 2001380 passed; xtask docs check passed; xtask schema backfill status --format json reported parsed-event-count-v1 registered.

title: Serialize xtask schema apply before background job queueing
description: Starting two xtask checks concurrently on 2026-07-03 caused both command frontends to enter checkout-local schema apply before one was queued behind the coordinated slot. One frontend hit PostgreSQL duplicate index/pg_class race creating ix_archived_events_ts_orig while the other was also applying schema. The job queue serialized the later background work, but not the pre-queue schema-apply preflight. Fix xtask so schema apply is covered by a process-wide/interprocess lock before any concurrent frontend can run it, or move schema apply behind the same coordinated slot assignment.
acceptance_criteria: Two simultaneous xtask check/test invocations cannot run schema apply concurrently. A regression launches two commands against a schema-pending checkout and proves one waits before DDL starts. Duplicate index/constraint races from concurrent schema apply no longer occur.
close_reason: Implemented in flake.nix: launcher-only xtask --bg requests now skip pre-exec SQLx bootstrap and enter the coordinator first; the actual foreground/background child still bootstraps SQLx under a wrapper-level flock at .sinex/state/xtask-sqlx-bootstrap.lock. Verified with nix-instantiate --parse flake.nix, nix eval --raw .#devShells.x86_64-linux.default.name, a regenerated nix develop launcher returning Started background job 2001365 in ~1s with no pre-launch schema output, a concurrent launcher returning Queued behind job 2001363 immediately, and xtask check -p sinexd job 2001366 passing.

comments.author: Sinity
comments.text: Implementation note 2026-07-03: the pre-queue DDL race is in the devshell xtask wrapper, not the Rust xtask background branch. Rust `xtask check --bg`/`xtask test --bg` already enter `coordinate_and_spawn` before foreground preflight, but the wrapper classified check/test/build/fix/deps as SQLx-needing before execing xtask, so concurrent frontends could both run checkout-local schema apply before one became a queued background job. Patch in progress in `flake.nix`: add a current-shell `flock` around `_sinex_xtask_ensure_sqlx_database` and skip SQLx bootstrap for launcher-only `--bg` requests (no `XTASK_BG_JOB_ID`/`XTASK_BG_INVOCATION_ID`, no `--fg`) so the coordinator owns serialization before schema work. Foreground commands and spawned bg children still run SQLx bootstrap.

title: Add crash-window harness for checkpoint and ack ordering
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. Create a targeted harness that can inject crashes/restarts around source emit/checkpoint, automaton confirmed-event consumption, invalidation ack/recompute, and recovery-spool overflow so the loss-window fixes can be verified with more than code inspection.
design: DECISION: build inside xtask::sandbox (the intentional shared harness — do not create a parallel test substrate). Shape: a crash-window scenario runner that (1) seeds a deterministic event/material stream, (2) runs the component under test (source driver loop / automaton consumer / invalidation handler / spool) to a named INJECTION POINT, (3) kills hard (SIGKILL, not graceful shutdown — the whole point), (4) restarts and asserts the recovery contract. Injection points map to the r6d children: after-emit-before-checkpoint (r6d.4), spool-at-cap-plus-one (r6d.5), confirmed-consumer-behind-retention (r6d.6), invalidation-acked-not-recomputed (r6d.7), plus ack-on-enqueue (vxu). Injection mechanism: prefer explicit test hooks (a fail-point feature flag compiled in test builds — check for an existing pattern in the sandbox before adding one) over timing races; each scenario must be deterministic, not flaky-by-design. Assertion vocabulary: DURABLE (all seeded occurrences present after recovery, possibly as new interpretations), or REPAIRABLE (loss detected AND surfaced as an operator-visible signal — DLQ row, health event, gap caveat); silent loss = failure. Scenarios run under xtask test --heavy (they restart processes; not the default loop).
acceptance_criteria: At least the four named windows have scenarios that FAIL on the pre-fix code (prove by running against a git rev before the corresponding r6d fix, recorded once) and PASS after; each r6d.4-7 close cites its scenario; scenarios deterministic across 10 consecutive runs; the r6d.1 matrix links each window row to its harness command.
notes:
2026-07-07 audit integration: named killpoints for the harness (from the verified loss-window matrix, new/sinex_loss_window_matrix_v2.csv — stale line numbers, use 021 grok note cites): after_materialize_before_parse; after_child1_emit_before_child2; after_automaton_checkpoint_before_output_emit (THE r6d.11/vxu window); after_invalidation_ack_before_recompute; after_spool_temp_rename_before_parent_sync; confirmed-ack-after-mpsc-enqueue with backlog. Env-gated deterministic fault injection; machine-readable report per scenario (scenario, killpoint, expected, observed, pass/fail); at least one scenario must FAIL on pre-fix code (red-before/green-after discipline).

Root problem discovered: the initial version tried to share NATS/checkpoint state between
parent and child via `.shared()` on both sides, assuming "shared" meant one server across the
whole test SESSION. It doesn't — xtask::sandbox::nats::ephemeral's shared-instance registry
(SHARED_NATS_REGISTRY) is an in-process Mutex-guarded map, scoped per OS PROCESS. Since the
child is a genuinely separate process (re-invoked via current_exe()), its own .shared() call
lazily started a SEPARATE ephemeral nats-server the parent could never see — the first
extended-test run correctly caught this (processed_count read back as 0, not 1).

Fix: the parent now extracts its own already-running ephemeral NATS server's connection URL
(ctx.nats_handle()?.client_url()) and passes it to the child via a new
SINEX_R6D9_NATS_URL env var. The child connects DIRECTLY via async_nats::connect(url) instead
of provisioning its own NATS at all, bypassing ctx.with_nats() entirely for the child role.
Both processes now genuinely share one NATS server, and a fixed (non-per-test-random)
checkpoint bucket/module/consumer name (R6D9_CHECKPOINT_BUCKET etc.) lets the parent look up
exactly the key the child wrote to.

Test now proves BOTH halves of the sinex-vxu contract in one deterministic run: (1) injection
— child exits(97) exactly at the checkpoint-saved/outputs-not-returned boundary; (2)
irreparability — a fresh CheckpointManager over the SAME durable checkpoint (simulating what a
restarted process's catch-up would read) shows processed_count==1, proving a restart would
skip this input as already-done even though its derived output was never captured anywhere.
This is the complete silent-loss shape sinex-vxu describes, not just "a process crashed once".

spool-at-cap-plus-one / rename-before-dir-fsync: store_recovery_spool_events_at_path
(event_transport.rs:697-741) IS a simple, self-contained associated fn (temp-file write ->

checkpoint scenario. But the specific killpoint this bead's design names for it
("rename-before-dir-fsync") is NOT actually observable via a process-exit fail point: killing
the PROCESS does not drop the OS page cache, so an un-fsynced directory rename is NOT lost by
a simple std::process::exit() the way it would be by a real machine crash/power-loss — the
kernel still has it buffered and will typically flush it regardless of whether the process
that made the call is still alive. Proving THIS specific durability property needs an actual
crash-consistency testing methodology (e.g. a VM-level kill, or a fault-injecting filesystem
like ALICE/dm-flakey), which is a different, heavier tooling investment than what this bead's
existing design assumes "process fail points" can cover. Worth a note on this bead's own
design section so a future session doesn't repeat this dead end.
