Live proof .agent/demos/sinex/attention-stream-ac-live-20260704T1310Z shows attention spans consumed by recall, all packet attention spans have raw support_refs, and source/readiness caveats render as explicit gap timeline items; focused test/check/build jobs 2001675/2001676/2001677 passed.

title: Cross-source composites (L3): the payoff enumeration
description:
The derivation catalog's L3 layer — where cross-source composition pays. Canonical reference: crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md (recipes, join algebra, extension rules). Every child here: joins ONLY via time (interval overlap on ts_orig) or entity (resolved ids); implemented as an automaton with semantics_version + volume budget; confidences evidence-derived or absent.

PRIORITY SEQUENCE (settled): attention.stream -> project.attribution -> work.episode, with interruption.event as the cheap side-demo. That quartet proves the tower with zero new capture and zero LLM. The rest sequence behind their capture prerequisites (browser content, email, calendar).
design: SPEC SOURCE: crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md — the composite table, join algebra, and extension rules are the authority; every child cites its recipe row. RULES (from the catalog, binding here): every composite = an automaton with semantics_version, joins ONLY via the two keys (time-join on overlapping/adjacent ts_orig intervals; entity-join on resolved ids — a third ad-hoc key means a missing entity kind); interval lifting is L2's job (capture stays points/transitions); volume-class before wiring anything into wildcard fan-in; confidence values evidence-derived or absent. PRIORITY SEQUENCE (value-per-effort, all inputs live): attention.stream (.1) -> project.attribution (.3) -> work.episode (.4), with interruption.event (.2) as the cheap side-demo — that quartet proves the tower with zero new capture and zero LLM. THEN: machine.context (.5) and routine.baseline (.6) once daily grain exists (k4c.2); screen.grounding (.7) after xv1 OCR; plan.vs.actual (.8) after calendar (rkv.4); change.episode (.9) after polylogue deep ingestion (4j2) + beads lane (hlv); consumption.episode (.10) after browser content (rkv.3); comm.thread (.11) after email runtime (di7). PREREQ QUALITY GATE: sinex-60r (attention sources unusable) blocks .1 in practice — fix first or .1 reconstructs garbage.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: the priority quartet (.1-.4 with .2) live on real data with query units and honest gaps; each composite's emitted kind is in the catalog with its recipe row updated from 'planned' to 'live'; no composite joins on an ad-hoc key; a recall window visibly renders composite output (sessions/episodes/attributions), not just raw families.

title: Demo: self-diagnosing capture outage (fault injection)
description: Wave 1 — productizes what the by-hand devloop probes did all week. Induced fault = built-in construct validity.
design: CLAIM: Sinex detects, explains, and heals its own capture failures. PROTOCOL: induce a real fault live (kill a source process; revoke a file permission); measure time-to-detection via the health aggregator; evaluate the remediation plan the runtime produces (the #2248-#2255 remediation machinery); let catch-up heal; verify source-continuity records the scar as an honest gap. INSTRUMENT: the induced fault is exact ground truth — detection latency and remediation-plan accuracy are real measurements, not narration. FALSIFIER: missed detection, wrong remediation plan, or a silently smoothed-over gap. PREREQ: none. CAPABILITY LANDING (required): the recurring by-hand probes this productizes (DLQ grouping, runtime presence, catch-up readiness) graduate into sinexctl ops verify checks — the demo may not remain a script that reimplements them.
acceptance_criteria: Two fault classes demonstrated (process kill + permission revocation); detection latency and remediation verdict reported as numbers; continuity gap visible in recall/source-status afterwards; the probes it used exist as ops verify checks; one-command rerun; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: planted-secret retroactive privacy (positive + negative controls)
description: Wave 1 — shippable with existing machinery. The unique story: retroactive redaction with the originals preserved under operator authority — no reingestion, no data loss, an interpretation-layer rewrite.
design: CLAIM: policy redaction applies retroactively via replay. PROTOCOL: plant N canary secrets + M benign lookalikes in a shell-history slice; ingest with zero rules -> DB grep finds all N (the honest baseline, demonstrating default-zero-redaction is real); enable rules; replay the source -> all N absent from live events, all M still present (false-positive control), originals inspectable in audit.archived_events under operator authority. INSTRUMENT: the grep, both directions, plus archive inspection. FALSIFIER: any canary survives replay, or any lookalike is eaten. PREREQ: none — privacy chokepoint, replay, and archive machinery exist end-to-end (chokepoint audited clean). CAPABILITY LANDING: the planted-canary fixture generator becomes a reusable test fixture; any rule-authoring friction found lands as sinexctl privacy UX fixes, not demo-local workarounds.
acceptance_criteria: One-command regeneration script plants N unique canary secrets and M benign lookalikes in a source-material-backed slice; baseline zero-redaction evidence shows canaries present in live core/reflection rows and any intentionally exercised projections. The demo enables rules without literal canary storage unless policy-material hits are explicitly classified; replay/rebuild removes canaries from live/rebuildable surfaces while preserving benign lookalikes. The packet includes the full privacy_invalidation_scope coverage report with before/after counts and commands for: core/reflection events, source material metadata/CAS, document chunks, email projection, KG rows, embeddings/cache, semantic lane outputs, telemetry materializations, operations_log, DLQ/processing failures, NATS raw/confirmed/source-material frames, recovery spool, KV checkpoints/schemas, logs, xtask history, exports, fixtures, backups/WAL, and audit archives. Archive inspection is honest: audit.archived_events may show originals under operator authority; if tombstone/purge is exercised the packet checks audit.archived_annotations/_embeddings/_tagged_items and reports residual side-table rows. Residual/impossible surfaces are caveated, not hidden: NATS retention windows, runtime spool, journald, xtask-history.db, exports/context packs, test/golden artifacts, PG physical/WAL remnants, backups. summary.json records claim, non-claims, rule ids, material/event/operation ids, per-surface statuses, exact verification commands, false-positive controls, residual caveats, and cold-reader rerun instructions.
notes:
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §16): the planted-secret demo passes only with a SURFACE MATRIX — search raw material blob, core event payload, material metadata/excerpt, search index, embedding rows, model_effect inputs/outputs, semantic lane outputs, derived summaries, context-pack artifacts, DLQ/error rows, backup/export manifests. Positive + negative controls across ALL surfaces; add privacy_invalidation_scope so redaction runs enumerate affected surfaces and produce a coverage report.
2026-07-07 surface matrix (new-gpt-pro 09: 42-row CSV + scope proposal + runnable demo commands): the demo is NOT a grep over core.events + audit.archived_events. Verified traps: document_chunks uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING so replay alone may not overwrite stale chunks; email projection upserts COALESCE/GREATEST-preserve old non-null metadata unless rows are cleared first; tombstone SQL deletes audit.archived_events but not the archived annotation/embedding/tag side tables; recovery spool holds full Event lines on disk.

title: Demo portfolio: the selected, sequenced set (selection is settled here, not in a radar)
description:
This epic IS the demo selection — the radar file/script were purged 2026-07-03 (operator direction). Demo candidates are beads under this epic, each carrying Claim / Instrument / Falsifier / Prereq in its design field plus a capability-landing requirement (a demo's packaging may be one-off; the facts it uses must land as composable capability).

INTAKE RULE (replaces the radar): a new demo idea = bd create with label demo + parent this epic, design filled with the four fields; the conductor judges it in Direction against this portfolio. The artifact shelf (.agent/demos/sinex, refreshed by devloop-refresh-demos) holds outputs; selection lives here.

THE SELECTED ARC (rationale):
- The recall-v2 baseline-arm campaign (sinex-9j9) is the committed UPLIFT demo and outranks everything here; the production restore campaign (sinex-mhk) is the live-substrate proof. Both are operator directives, tracked as top-level campaigns, not under this epic.
- Wave 1 (no prerequisites, machinery exists): planted-secret retroactive privacy; self-diagnosing outage. Both have built-in controls, both are cheap, both exercise the substrate story no competitor can show.
- Wave 2 (gated on one correctness fix): retroactive bug-fix over months — the deepest single differentiator; doubles as the replay-crash fix's acceptance test.
- Wave 3 (gated on the embedding worker): run-twice-costs-once + hybrid hit@k — the "LLM plane comes alive" pair; the lane-duel adjudication demo follows once lane promotion exists.
- Headline (gated on the campaign): the blinded resumption duel — the thesis demo, an upgrade of the campaign's baseline arm to a blinded two-agent protocol.

FOLDED (not separate demos): "Reconstruct my Tuesday with honest gaps" -> IS the recall-v2 campaign artifact (sinex-9j9). Closed-loop focus actuation -> the acceptance demo of the instruction-verb registry (sinex-r7h). Devloop-as-source self-hosting -> the demo packaging of the beads/devloop ingestion issue (sinex-hlv).
design: STRUCTURE: children are the selected portfolio — each carries Claim / Instrument / Falsifier / Prereq in its design and a capability-landing requirement (demo may be one-off; its facts must land as composable capability — see the refinement doctrine in notes). WAVES: 1 = cem.1 (planted secret) + cem.2 (outage self-diagnosis), no prereqs, buildable now; 2 = cem.3 (retroactive bug-fix; doubles as sinex-afd acceptance); 3 = cem.4 + cem.5 (both gated on 5v6 embeddings), then cem.6 (lane duel; + lane-promote 2nd + golden set a4w.1); headline = cem.7 (blinded resumption duel); breadth = cem.8 (Tuesday; gated on recall latency 0tj/g3k for full-day windows); frontier = cem.9 (actuation; gated r7h), cem.10 (devloop-as-source; gated hlv). EVIDENCE GENRE: every terminal packet has claim/non-claims/falsifier-run/regenerate.sh/cold-reader pass — the recall-v2 packet (.agent/demos/sinex/sinex-recall-v2-multisource-20260701T2200-0000) is the reference shape. External promotion of scrubbed packets is ej9/83g scope, not this epic's.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: every child either terminal (packet + independent cold-reader PASS + falsifier actually run + capability landed in product surface) or explicitly parked with its gate named; at least one wave-3 semantic demo and the cem.7 headline reach terminal; no demo contains logic that bypasses product query surfaces.
notes:
2026-07-03 refinement doctrine (operator): demos should be refined and made MORE IMPRESSIVE — but impressiveness is bought by enhancing actual capability (the algebraic-composability rule), never by demo-side machinery. A demo is thin glue: a script composing query units/refs/cards/projections that already exist as product surface, plus narration. If making a demo more impressive requires code, that code lands as a shared capability (query unit, view, composite, renderer) and the demo shrinks back to glue. Applies retroactively: recall-v2 is terminal as a campaign artifact but should be superseded by a more impressive successor once attention.stream (a4w.3.1) + the derived plane wiring (sinex-vhm) land — the successor's wow should come from those capabilities, its glue no thicker than regenerate.sh. Test for every portfolio child: could an agent reproduce the demo's substance through public product surfaces alone? If no, a capability is missing — bead it, build it, then demo it.
2026-07-07 portfolio additions (adversarial round): three new demos filling proven gaps — occurrence-idempotence re-import (regime-2 trust + replay-non-idempotence contrast), interval revision honesty (y8v contract made visible), crash-no-loss (r6d receipt barrier as showable artifact). Also: cem.6's lane-duel shell should eventually host a COUNTERFACTUAL SESSIONIZATION variant (gap-threshold parameter shadow lane -> diff -> promote, riding 0vx.7) — noted here rather than a new bead until 0vx.7 lands.

title: Consumption: recall and the derived plane pay rent
description: The hourglass diagnosis: derivation runs but almost nothing consumes it. This epic makes the derived plane pay rent — truthful product recall, query latency/read-model, query units for derived events, the embedding worker, lane promotion, ambient agent context, and honest object resolution. The strategic lever for every demo.
design: DOCTRINE: the derived plane pays rent when consumption surfaces answer real questions faster/better than raw tools, with provenance. LIMBS by function — TRUTHFUL: vhm (derived-plane wiring, no fabricated confidences). FAST: g3k (ts_orig read-model decision) + x69 (bounded diversity query); 0tj hardening feeds both. SALIENT: uxd (measured salience terms). RICH: a4w.3 composites (the payoff enumeration — attention.stream first), lv8 (query units over the derived plane), a4w.4+a4w.1 (entity quality: bounded multi-entity + golden set). DELIVERED: a4w.2 (citation-required brief), the 7z0 UX epic (daily surface/adjudication/agent arrival) consumes this epic's outputs. SEMANTIC: 5v6 (embedding worker) unlocks cem.4/5/6 and the vector plane. SEQUENCING: vhm + g3k first (truthful+fast recall is the substrate everything renders on); attention.stream next; brief once sessions/summaries are real; 5v6 parallel-safe.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: recall answers session-anchored, family-diverse, honestly-gapped timelines from derived events (not degenerate heuristics) at interactive latency on the production store; day-grain and window-grain questions are one query expression; consumption telemetry (nbi.2) shows operator+agent surfaces invoked routinely; demos cem.5/6/7 unblocked.

title: Pipeline robustness under load
description: The pipeline must survive sustained ingestion, historical imports, and its own telemetry: stream retention semantics, import pacing, boot-time schema-apply races, reflection material fragmentation, confirmation-plane consolidation, and telemetry lane separation. Most items trace to the 2026-06 prod flap and dev-loop jam incidents.
design: DOCTRINE: robustness = everything bounded and budgeted, nothing surprising at 10x. LIMBS: 9u4 (stream retention: Limits+discard:Old for confirmed-events, WorkQueue decision for raw — supersedes gh#2204's Interest claim, see bead); 2n9 (import pacing — the 1.24M-backlog prod flap must be unrepeatable); ddy remaining scope (paced restart drain, DLQ triage); exc (backfills explicit/resumable/observable); egs (confirmation-plane simplification removes the watermark lane's failure modes); x9i (telemetry lane separation so self-observation cannot drown activity). MEASUREMENTS that settle designs by numbers: n23.1 (derivation lag p50/p99 per automaton), n23.2 (storage growth model — settles granularity/pacing), n23.3 (NATS capacity model — one budget table, enforced loud at startup; the #2186->#2200->#2204 lesson generalized). SEQUENCING: measurements can run anytime (analysis lane); 9u4+egs are the structural pair; pacing before any next big import.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: a deliberate 10x backlog event (test or paced import) drains without operator surgery, OOM, or stream jam; startup fails loud when consumer/stream worst-case exceeds the host budget table; import pacing is operator-visible (rate/position/ETA); lag and growth numbers exist and the granularity/pacing decisions cite them.

title: Substrate honesty: no silent data loss, no false claims
description: The substrate's honesty is the product's premise. Groups every verified correctness/data-loss defect: replay crash-window loss, source checkpoint prongs, automaton ack window, the small-bug sweep, the CAS path escape, and schema convergence gaps. Order of attack inside this epic: crash-loss windows first (replay, checkpoints, acks), then hardening (CAS, schema-apply gaps).
design: THE LOSS-WINDOW MAP (this epic is done when this map is closed): (1) source emit-before-cursor-checkpoint — r6d.4; (2) recovery-spool discard cap — r6d.5; (3) automaton confirmed-event catch-up gap — r6d.6; (4) invalidation ack-before-recompute — r6d.7; (5) automaton ack-on-mpsc-enqueue — vxu (NB: much already on unmerged automaton-confirmed-delivery branch, merge via egs first); (6) source checkpoint prongs (page-0, journalctl cursor, bash dedup, browser context) — jda; (7) replay crash between archive-commit and re-ingest — afd. INSTRUMENTS: r6d.1 (the matrix: per-window classification bounded/unbounded, evidence, verdict) frames the campaign — run it FIRST if not concurrent; r6d.9 (crash harness) is how fixes get proven beyond inspection; r6d.8 (replayability classification) determines which windows are self-healing via replay vs true loss. HONESTY LIMBS beyond data loss: r6d.3 (health that cannot lie), r6d.2 (privacy shadow audit), w98 (backup/CAS scrub — the L0-forever claim), qig (CAS read boundary). SEQUENCING: egs (confirmation-plane merge) before vxu/r6d.6 — do not fix the old plane the branch deletes.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: the r6d.1 matrix shows every enumerated window either CLOSED (with a r6d.9 harness scenario or focused test proving it) or ACCEPTED with recorded operator rationale; no health check defaults to true; ops verify has at least one correctness-class probe per subsystem; backup/scrub capability exists (w98). The epic's claim — no silent data loss, no false claims — is then a checked assertion, not a slogan.
notes: 2026-07-07 loss-window map additions (GPT-Pro batch, verified): (8) event-engine multi-event-intent per-child settlement on a shared raw message — r6d.12 (consumer-side; distinct from every producer-side window in the map); (9) confirmed stream publishing the pre-redaction event image — z8p (authority divergence, not loss, but same honesty claim); also material-assembler DLQ-swallow (wb1) and DLQ replay-mutation (6gx) belong to the no-false-claims exit criteria. The receipt primitive (r6d.11) is now the shared fix for windows 1/4/5 + w4i, gated on r6d.5 and r6d.9. Window 5 note about the automaton-confirmed-delivery branch is stale — that content merged via egs' predecessor work; vxu is re-scoped to the prepare/commit split.

title: Verify deployed static imports (git + raindrop) after next Sinnix switch/restart
description: Nix module eval already exposes default static imports for git-commit-history and raindrop-bookmarks; dev-local drivers run. Next proof: after a Sinnix switch/restart, confirm sinexd receives those bindings through SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH and emits/updates source coverage WITHOUT relying on xtask infra dev-bindings. Cheap, gates trusting the deployment path for the rest of P12.
design: DECISION: this is a pure verification task riding the next Sinnix switch — no code unless the proof fails. Proof sequence on the live host after switch/restart: (1) systemctl show sinexd -p Environment | grep SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH and cat the manifest — confirm git-commit-history + raindrop-bookmarks bindings present from the NixOS module (not xtask dev-bindings); (2) journalctl -u sinexd since the restart — both drivers initialize, no binding-parse errors; (3) sinexctl sources status git-commit-history / raindrop-bookmarks — materials registered, recent output nonzero after the scheduled timer fires; (4) sinexctl events query with source filter — events present with post-restart ts_coided. FAILURE BRANCH: if bindings absent, the gap is nixos/modules/sources.nix staticImports rendering vs SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH plumbing — diagnose there, file discovered-from bead if structural.
acceptance_criteria: Both sources show materials + fresh events on the production runtime sourced from the NixOS-rendered binding manifest, with zero xtask/dev-bindings involvement; the four proof commands + outputs recorded in the closing comment; failure branch either empty or filed. ADDITIONS (2026-07-07): before live proof, cat the rendered SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH manifest and verify both git-commit-history and raindrop-bookmarks carry expected runtime_config plus required extra_env. The git proof must confirm GIT_CONFIG_COUNT=1 / GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=safe.directory / GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=/realm/project/sinex reach SourceBinding.extra_env (or an equivalent safe.directory mechanism) — otherwise file a blocker against the manifest renderer (extra_env is currently DROPPED by activeManifestBindings, so this is part of the proof, not a precondition). Journal lines show hosted bindings loaded from the Nix manifest with nonzero fresh material/event counts after restart/timer. If bindings are absent, distinguish sourceRuntime-disabled vs empty manifest path vs renderer omission vs exportedJson misconfiguration.
notes:
2026-07-07 drift audit correction: the git safe.directory env is expected to be MISSING at runtime today (manifest renderer drops extraEnv). The proof will likely fail on that leg first — that failure is the finding, route it to nbi.11's extra_env AC.
Accelerant pointer (2026-07-08): deployment-proof packet = .agent/scratch/new/sinex_urgent_correctness_systematic_v2.md section 14 (post-restart evidence packet spec). Re-verify against master on claim.

title: Ambient context injection: SessionStart hook feeding agents machine context
description:
Cheap + big: once sinexctl recall is honest and compact, a SessionStart hook (like the polylogue-recall hook) gives EVERY future agent session ambient machine context — the compounding consumption unlock. Implementation lives in Sinnix (dots/claude hooks + codex equivalent); Sinex owns the command output contract and runtime-target descriptor. Budget-bounded, honest-caveats output (no fabricated confidence). Gate on recall truthfulness (P7 continuation) being good enough that the injected context is not misleading.

Refs: handoff pack P13.
design: DECISION: SessionStart hook in the sinnix repo (dots/claude hooks; pattern: sessionstart-polylogue-recall.sh) injecting compact sinexctl recall output against the prod/dev runtime target via the runtime-target descriptor for auth; degrade SILENTLY when sinexd is down; strict token budget (<1K tokens). Sinex side owns only the command output contract. Coordinate: implementation commit lands in sinnix, not sinex.
acceptance_criteria: New Claude/Codex sessions open with a machine-context block; zero hang and zero error noise when the daemon is down; injected block stays under budget on a busy window.
notes: 2026-07-03 completed via Sinnix commit 247888330d8b04431adb994241d113969a1822f3: added sessionstart-sinex-recall hook, Claude SessionStart registration, Codex generated SessionStart hook registration, executable shim, and shared docs. Activated with nix develop --command switch and pushed to Sinnix master. Installed verification: ~/.claude/hooks/sessionstart-sinex-recall.sh and ~/.local/bin/sessionstart-sinex-recall are executable store paths; ~/.codex/hooks.json SessionStart includes sessionstart-sinex-recall; installed hook emitted a 98-word checkout-local machine-context block; no-target mode emitted 0 bytes stdout/stderr. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/ambient-agent-recall-sessionstart-20260703T141826Z.
close_reason: Completed: Sinnix commit 247888330d8b04431adb994241d113969a1822f3 adds the shared sessionstart-sinex-recall hook, Claude SessionStart registration, Codex generated SessionStart hook registration, executable shim, and docs; it was activated with nix develop --command switch and pushed to Sinnix master. Installed verification: ~/.claude/hooks/sessionstart-sinex-recall.sh and ~/.local/bin/sessionstart-sinex-recall are executable store paths; ~/.codex/hooks.json SessionStart includes sessionstart-sinex-recall; installed hook against the checkout-local Sinex target emitted a 98-word machine-context block; no-target mode emitted 0 bytes stdout/stderr. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/ambient-agent-recall-sessionstart-20260703T141826Z.

title: Embedding worker over the local LiteLLM gateway: activate the dormant vector/LLM plane
description:
One worker loop activates five dormant subsystems: events_without_embeddings -> embed via LiteLLM gateway (127.0.0.1:4000, modules/services/litellm.nix) -> store_event_embedding, recording ModelEffectRecord and emitting llm.budget.ledger events.

This turns on: pgvector knn/hybrid search (currently zero production callers), doc-search VectorSearchParams, model_effects repo, sinexctl llm budget-report (currently reports over events nothing emits), and — most importantly — establishes the record/replay ModelEffectRecord pattern the whole L3 semantic plane needs (llm.rs is a complete typed contract with NO model client anywhere).

Embedding_producer currently emits receipts for embeddings never computed (embedding_producer.rs:50-71) — make the receipts true or replace them.

Refs: handoff P8; audit 015 §B6.
design: DECISION: a worker loop, preferably an automaton for provenance (not a sidecar): events_without_embeddings -> embed via LiteLLM local model (127.0.0.1:4000; model name from litellm model_list in sinnix modules/services/litellm.nix) -> store_event_embedding + write ModelEffectRecord (provider/model/input-hash) + emit llm.budget.ledger event. Wire hybrid doc search's dormant VectorSearchParams (document_search.rs:80-88). TARGETS: sinex-db/src/repositories/embeddings.rs:79-270 (store_event_embedding, knn_search, hybrid_search, events_without_embeddings — all currently caller-less), automata/embedding_producer.rs:50-71 (receipt-only producer to make truthful or replace). Record dimensions/model in the effect key so model swaps re-derive. Batch + rate-limit.
acceptance_criteria: llm budget-report non-zero; knn/hybrid search returns results on the dev store; re-run over the same corpus -> ~100% ledger cache hits (this is the record/replay demo substrate). VERIFY: xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(embedding)'; live: embed a backlog on the dev store, run a hybrid search via sinexctl docs.
notes:
2026-07-06 symbol map (all verified on master, zero-discovery start): DB SIDE — core.embedding_models registry: register model via INSERT (embeddings.rs:30), get model_id UUID; store_event_embedding validates dims via validate_embedding_for_model (:79,:87); events_without_embeddings(model_id, event_types, limit) returns EmbeddingTarget{event_id, event_type, text_for_embedding=payload::text} (:145-175, NOT-EXISTS anti-join on core.event_embeddings). EFFECTS — ModelEffectRequest.composite_key() = blake3(provider/model/prompt_hash/schema/input_hash); ModelEffectRecord::new auto-hashes output (llm.rs:203-243); hash_model_input (:246); can_replay(policy) (:253); repo: model_effects.rs find_by_composite_key(:37)/insert(:55)/has_effect(:100). BUDGET — LlmBudgetLedgerPayload exists (events/payloads/llm.rs:90). WORKER SHAPE — a polling loop fits the automaton contract cleanly: implement the automaton with scan_historical = backlog sweep via events_without_embeddings (satisfies the bridge's supports_historical REQUIREMENT, automaton_runtime.rs:197-202) and continuous = process document.chunked confirmed events with the real embed call, replacing/absorbing the receipts-only embedding_producer.rs. HTTP — workspace reqwest has NO form feature; LiteLLM is OpenAI-compatible: POST 127.0.0.1:4000/v1/embeddings JSON body. Effect-first idempotency: has_effect(composite_key) before calling; on hit, reuse recorded output (can_replay).
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §14): embedding units are semantic materials, NEVER raw high-rate telemetry. Initial allowlist: rawlog entries, knowledgebase note chunks, Polylogue session/work-event summaries (post-h2x quarantine rule), canonical terminal command+cwd+bounded output summary, browser page title/url/snippet, context-pack summaries, analysis claims. Excluded by default: journald, systemd state, fs events, provider telemetry, raw terminal output. Gate: hit@k benchmark (cem.5) before any bulk sweep. Preflights h2x (agent-session quarantine) + 2k2 (cost fuse) now block this bead — the former 4j2.8/a4w.5 hard blocks were removed as false full-blockers.
2026-07-07: the dr-04 embedding-retrieval DeepResearch run FAILED (prompt did not attach — generic filler in dr-failed-generic-*.report.md). Re-run /realm/inbox/gpt-pro-handoff/sinex/dr-04-personal-corpus-embedding-retrieval.md before committing to an embedding strategy, or rely on cem.5's paired experiment as the decision gate.
2026-07-08 (post-ratification, two rulings bind this bead — both settled, do not re-litigate):
1. h2x FAIL-CLOSED ALLOWLIST is the activation contract. First run embeds ONLY event/material families named in an explicit worker-config allowlist; everything unlisted is skipped AND COUNTED (skip counts in the worker report). Agent-session/Polylogue-origin content and the ENTIRE reflection lane are off the allowlist. A denylist is forbidden: it silently sweeps sources that land mid-flight. Ship the dry-run fixture (h2x AC) proving the selected family list + per-family counts BEFORE the first real run.
2. 4j2.8 SHARED EFFECT-LEDGER is the ratified ownership shape. Bake the effect key as (provider, model, content-hash) from day one — plus embedding dimensions in the key so model swaps re-derive rather than silently mixing vector spaces. This is exactly the ModelEffectRecord composite key this bead already establishes; getting it right here means cross-repo reuse with Polylogue costs nothing later and needs no migration. Polylogue keeps its own pipeline; neither system depends on the other existing.
3. embedding_producer stays DEFAULT-OFF (ratified nbi.4) until this bead lands real model effects — its receipt-only emissions are a truthful-looking noop. Re-enabling it is part of this beads landing, via ijz6s default set.
4. This bead produces the cost measurements 4j2.8 remains open for (corpus size x embedding cost, cache-hit rate on re-run). Record them in the close reason so 4j2.8 can finish.

title: Recall/event-query latency: diagnose source-filtered path, decide ts_orig projection read model
description:
Live evidence: browser/git source-specific event queries TIMED OUT via the API; broad direct core.events source scans were slow enough to cancel. First proof: bounded EXPLAIN (or query-planner instrumentation) of the generated event-card/source-filter query — no more unbounded scans.

Hard constraint (U5, verified): a ts_orig continuous aggregate is IMPOSSIBLE — TimescaleDB CAs must bucket on the partition dimension, and core.events partitions by UUIDv7 id (ts_coided). ts_orig btree indexes exist but give NO chunk exclusion. If historical recall windows need speed, the answer is a projection/read table keyed by ts_orig (maintained by trigger or consumer), NOT a CA and NOT repartitioning (rejected in gh#2182 — replay economics depend on id partitioning).

Deliverables: EXPLAIN evidence, indexed-path fix if one exists, else a designed recall read-model projection with freshness/invalidation semantics (DerivationSpec).

Refs: handoff pack U5 + P16; ACTIVE-LOOP Next Action 2.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03): measurement-gated two-branch plan. The EXPLAIN evidence picks the branch; BOTH branches are fully designed so the executor only measures and executes.

STEP 1 — EVIDENCE (always): EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) the exact generated shapes: (a) recall/context window query (ts_orig range + activity families), (b) source-filtered event query (source='browser.history' + ts_orig range — the one that timed out), (c) show-ref lookup by id. Run on the dev store at current scale. Record plans in the bead/PR. Constraint (verified, do not re-derive): core.events partitions by UUIDv7 id, so NO ts_orig chunk exclusion is possible and a ts_orig CA on core.events is IMPOSSIBLE; repartitioning is rejected (replay economics depend on id partitioning; see gh#2182 trail).

BRANCH A — index path suffices (plans show index scans with acceptable buffers after, e.g., a missing composite index like (source, ts_orig) or (event_type, ts_orig)): add the composite index(es) to EVENTS_REQUIRED_INDEXES (note gh#2196: indexes outside the required-set are invisible to drift detection — add them THERE), done. Choose A when interactive windows (<=48h) come in under ~200ms on dev-scale data.

BRANCH B — recall projection table (choose when index scans still touch all chunks / historical windows stay seconds-slow):
- recall.timeline: a ts_orig-PARTITIONED TimescaleDB hypertable. KEY UNLOCK: the ts_orig-partitioning impossibility applies only to core.events (partitioned by id); a PROJECTION table may partition on ts_orig freely — giving chunk exclusion for time windows AND, later, real ts_orig-bucketed CAs (hourly activity rollups by occurrence time) for free.
- Columns: ts_orig, event_id, source, event_type, family, session_id (nullable), title_snippet (card-grade, disclosure-filtered), lane. No payloads — cards hydrate by id on demand.
- Maintenance: insert hook in the event engine post-admission (same site as confirmed publish), activity lane only; plus a rebuild operation. Declared as DerivationSpec derivation:recall.timeline_projection@v1 with RebuildOnInputChange freshness and replay/archive/redaction invalidation (redaction invalidation matters: title_snippet must re-render when privacy rules change).
- This is honest cache doctrine: the projection is rebuildable, never authoritative; recall reads it, show/trace resolve against core.events.

SEQUENCING: coordinate with telemetry lane (sinex-x9i) — if the lane split lands first, measure AFTER it (reflection volume may be most of the scan cost, changing the branch decision).
acceptance_criteria: EXPLAIN(ANALYZE,BUFFERS) plans for the three shapes recorded BEFORE code; branch decision written with the numbers; Branch A: new composite indexes present in EVENTS_REQUIRED_INDEXES and interactive windows meet budget; Branch B: recall.timeline hypertable partitioned on ts_orig with DerivationSpec + rebuild op + engine insert hook, browser/git source queries return interactively on the dev store, and a redaction change invalidates/re-renders snippets. VERIFY: EXPLAIN artifacts in the PR; live dev-store latency before/after; xtask test -p sinex-db on the projection repo.
notes:
Branch B variant note (U5): a PLAIN day-partitioned postgres table (native range partitions) is the acceptable simpler alternative to a ts_orig hypertable — same chunk-exclusion benefit, less machinery. Choose the hypertable ONLY if ts_orig-bucketed CAs (hourly occurrence-time rollups) are actually wanted at implementation time; historical-backfill CA refresh windows are a known caveat either way.

2026-07-03 Branch A decision/proof: live dev store had ~69.3M core.events rows and ~57.8M webhistory rows. Before: source-filtered browser query with source = ANY(singleton), ts_orig >= now()-48h, ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 201 scanned pkey/id order, removed 880,681 rows, hit/read 193,876/107,747 buffers, and took 18,647.982 ms. Seven-source recall-family shape with ORDER BY id took 1,341.830 ms. Branch A proof: singleton source equality plus ORDER BY ts_orig DESC, id DESC used ix_events_source_ts_orig and returned full event-card rows in 1.156 ms; broad 48h occurrence-window shape used ix_events_ts_orig and returned 201 full-card rows in 9.923 ms. Decision: fix generated composable-query SQL shape; no projection table for this slice. Tracked report: crate/sinex-db/docs/recall_query_latency_2026_07_03.md. Verification: xtask test -p sinex-db -E test(time_window_listing_paginates_by_ts_orig_then_id) job 2001387 exit 0; xtask check -p sinex-db job 2001388 exit 0; xtask check -p sinex-primitives job 2001389 exit 0.
close_reason: Completed by PR #2271. Branch A selected from live EXPLAIN evidence: singleton source equality plus occurrence-order event-card listings use existing ts_orig indexes and meet interactive budget on the 69.3M-row dev store; no projection table needed for this slice. Verification: focused sinex-db regression job 2001387, sinex-db check job 2001388, sinex-primitives check job 2001389, and pre-push changed-strict passed before merge.

title: Recall truthfulness: derived-plane wiring, honest confidences, browser/git participation
description:
The highest-value consumption packet, partially landed (root sinexctl recall + source caveats exist on local master). Remaining to make the command truthful and complete:
1. Wire recall/context to the derived plane: consume activity.session.boundary + hourly/daily summaries instead of the degenerate one-session heuristic (context.rs:718-732). Six automata currently run as pure cost.
2. Kill fabricated numbers: hardcoded confidences 0.72/0.55/0.45 at context.rs:508,530,847 — replace with evidence-derived values or remove the field (every number carries evidence-provenance or is absent).
3. Diagnose browser/git non-participation: recall windows 2h-48h returned only journald/shell.atuin/fs-watcher despite browser/git events existing (~58M page.visited parsed). Depends on the query-latency diagnosis issue.
4. Sessions/summaries/caveats as real product semantics so demos never need raw SQL.

Refs: handoff pack P7; feeds the recall-v2 campaign.
design: DECISION: session-anchored, family-diverse timeline built from activity.session.boundary + activity.summary.hourly + canonical commands + page visits + docs; every row an EventCardView with resolvable refs; honest gaps rendered from source-continuity data (caveats already landed). Replace hardcoded confidences (context.rs:508,530,847) with evidence-derived values (evidence-count/recency) or drop the numbers — every number carries evidence-provenance or is absent. Degenerate focus-session heuristic (context.rs:718-732) is replaced by real session boundaries. Proven query shape: demo/sinex-recall/recall.sh + recall-v2 shelf artifacts. Keep --desktop heuristics only if re-derived honestly.
acceptance_criteria: Recall rows resolve via show <ref>; session rows come from the session detector (disable it -> recall degrades visibly); capture gaps render as gaps; JSON stable for MCP; no fabricated confidence numbers remain (rg '0\.72|0\.55|0\.45' crate/sinexctl clean). VERIFY: live checkout-local dev store (xtask run core --bg, manifest .agent/dev/dev-source-bindings.json); parity check against a recall-v2 shelf artifact. PITFALL: measure ts_orig query latency with EXPLAIN first (sinex-g3k) — do not promise interactive latency.
notes:
2026-07-03 follow-up proof update: recall source caveats now include source-status gap details even when expected sources contribute rows, and expected source descriptors split event_source_id from coverage_source_id (shell.atuin->terminal.atuin-history, webhistory->browser.history, fs-watcher->fs, git->git-commit-history). Proofs: focused recall tests job 2001344 exit 0 (4 passed); final sinexctl build job 2001345 exit 0; live recall --window 1m --limit 50 showed terminal/browser/filesystem/git absent from the tiny window while active source-status caveats pointed at source_driver:terminal.atuin-history, source_driver:browser.history, source_driver:fs, and source_driver:git-commit-history; terminal/git gap caveats carried runtime_binding_stalled details. Demo packet: .agent/demos/sinex/recall-source-gaps-live-20260703T0919Z (manifest refreshed). 2026-07-03 PR #2269 added ContextSessionView and recall payload sessions[] populated from derived.session-detector/activity.session.boundary cards, plus recall.sessions.absent caveat when recall has activity but no session rows. Proofs: job 2001381 recall_machine_output tests passed; jobs 2001382/2001385 primitive context schema test passed; jobs 2001383/2001384 package checks passed; pre-push changed-strict checked sinex-primitives+sinexctl. Remaining before close: recall-v2 shelf parity/cold-reader proof and stronger live session-detector degradation proof.

2026-07-03 live proof update: produced .agent/demos/sinex/recall-truthfulness-live-20260703T1558Z and refreshed demo indexes. Production recall --window 48h --limit 5 returned six source families (activitywatch, git, wm.hyprland, fs-watcher, webhistory, shell.atuin) in 175 ms total, with explicit terminal/browser runtime_binding_stalled caveats and no old hardcoded confidence literals (rg '0\.72|0\.55|0\.45' crate/sinexctl/src crate/sinex-primitives/src clean). This resolves the browser/git participation and fabricated-confidence proof parts; remaining before close is live checkout-local/dev parity plus real session-detector degradation/session-row proof.
close_reason: Completed: live production proof showed browser/git participation and honest source caveats; checkout-local dev proof packet .agent/demos/sinex/recall-v2-dev-parity-current-20260703T1604Z verifies Recall v2 parity, payload.sessions with a derived.session-detector activity.session.boundary row, and show-resolvable hourly/git/browser/session refs. Focused regression job 2001391 passed and sinexctl rebuild job 2001392 preceded the final command-path capture; no old hardcoded confidence literals remain.

title: Confirmation-plane consolidation: merge automaton-confirmed-delivery branch, delete watermark lane
description:
Two confirmation planes run simultaneously: SSE consumes the WATERMARK stream + DB refetch (api/sse_bus.rs:1-7,351) while the confirmed stream carries full payloads (persist.rs:135-143); ConfirmationBuffer is a constructed, registered, health-probed zombie fed by nothing (initialize.rs:150-159, service_container.rs:658-713).

PRIOR WORK — USE IT: unmerged branch feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery already implements most of this (Option-C, verified 2026-06-28 live dogfood: per-automaton confirmed-Event consumers, ConfirmationBuffer/provisional/refetch/watermark-consumer/#2202-fallback DELETED, SSE migrated, producer durability gate, net -2021 lines, e2e test_jetstream_e2e_event_flow passing). Rebase/merge that branch rather than reimplementing. Then finish Phase-3b: watermark-topology teardown (watermark still published with no reader).

Interacts with gh#2204 (retention: Interest policy fits the post-merge consumer topology) — sequence them together.

Refs: handoff pack P3; memory session_2026_06_28/29; branch log.
design: DECISION: one bus — the full-payload confirmed stream. Order: (1) rebase/merge feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery (verified: SSE migrated, ConfirmationBuffer/provisional/refetch/watermark-consumer deleted, -2021 lines, e2e passing) instead of reimplementing; (2) Phase-3b watermark-topology teardown — watermark publish paths (persist.rs:174-376, jetstream_consumer/prepare.rs:346-504), retry queue, _CONFIRMATIONS stream provisioning; (3) any residual ConfirmationBuffer/confirmation_handler.rs/service_container health plumbing on the merged result. Pre-release: no deprecation shims. NATS stream config lives in runtime/jetstream_streams.rs AND nixos provisioning — update both. Preserve the SSE disclosure boundary (filter by source/type before payload disclosure).
acceptance_criteria: SSE clients receive events with no DB refetch; rg -n 'ConfirmationBuffer|confirmations\.' crate/ --type rust -> tests only, then tests deleted; NATS stream inventory no longer provisions the watermark lane; each event written exactly once to NATS post-persist. VERIFY: e2e SSE subscription test; xtask test -p sinexd; xtask infra smoke --run-core; check sinexctl events stream/watch paths for watermark assumptions.
notes:
2026-07-03 confirmation-plane teardown branch feature/runtime/confirmation-plane-teardown: removed legacy watermark/confirmation-retry streams, provisional buffer runtime, service health buffer component, and SSE DB-refetch path. Confirmed-events stream now carries one full post-redaction event per persisted event; tests updated away from per-kind watermark semantics. Fixed helper to emit events.confirmed.*.*.<type>, added focused primitive test, and corrected docs stream name from _CONFIRMED_EVENTS to _CONFIRMED. title: Telemetry lane separation: self-observation must not drown the activity plane
description:
Fable packet P5 + live evidence: self-observation-like sources were 84.8% of top-source 1h events and 53.1% of 24h during dev catch-up; bursts of derived.activity-window catch-up hit 97% of 5-min windows; a classifier bug counted bare source='sinex' as ingestion in telemetry components. Short recall windows drown in the system's own reflection.

Design decision (do NOT just add a CLI flag): physically separate telemetry (own stream/table/retention), throttle/sample self-observation, or a projection/read-model that keeps product recall activity-first. Source role/lane semantics belong in shared primitives or schema. Ground in current counts (whole-store mix is ~84% page.visited / 11% entity-derived / 2.6% telemetry-like — the dominance is window-relative, not absolute).

Overlaps the stream-retention issue prong 3 (self-obs volume). Refs: handoff pack P5; ACTIVE-LOOP Next Action 3.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03): physical lane separation. Sampling/throttling and query-side-only filtering are REJECTED (rationale at bottom).

SHAPE:
1. Shared SourceRole in sinex-primitives (Activity | Reflection), resolved from the source registry — ONE definition site. The throughput classifier bug (bare source='sinex' counted as ingestion) is fixed by consuming this enum; no string prefix matching anywhere. Land this first as its own slice — it is independently valuable and the migration key.
2. Reflection events publish to their own stream: {env}.sinex.reflection.> with its own retention (short, e.g. 7d / tight byte cap — reflection is operational evidence, not life history). Coordinate naming with the self-observation -> reflection rename (sinex-lb8): do the rename's event-type/source-id decisions in the same design pass so streams/tables are named once.
3. Reflection persists into its own hypertable (reflection.events, IDENTICAL column schema to core.events, same provenance constraints). The decisive argument for a separate table over a role column: retention and compression policy can only diverge at hypertable level, and reflection wants aggressive retention that must NEVER be able to touch operator life-events. Rebuildability makes short retention safe.
4. Default consumption reads activity only: automaton fan-out subscribes the activity confirmed stream (reflection-consuming automata opt in explicitly); recall/context/query units default to activity; --include-reflection (or lane: reflection query constraint) opts in. Telemetry CAs move to the reflection table (they bucket on ts_coided — fine there).
5. Cross-lane queries UNION explicitly through the query substrate's lane parameter — uniform event semantics, two physical homes.

MIGRATION ORDER: (1) SourceRole + classifier fix; (2) reflection stream + publish routing; (3) reflection.events table + engine consumer; (4) CAs move; (5) retention policies; (6) default-exclusion in consumers/query surfaces. Each step independently verifiable; the system runs mixed mid-migration (old rows stay in core.events; queries treat pre-migration reflection rows via SourceRole filter until a scoped archive sweep moves or ages them out).

REJECTED:
- Sampling/throttling: destroys operational evidence and does nothing for the rows already dominating short recall windows.
- Query-side filtering only (projection/WHERE): keeps paying storage, stream fill, fan-out, and retention coupling; the classifier bug proved string-filter approaches drift.
- Same table + role column: cannot diverge retention/compression; every activity query still scans reflection chunks in shared windows.

EVIDENCE BASE: self-observation-like sources = 84.8% of top-source 1h events, 53.1% of 24h during catch-up; whole-store mix only ~2.6% telemetry — the dominance is window-relative, which is exactly why lane separation (not deletion) is right.
acceptance_criteria: SourceRole enum is the single classification site (rg finds no ad-hoc self-observation string matching in telemetry components); classifier counts bare 'sinex' as reflection; reflection stream + table exist with their own retention; automaton consumers and recall/query default to activity lane (disable-and-observe test: reflection volume no longer appears in recall windows); telemetry CLI + CAs still work against the reflection table; a mixed-lane UNION query works through the lane parameter. VERIFY: live dev capture before/after; CA refresh; xtask test vm --category smoke for the deployment shape.
notes:
2026-07-04 reflection storage/read-model slice: added reflection schema metadata and managed reflection.events hypertable via schema apply (LIKE core.events shape, FK fixups, no-update/payload/material-bounds triggers, 1d chunks, 30d retention, 7d compression policy). Moved self-observation telemetry read models/current_health/gateway/stream/assembly/source/metric/batch CAs to reflection.events while keeping activity read models on core.events. Updated transport decision doc to state remaining scope is lane-aware event-engine writes and general query defaults. Verification: xtask test -p sinex-schema --lib -E 'test(reflection_event_trigger_sql_targets_reflection_table)' job 2001705 passed; xtask test -p sinex-db --test database job 2001709 passed; xtask check -p sinex-schema -p sinex-db job 2001710 passed; xtask schema strict-diff reported drift_count=0; git diff --check passed. Selector/tooling note: filtered sinex-db registry test attempts 2001706/2001708 matched no tests due xtask/nextest output-shape/selector confusion, so verified with the whole database test binary.

2026-07-04 PR opened for reflection storage/read-model slice: https://github.com/Sinity/sinex/pull/2363. Branch feature/telemetry/reflection-storage, commit e8c6f3d2e. Pre-push xtask check --changed-strict origin/master passed for sinex-db + sinex-schema. Remaining sinex-x9i scope unchanged: lane-aware event-engine writes into reflection.events, query/recall activity defaults, explicit mixed-lane UNION, and live proof once writes route to reflection.

2026-07-04 reflection event routing slice on feature/telemetry/reflection-event-routing: stream-batch persistence now carries an explicit EventStorageLane, with activity/default rows going to core.events and reflection consumer rows going to reflection.events. IngestService passes JetStreamEventLane::Reflection into JetStreamConsumer, which configures AdmissionService to call insert_stream_batch_into(EventStorageLane::Reflection). Direct EventRepository::insert/insert_with_tx remain activity/core by default. Verification: xtask test -p sinex-db --test database job 2001713 passed and includes reflection-lane insertion proof; xtask test -p sinex-db --lib -E 'test(event_storage_lane_targets_are_explicit)' job 2001714 passed; xtask check -p sinex-db -p sinexd job 2001715 passed. Note: exact database test filter job 2001712 matched no tests, so the full database binary was used. xtask check --fmt job 2001716 exposed unrelated pre-existing formatter drift outside this slice; touched files were manually aligned with formatter output.

2026-07-04 PR opened for reflection event routing slice: https://github.com/Sinity/sinex/pull/2364. Branch feature/telemetry/reflection-event-routing, commit 0ccd1d511. Pre-push xtask check --changed-strict origin/master passed for sinex-db + sinexd. title: Automaton progress outruns durable outputs: checkpoint/state-save before emit durability (incl. ack-on-mpsc-enqueue)
description:
Automaton progress can durably outrun output durability — the catch-up mitigation does NOT close this window. Verified on master eedc05256 (021 grok, supersedes the 2026-07-06 reconciliation that called this structurally repaired): process_one records processed input in memory (adapter/process.rs:231, also on benign-failure settlements), process_batch may durably save the adapter checkpoint BEFORE returning outputs (process.rs:389-424), the caller emits only after return (adapter/mod.rs:517-527), and emit is mpsc-only (handles.rs:364-413). The bridge KV checkpoint layer (#2358) is INERT for adapter automata (manages_own_checkpoints=true, adapter/mod.rs:479 -> checkpoint_manager=None), so restart catch-up resumes from the possibly-premature adapter checkpoint (automaton_runtime.rs:205-220) and can never repair the gap. Crash between checkpoint-save and NATS-persist of outputs = input behind checkpoint, derived output never durable, silent loss. Same class: Windowed emit() mutates accumulator state before output durability (session.rs:171,199; analytics.rs:293) and shutdown saves final state regardless of outstanding receipts (adapter/mod.rs:545-574).

REMAINING SCOPE (both required): (1) the checkpoint-after-durable-emit reorder — prepare/commit split so record_processed_input/checkpoint/state-save advance only on a durable emission receipt (r6d.11 boundary), implemented ONCE in the consolidated lifecycle core (p5ou) covering process_one/process_batch, WindowedWrapper::emit, timer flush, and shutdown; (2) the r6d.9 crash harness scenarios (checkpoint-before-output, output-RawAccepted-before-DB-settle, confirmed-ack-mpsc-backlog) as proof. Historical catch-up (PR #2299/#2358) remains correct but is sound only under the invariant this bead restores: checkpoints never outrun durable outputs.
design: DECISION (settled): prefer making historical catch-up mandatory for ALL bridge-checkpointed automata (uniform) over restructuring ack ordering; only a pure-stream automaton that cannot scan history gets its ack moved downstream of processing. TARGETS: runtime/jetstream_consumer.rs:322-331 (ack site), stream/runner/listener.rs RunnerConfirmedEventHandler, automaton_runtime.rs:224-239 (catch-up), :267-270 (checkpoint cadence), :530 (DeliverPolicy::New assumption — you are making its catch-up assumption universally true). PRECONDITION: audit feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery first — its per-automaton confirmed-events consumers may already reshape this; do not write against master if the merge (sinex-egs) lands first.
acceptance_criteria: Automaton live bridge, historical replay, failure-settlement, timer flush, and shutdown paths do not call record_processed_input or save state/checkpoints past an input/flush until output, no-output (NoOutputSettled), or durable-debt receipt unlocks progress. process_one/process_batch split into prepare_outputs and commit_processed_inputs phases. Benign settlements (Commit-with-no-output, failure-queue/park/quarantine) require durable failure/debt evidence before marking processed — direct-transport warning-only failure routing (event_transport.rs:106-134) does not count. Windowed timer flushes get a flush_id and a state-commit barrier: shutdown must not save state past an unsettled flush receipt. Crash harness covers checkpoint-before-output, output-RawAccepted-before-DB-settle, and confirmed-ack-mpsc-backlog windows.
notes:
2026-07-06 curation: description reconciled with master; prior-work trap (feature/dev/automaton-confirmed-delivery) DEFUSED — merged via sinex-egs/PR #2289. Added dependency on sinex-r6d.9: the remaining AC is exactly one harness scenario (kill-9-with-mpsc-backlog). Earlier notes: PR #2299 catch-up mandate; feature/runtime/automata-bridge-contract AutomatonSpec::contract coverage; feature/runtime/automaton-lossless-catchup checkpoint-before-catchup regression.

2026-07-07 grok verification (master eedc05256): the 'structurally repaired' framing is INVALIDATED. Catch-up heals ack-on-mpsc ONLY if checkpoints never outrun durable outputs — master violates this: process_one records processed input (process.rs:231), process_batch may durably save the adapter checkpoint BEFORE returning outputs (process.rs:389-424), caller emits only after return (adapter/mod.rs:517-527), and emit is mpsc-only (handles.rs:364-413). Bridge KV checkpoint (#2358) is INERT for adapter automata: manages_own_checkpoints=true (adapter/mod.rs:479) → checkpoint_manager=None → catch-up resumes from the adapter's own (possibly premature) checkpoint (automaton_runtime.rs:205-220). Crash between checkpoint-save and NATS-persist of outputs = input behind checkpoint, output never durable, catch-up never repairs. REMAINING SCOPE therefore = code reorder (checkpoint/record-processed only after durable output receipt; shared receipt primitive with r6d.4/r6d.7/w4i per new/sinex_urgent_correctness_systematic_v2.md packet 4) PLUS the r6d.9 harness proof. See .agent/scratch/021-grok-2026-07-07-audit-verification.md.
2026-07-07 red team confirms the prepare/commit split shape and adds: timer_flush mutates persisted_state then emits via mpsc (process.rs:329-356) — a closed-in-memory accumulator can be checkpointed at shutdown with its outputs never durable. Shutdown is not a receipt boundary today (adapter/mod.rs:545-574 saves final state regardless of outstanding receipts).
2026-07-07: reset in_progress -> open. No WIP branch exists and the bead is hard-blocked by egs + r6d.11 + r6d.9 (Batch-A ordering: receipt primitive first, then this checkpoint reorder). The stale claim predated the reframe that falsified the 'structurally repaired' status.
2026-07-07: another instance of the same class — Windowed emit() mutates accumulator state BEFORE output durability (session.rs:171 mem::take(window_event_ids), :199 reset_session(); analytics emit does the same via take at analytics.rs:293) so a crash after emit-return but before durable output loses the whole session/window irrecoverably (state reset persists at next checkpoint). The prepare/commit split in this bead's AC must cover WindowedWrapper::emit, not only process_one/process_batch.
2026-07-08 architecture: implement the prepare/commit split ONCE in the consolidated automaton lifecycle core (p5ou) rather than per-wrapper — vxu/r6d.11/5s6/0vx.2 otherwise each become a 4x cross-product patch across the wrapper zoo.
2026-07-08 planning session: description rewritten to lead with the verified defect chain (previously led with the falsified structurally-repaired framing; the correction lived only in notes). Title updated to match. Remaining scope unchanged: prepare/commit reorder via p5ou core against the r6d.11 receipt boundary + r6d.9 harness proof.
2026-07-08 session: not started. Hard-blocked on sinex-r6d.11 + sinex-r6d.9 per this bead's own DEPENDS ON; r6d.11 only got its mechanism half (4as3) landed this session. The prepare/commit split this bead needs (process_one/process_batch, WindowedWrapper::emit, timer flush, shutdown) is explicitly meant to land ONCE in the consolidated automaton lifecycle core (sinex-p5ou) per the 2026-07-08 architecture note already on this bead, not as a standalone patch — do not implement piecemeal against process.rs directly.
2026-07-09 session: located the EXACT injection point for the r6d.9 harness's primary
scenario (checkpoint-before-output), narrowing this bead's own "process.rs:389-424" citation
to an actionable line pair:

crate/sinexd/src/runtime/automaton/adapter/process.rs:
- Line 391-394: `save_state_with_file_fallback(...)` — the DURABLE checkpoint write —
  succeeds inside the `Ok(())` branch.
- Line 430: `Ok(outputs)` — process_batch returns outputs to its caller for emission, which
  happens LATER in adapter/mod.rs (~517-527), after this function has already returned.

A fail-point armed between these two points (after line 394's Ok(()), before line 430) and
triggered via `std::process::exit()` reproduces the exact crash window this bead describes:
checkpoint durably saved, outputs never durably emitted.

Did NOT implement this fail-point or the surrounding crash-window test this session. Two
reasons: (1) AutomatonRuntime<N> (adapter/mod.rs:84-122) has no existing test-hook field
comparable to jetstream_consumer's `confirmation_failures_remaining` pattern — adding one
means touching every constructor call site, a wider blast radius than the fail-point itself;
(2) building the REST of the harness (spawn real sinexd via xtask::sandbox::orchestrator's
already-existing `start_test_event_engine_with_config`/`TestEventEngineHandle` process
infra — confirmed this exists and is exactly the right primitive, so r6d.9 is NOT starting
from zero on process orchestration — then drive a real event through live NATS+DB, kill at
the armed fail-point via `child.start_kill()`, restart, assert the input is durably behind
the checkpoint) is genuinely substantial integration-test engineering, and this session was
already very long with the host under sustained heavy concurrent-agent load (every compile
in this session took 100-400+ seconds). Attempting to rush this to "done" now risked either
not finishing correctly or finishing without adequate verification — exactly the kind of
false-claim risk this epic (sinex-r6d: "no silent data loss, no false claims") exists to
prevent.

CONCRETE NEXT-SESSION STARTING POINT (should make this bounded, not open-ended): (1) add one
`#[cfg(any(test, feature = "testing"))] fail_point_after_checkpoint: Option<Arc<AtomicBool>>`
field to AutomatonRuntime, threaded through its test-only constructor path only (production
constructors pass None, zero behavior change); (2) call it right after process.rs:394's
Ok(()) arm; (3) reuse xtask::sandbox::orchestrator::start_test_event_engine_with_config to
spawn a real sinexd, arm the fail-point via env var, drive one event, assert the process
self-exits at the fail point (proves the hook fires), then verify on restart that
catch-up/backfill does NOT repair the gap (proves the RED/pre-fix case this bead's AC wants
proven before the fix lands).
2026-07-09 session (2nd investigation pass): attempted to extend the validated fail-point
pattern to the invalidation-ack-before-recompute scenario (r6d.7's window) for real this time
(not just assessing feasibility) — got through the setup investigation, stopped short of
implementation once the remaining unknowns compounded past what's safely completable in this
session.

Additional concrete findings beyond the 4th-pass note (crate/sinexd/src/runtime/automaton/adapter/run.rs):
- The invalidation stream name is a SIMPLE, direct computation —
  env.nats_stream_name("SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_DERIVED_INVALIDATIONS") (run.rs:60) — NOT tied to the
  full JetStreamTopology bootstrap. A test does not need the whole event-engine topology
  bootstrapped; creating just this one stream directly is enough to unblock subscription setup.
- The subscription itself is an EPHEMERAL JetStream push consumer with a queue group keyed by
  automaton name (run.rs:61-71) — no durable_name, so no restart-position-persistence subtlety
  like the confirmed-events consumer has.
- The payload format is NOT a free-form byte string: handle_invalidation_message
  (adapter/invalidate.rs:394-410) deserializes it as DerivedScopeInvalidation via serde_json —
  a real domain type whose exact required fields I have not yet traced. This is the last
  concrete unknown before implementation could start.

Also: run_continuous (run.rs:32+) is a long-running loop (tokio::select! over shutdown/
invalidation/debounce-timer/periodic-checkpoint), not a call-and-return function like
process_batch — the fail point would need threading through recv_invalidation's signature
(currently a free fn taking &mut Option<...>, would need an added flag parameter) plus its
caller in the select! arm, a materially bigger production-code touch than the single Ok(())
arm in process.rs used for the checkpoint scenario.

Stopped here rather than rushing the remaining implementation (DerivedScopeInvalidation
field-tracing, the recv_invalidation signature change, wiring a real ephemeral consumer +
publish + fail-point-armed run_continuous invocation) within an already very long session on
a host under sustained heavy concurrent-agent load. This narrows the remaining unknown to
exactly one thing (DerivedScopeInvalidation's required shape) for whoever picks this up next.
sinex-r6d.9's invalidation-ack scenario (this bead's third named window) is now empirically confirmed self-healing on restart, not permanent loss — see sinex-r6d.7's latest note for the test and result.

title: Stream retention jams pipeline: bounded confirmed-events + WorkQueue decision for raw
description:
Sustained ingestion wedges the pipeline: Limits+discard:New streams fill (2M msgs), rejecting publishes -> the durability gate (raw-ack gated on confirmed publish) turns rejection into an unbounded redelivery storm. Interim fix already applied: confirmed-events -> discard: Old (safe: Postgres is the archive).

Remaining structural work:
1. confirmed-events -> RetentionPolicy::Interest (drain once every durable automaton consumer acks; verify ephemeral SSE consumers do NOT pin retention).
2. raw events stream: if event-engine is the sole consumer (automata on confirmed-events, replay via source scan, DLQ separate), RetentionPolicy::WorkQueue keeps it to in-flight size — verify consumer topology first.
3. self-observation firehose volume for dev loop (lower-rate/sampled profile) — overlaps telemetry-lane issue.
4. Live reconfiguration: create_or_update_stream may not change discard/retention on existing streams — prod/dev streams may need explicit recreation.

Evidence: 2026-06-29 dogfood stall at exactly 2M confirmed-events, 12,661 max-messages errors.
design:
DESIGN (settled 2026-07-03 — this SUPERSEDES the gh#2204 body's "Interest is the correct shape" claim):

confirmed-events steady state = LIMITS-BASED + discard:Old (already applied as interim), kept deliberately — paired with the mandatory DB catch-up contract (sinex-vxu: DeliverPolicy::New + startup catch-up from Postgres is how any consumer reads pre-subscription history; the stream is a bounded delivery bus, Postgres is the archive).

WHY NOT Interest (the reconciliation): Interest retention deletes a message once all bound durable consumers ack — but (a) an ORPHANED durable consumer (the consumer-name-embeds-filter-list problem: changing an automaton's input set strands the old durable) never acks, so Interest re-creates exactly the fill-and-jam this issue exists to kill, just via a different mechanism; (b) new-automaton history reads would silently depend on retention accidents instead of the DB catch-up contract. Interest becomes eligible ONLY after consumer-GC exists (runtime startup deletes durables matching the service prefix but not current names — tracked in the perf packet) AND a test proves ephemeral SSE consumers don't pin retention. Until both hold, Limits+discard:Old is strictly safer and loses nothing (tail loss recovers from Postgres).

raw events stream: WorkQueue iff verified single-consumer — check consumer topology in dev AND prod (only the event-engine durable may exist; automata are on confirmed-events, replay re-ingests via source scan, DLQ is separate). Add a startup assertion that fails loud if another durable appears on the raw stream. Live reconfiguration caveat stands: create_or_update_stream cannot change discard/retention in place — plan explicit stream recreation/migration for existing dev+prod streams.

self-observation volume prong: superseded by the telemetry-lane design (sinex-x9i) — reflection gets its own stream; do not implement sampling here.
acceptance_criteria: Sustained-load dev run (2026-06-29 repro shape) never jams: confirmed-events bounded by limits+discard:Old with consumers recovering tail from DB catch-up (test: force a consumer >max_messages behind, verify it heals); raw stream WorkQueue only with the startup single-consumer assertion in place and passing; stream recreation/migration executed explicitly for existing dev+prod streams; a written note in the PR states the Interest preconditions (consumer-GC + ephemeral non-pinning proof) for future revisit.
notes:
2026-07-03 raw-consumer topology slice: live dev NATS before restart showed DEV_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS with stale pre-consolidation automaton durables causing ~1.15M aggregate pending while event-engine-dev itself was caught up. Branch feature/runtime/raw-consumer-topology adds startup reconciliation: keep only event-engine durable on raw, delete legacy sinex-*-automaton-confirmed-v2 durables from raw, reject any other unexpected raw durable. Live proof after restarting dev sinexd job 2001424: raw stream consumer_count=1, consumer=event-engine-dev, pending=0, ack_pending=1, redelivered=0. Remaining sinex-9u4 scope: WorkQueue/recreation decision, confirmation watermark stream cleanup via sinex-egs, DLQ triage.

2026-07-04 automaton catch-up prerequisite strengthened on feature/runtime/automaton-lossless-catchup: bridge-managed automata now load the saved bridge checkpoint before DB historical catch-up, supporting the bounded confirmed-events stream model in nats-transport-decision-record.md. This does not close 9u4: raw WorkQueue migration, explicit dev/prod stream recreation, and sustained-load jam proof remain.

2026-07-04 confirmed-consumer GC slice on feature/runtime/confirmed-consumer-gc: startup now deletes stale confirmed-event durable consumers for the same automaton service root, including old filter-sibling names. Live proof after patched all-automatons job 2001689: confirmed stream DEV_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS_CONFIRMED consumer_count dropped 19 -> 16; stale interval-lift durables with pending 5787, 6022, and 961 disappeared; current interval-lift durable pending=0/ack_pending=0. Verification jobs: 2001686 focused confirmed_consumer_retirement/confirmed_filter_subject tests passed; 2001687 xtask check -p sinexd passed. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/confirmed-consumer-gc-live-20260704T1412Z. This satisfies the consumer-GC prerequisite for revisiting Interest retention, but does not close 9u4: raw WorkQueue migration/recreation decision and sustained-load jam proof remain.

2026-07-04 raw WorkQueue migration slice on feature/runtime/raw-workqueue-recreation: disposable NATS experiment proved Limits->WorkQueue cannot be updated in place (error 10052), so runtime now recreates only a drained raw stream. Guard requires one expected event-engine consumer, no unexpected consumers, pending=0, ack_pending=0, redelivered=0, and ack_floor >= stream tail. Live dev proof: DEV_SINEX_RAW_EVENTS migrated from Limits with 195157 retained messages / 2147483533 bytes to WorkQueue discard:Old with 0 messages / 0 bytes and one event-engine-dev consumer. Runtime jobs 2001692 core and 2001693 automata are running. Verification jobs: 2001690 raw_workqueue_recreation tests passed; 2001691 xtask check -p sinexd passed. Artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/raw-workqueue-migration-live-20260704T1426Z. Remaining 9u4: sustained-load no-jam proof and production deployment/recreation proof if production NATS is re-enabled.
2026-07-05 stream-retention behavior proof slice: added focused sinexd NATS tests in crate/sinexd/src/runtime/jetstream_streams_test.rs proving confirmed-events Limits+discard:Old stays bounded and accepts publishes beyond max_messages, and raw WorkQueue removes acked messages.
