title: Schema backfill runner: persist failure state, expose interrupted-vs-active, guard replay/ingest concurrency
description: schema_backfill_runs has failed/error_message columns but the runner never persists them — errors unwind leaving status='running' with a stale phase/cursor, indistinguishable from an active run (backfill.rs:164-176). Correctness also depends on writer quiescence: replay/ingest can archive rows inside the frozen horizon or add rows after it, producing stale parsed_event_count — the --assume-quiescent flag is load-bearing, not ceremony. Source: new-gpt-pro 08 backfill section.
acceptance_criteria: Runner catches errors and persists status=failed + error_message while still releasing the advisory lock. Status output distinguishes active-running / interrupted-running / failed / succeeded / registered. Rerun semantics explicit: resume interrupted by default, reset only on --restart or failed policy. Backfill refuses to run while replay/ingest writers are active or takes a shared writer-quiescence lock. Tests: crash/resume during scan, failure during apply, rerun after failed, concurrent-replay guard.

title: Schema registry integrity: content_hash can go stale against schema_content
description: Schema sync compares stored content_hash metadata (schema_management.rs:840-895) while runtime validation compiles schema_content from DB (validation.rs:600-685). A manual schema_content edit with stale content_hash false-cleans sync and silently changes validation semantics. Detection today is event rejection behavior, not schema diff. Source: new-gpt-pro 08 finding 8.
acceptance_criteria: Schema sync or strict diff recomputes content_hash from live schema_content for sinex_schemas.event_payload_schemas; mismatch is reported as drift or repaired from the generated bundle. Test covers manual schema_content mutation with unchanged content_hash.

title: Strict trigger/function drift: enabled state, trigger definition, exact function bodies
description: VERIFIED on master: ensure_trigger_set_sql skips SQL when the trigger NAME exists (apply.rs:1388-1402); trigger_exists checks only tgrelid/tgname/NOT tgisinternal (apply.rs:1367-1386) — no tgenabled, timing, event mask, WHEN, or target function. strict_diff.rs module comments (lines 18-21, 344) claim trigger functions are installed by CREATE OR REPLACE every apply — FALSE for the guarded set. Blast: ALTER TABLE ... DISABLE TRIGGER or a same-name retargeted trigger silently bypasses payload validation / append-only / archive-before-delete enforcement until data corruption is noticed. Function marker-checks are substring-based and pass body edits preserving markers. Source: new-gpt-pro 08 finding 1 + main correction.
acceptance_criteria: Strict diff checks tgenabled, timing, event mask, row/statement mode, WHEN clause, and target function for all schema-managed triggers; exact normalized pg_get_functiondef (or source-declared hash) for load-bearing functions, bound to full signatures. The stale CREATE-OR-REPLACE comments in strict_diff.rs are corrected. Tests prove DISABLE TRIGGER and same-name retarget drift are detected.
notes: 2026-07-08 architecture guidance: implement as TriggerExpectation/FunctionExpectation kinds in the schema expectation IR (32p2) interpreted by both apply and strict-diff — the lying comments become structurally impossible when both read the same expectation.

title: Live-schema strict comparator: column type/generated/default/collation, named CHECK bodies, FK actions, index definitions
description: Same-name false-clean drift class, verified: convergence introspects only column names + nullability (converge.rs:246-285,1310-1397); index checks key on name not pg_get_indexdef (apply.rs:901-966,1451-1465); named CHECK expression drift is explicitly not converged (converge.rs:509-516); strict_diff pins only 3 defaults, 5 inline checks, 2 FK actions. Highest blast: ts_coided generated-expression drift (the interpretation clock, defs/events.rs:200-205) and a weakened events_material_anchor_required (occurrence identity) pass every current check. strict_diff DbCheck is also weaker than apply (checks expected literals present but not extra literals — enum widening false-cleans strict-only). Source: new-gpt-pro 08 findings 2-4 + false-clean audit.
acceptance_criteria: Strict diff compares live pg_attribute type/generated expression/default/collation for every convergible-table column with a source-declared contract; pg_get_constraintdef for named CHECKs (normalized); all declared FK definitions/actions; pg_get_indexdef including partial predicates/expressions/opclasses. DbCheck strict check rejects extra literals like apply does. False-clean tests: same-name wrong index, same-name weakened CHECK, changed FK action, ts_coided generated-expression drift.
notes: 2026-07-08 architecture guidance: implement as the verify-interpreter over the schema expectation IR (32p2) — add ColumnExpectation/ConstraintExpectation/IndexExpectation kinds and interpret, do NOT add another hand-enumerated check list to strict_diff.rs (that pattern is the origin of the drift this bead fixes).

title: xtask impact-mode evidence soundness: completeness tokens, historical-test liveness, risk-class fallbacks
description: Three balanced-mode false-skip classes (new-gpt-pro 03 P1 x3): (1) hunk 'fully covered' means at least ONE historical evidence row overlaps each hunk (impact.rs:495-517) — not that ALL tests needing the hunk are known; (2) filters emit historical test names with no nextest-list liveness check — renamed/deleted tests become zero-test proofs recorded as success (impact.rs:291-324; runner only fails on stats.failed>0); (3) the syntactic item scanner under-models cross-crate macros, build.rs/env-cfg, generated code, and SQLx/live-DB coupling (impact.rs:647-748; workspace triggers only Cargo/flake/config at 257-264). Owner context: sinex-pke.
acceptance_criteria: Test-name filtering requires a per-package coverage-completeness token; without it fall back to affected-package or workspace scope. Every history-derived filter is liveness-checked against current nextest discovery; a filter selecting zero current tests invalidates evidence and falls back, never records success. Risk-class fallbacks: proc-macro/macro crates, build.rs, schema/sqlx-bearing files, generated-code roots, and env-cfg surfaces escalate to package-closure or workspace scope unless the proof includes reverse-dependency evidence plus a DB/schema seal.

title: xtask proof substrate seal: fingerprints omit live DB/NATS/env/toolchain and proof reuse skips preflight
description: Proof fingerprints cover HEAD, scoped dirty content, seven shared config files, and semantic CLI args (coordinator.rs:35-43,883-904,1042-1105; test.rs:849-947) — but NOT live DB schema state, NATS topology, DATABASE_URL, RUSTFLAGS/RUSTC_WRAPPER, or rustc/cargo/nextest versions, even though sqlx compiles against the live DB by doctrine. Worse: exact-impact and package proofs are consumed BEFORE compile/runtime preflight (test.rs:1302-1344,1671-1724 vs preflight at 1812-1835), so a cached proof can report success while the substrate needed for a real run is broken. Fix shape: a substrate seal (env whitelist, rustc -Vv, tool versions, schema digest/strict-diff hash, NATS topology digest, preflight outcome id) folded into the proof key, or a cheap seal validation before proof consumption. Source: new-gpt-pro 03 P1 x2. Related: sinex-6cb, sinex-9wx (whose original pre-queue DDL race IS fixed on master — wrapper flock at flake.nix:1884-1897, launcher-only --bg skips bootstrap, schema apply holds exclusive lock at preflight.rs:977-1011).
acceptance_criteria: check/build/test proofs embed a substrate seal: normalized env whitelist, rustc/cargo/nextest versions, devshell/cache identity, live schema digest, NATS topology digest for runtime tests, preflight outcome id. Proof reuse validates the seal (or runs a cheap preflight) before returning success. Detection commands from the audit reproduce the false-pass before, and fail closed after: proof hit + xtask infra stop + rerun same test without --no-reuse must not report success.

title: Material-assembler DLQ publish failure is swallowed before ACK/terminal-failed settlement
description: route_material_error returns () and only logs DLQ publish failures (finalize.rs:138-202); the pipeline then marks the material failed and ACKs the frame (pipeline.rs:95-118) — no redelivery, no DLQ record. Raw-event DLQ does the stricter thing (ACK only after DLQ publish succeeds, dlq.rs:213-238); material should match. Source: new-gpt-pro 02 EE-08.
acceptance_criteria: route_material_error returns a Result. Frame-level DLQ decisions ACK only after DLQ publish confirmation or an equivalent durable failure record. DLQ publish failure causes NAK/retry, never silent terminal settlement. Tests simulate NATS publish failure and assert no ACK/terminal mark without durable failure evidence.

title: DLQ retry republishes redacted/stubbed preview as if it were original raw payload
description: DLQ entries store policy-redacted original_payload; parse failures suppress raw bytes (dlq.rs:81-100); oversize entries store a metadata stub (dlq.rs:134-159). dlq_retry serializes that field and republishes it to the original raw subject unless _raw_bytes_base64 exists (dlq_retry.rs:693-728, 436-475), then deletes the DLQ message. Replay can therefore inject mutated/stub events that never existed on the raw stream, or re-emit whole multi-event envelopes. Source: new-gpt-pro 02 EE-07.
acceptance_criteria: DLQ entries carry an explicit replayability flag; suppressed/redacted/stubbed entries are not retried by default. Replayable raw bytes stored separately from operator preview with the durability/access model documented. Retry tests cover redacted payload, oversize stub, parse-failure suppression, and normal replayable payload.

title: Derived batch chunk validation falsely rejects forward parent references across the 2700-row boundary
description: Full-batch cycle check passes (persistence.rs:890-903) but per-chunk liveness validation exempts only the current chunk's ids (persistence.rs:905-939): a child in chunk 0 referencing a parent in chunk 1 of the same acyclic batch queries core.events, finds nothing, and is isolated as a poison row -> DLQ while siblings commit. Triggers on burst replay/import of large derived DAGs (>2700 rows). Source: new-gpt-pro 02 EE-06.
acceptance_criteria: Parent-liveness validation sees the full batch ID set, or chunks are topologically ordered so parents commit first. Valid acyclic full-batch DAGs never fail on chunk boundaries. Tests cover child-before-parent across the boundary and a genuinely missing parent. Bisection/DLQ still isolates real invalid parents.

title: payload_schema_id FK strip-retry silently diverges DB row from confirmed stream
description: On payload_schema_id FK violation, persistence retries with every row's schema id set to NULL (admission.rs:970-1000, matcher 1316-1327) and commits; confirmation still publishes the original event advertising the validated schema id. Schema-cache staleness becomes silent authority drift: DB says no-schema, confirmed stream says schema S. Adjacent to sinex-5ai but not covered by it. Source: new-gpt-pro 02 EE-05.
acceptance_criteria: FK violation triggers schema reload + revalidation or terminal schema-management failure — never silent schema-id stripping. If a retry lands, DB row and confirmed event agree on payload_schema_id. Metric/audit signal identifies schema-cache races. Tests cover stale schema id, missing schema row, successful reload/revalidate.

title: Reflection-lane admission is lane-blind: dedupe, tombstones, and parent liveness query core.* only
description: Reflection consumer writes reflection.events but equivalence-key dedupe SQL queries only core.events (queries.rs:671-705), tombstone filtering only core.event_tombstones (queries.rs:645-667), and ensure_source_event_ids_are_live only core.events (persistence/validation.rs:85-150). Consequences: replayed deterministic reflection events duplicate in reflection.events; reflection-derived events with reflection parents are falsely rejected as non-live; cross-lane parent semantics are accidental. Related to sinex-x9i (lane separation) but x9i does not cover this admission/persistence parity hole. Source: new-gpt-pro 02 EE-04.
acceptance_criteria: Repository APIs for equivalence keys, tombstones, and parent liveness accept EventStorageLane. Reflection events dedupe against reflection.events; reflection-derived events can reference same-lane parents; cross-lane parent policy is explicit, tested, and documented. Activity-lane behavior unchanged.

title: Re-run ts_orig plausibility after material timestamp resolution
description: Material-provenance events may pass admission with ts_orig=None (allowed, admission.rs:529-538); plausibility lower-bound/future-skew checks only run inside 'if let Some(ts_orig)' (admission.rs:546-589). resolve_ready_ts_orig later stamps ts_orig from the material registry/temporal ledger AFTER readiness gating (persist.rs:80-87, prepare.rs:159-176) and row conversion only checks non-null. A registry start_time of 1900 or 2100 persists without ever passing the gates non-material events face. Source: new-gpt-pro 02 EE-03.
acceptance_criteria: Resolved material ts_orig values are checked against ts_orig_lower_bound and future_ts_skew before persistence; violations route through validation/DLQ settlement (subject to the aggregate-settlement fix). Tests cover too-old, too-future, and valid resolved material timestamps. Admission-time checks for non-material events unchanged.

title: Entity/relation insertion defaults fabricate authority: confidence 1.0, empty source refs, global canonical_name
description: Verified on master (2026-07-07): entity and relation creation default confidence_score to 1.0 when unset (sinex-db/src/repositories/knowledge_graph.rs:335 entity, :656 relation) and accept empty source_event_ids (unwrap_or_default) — an unreviewed guess becomes maximal authority with zero provenance. Additionally core.entities.canonical_name is globally UNIQUE across ALL entity kinds (sinex-schema/src/defs/entities.rs:100-104), so 'Claude' the person/model/product collide into one identity slot (the partial uk_entities_type_name (type,name) index shows the kind-scoped intent, but canonical_name ignores kind). Adversarial audit section 10 failure mode: relations attach to the wrong node; context packs later cite the relation as fact.
design: Repair direction (audit section 10 + storage/authority doctrine): (a) default confidence for unreviewed candidates = NULL/unknown or explicit low — never 1.0; (b) require non-empty support refs OR an accepted judgment before a relation is surfaced as canonical; (c) entity identity = (entity_kind, namespace, external_id_or_hash); canonical_name becomes display, not identity — drop or kind-scope the global unique; (d) resolution creates entity_identity_candidate rows (basis: exact_email|git_author_email|same_provider_id|lexical|cooccurrence|model_guess), never direct merges. Coordinates with pq5 (consume-or-retire decision) — if pq5 decides retire, this shrinks to just removing the lying defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Audit of all entity/relation insertion paths shows no 1.0-confidence default and no empty-support canonical surfacing; a test proves an unreviewed candidate relation cannot appear as canonical; canonical_name uniqueness is kind-scoped or demoted to display. VERIFY: xtask test -p sinex-db -E 'test(entity)' + grep showing unwrap_or(1.0) gone from knowledge_graph.rs.
notes: Repro: create a relation via pool.knowledge_graph().create_relation_with_executor with confidence_score=None and source_event_ids=None — row lands with confidence 1.0 and empty refs (knowledge_graph.rs:656). Entity same at :335. Cross-kind collision: create entity kind=person name-canonical 'Claude', then kind=model canonical 'Claude' — second insert violates the global unique.

title: Analysis: sinex-corpus extraction — recovered-requirements ledger from 300 design chats
description:
Operator directive (2026-07-06, also rawlog 06-18 22:23 'mass-grabbing every sinex-related chatlog ever... stop feeling like I'm missing something'): systematically mine the ~300 exocortex/sinex sessions in the Polylogue archive (aistudio-drive 98, chatgpt-export 157, claude-ai-export 45) for design intent that never reached the vision docs. First slice done 2026-07-06 (scratch 020 Round-4/5: origin arc, AT-SPI2 provenance, two founding-intent quotes, RecordKeeper era). Remaining: the Vault series (Feb-Apr 2025, 400+msg threads), Sinex_Totality + Sinnix_Exoco (June 2025), and the chatgpt-export set.

## Method (proven this session — route around polylogue-rsad frictions)
(1) facets contains=<term> for origin counts; (2) list_sessions origin+contains, jq '.items|unique_by(.id)' from persisted output; (3) get_messages message_role=user (operator words = intent), extract via jq from persisted files — NEVER read raw MCP output inline; (4) for merged-report sessions, prefer the distilled artifact in sinex-target-vision over 100K-token chat outputs; (5) per session: extract {feature/kind/UX idea, quote, verdict: already-covered | gap->bead | superseded}.

## Acceptance Criteria
A recovered-requirements ledger (scratch/research) covering the Vault series + top-10 chatgpt design sessions: every extracted item has a quote + session ref + verdict; gap verdicts become beads; the ledger cross-referenced from rl4/pi1. Falsifier check per U11 risk 1: note anything contradicting current doctrine rather than silently dropping it.
acceptance_criteria: A recovered-requirements ledger (scratch/research) covering the Vault series + top-10 chatgpt design sessions: every extracted item has a quote + session ref + verdict; gap verdicts become beads; the ledger cross-referenced from rl4/pi1. Falsifier check per U11 risk 1: note anything contradicting current doctrine rather than silently dropping it.

title: Historical archive backfill: the full Polylogue corpus through the staged-import plane
description:
4j2.1 defines the vocabulary; this bead lands the CORPUS: the full historical Polylogue archive (years of sessions incl. 100K+-message ones) ingested as materials + anchored events through the normal staged-import machinery — paced (2n9), resumable (checkpoint cursors), identity-stable across re-export (occurrence keys from session_id + line identity, NOT import order). This is the largest single historical import Sinex will have done; it is also the proving load for import pacing and mega-material lessons (#2182: one material per session export segment, not per archive).

## Acceptance Criteria
Full archive imported on dev with: zero mega-chunk regressions (chunk sizes within policy), pacing keeping the host usable (PSI bounds respected), a resumable interrupted-import proof (kill mid-import, resume, no dupes — occurrence identity holds), per-session material granularity recorded, and counts reconciling against Polylogue's own totals (coverage-error style: Sinex states what it holds vs what Polylogue exports). VERIFY: import run artifacts + reconciliation report.
design: Route: staged export parser per crate/sinexd/docs/sources/adding_staged_export_parser.md + the polylogue source contract (source_contracts/polylogue.rs). Material granularity DECISION: per-session-export file (bounded), never one giant archive material (the #2182 lesson) — segment long sessions if a single export exceeds material policy. ts_orig = provider timestamps (IntrinsicContent) per the settled doctrine. Sequence: vocabulary (4j2.1) first, then backfill; moment search (4j2.2) needs the corpus to be worth demoing.
acceptance_criteria: Full archive imported on dev with: zero mega-chunk regressions (chunk sizes within policy), pacing keeping the host usable (PSI bounds respected), a resumable interrupted-import proof (kill mid-import, resume, no dupes — occurrence identity holds), per-session material granularity recorded, and counts reconciling against Polylogue's own totals (coverage-error style: Sinex states what it holds vs what Polylogue exports). VERIFY: import run artifacts + reconciliation report.

title: Reverse direction: Polylogue reads Sinex — ambient machine context in session views
description:
The 'optional backend' semantics cut BOTH ways (operator 2026-07-06): Sinex isn't just where Polylogue evidence lands — it's what makes Polylogue's product richer when present. When configured with a Sinex endpoint, Polylogue session views gain ambient machine context: what was on screen, which commands ran, which files changed DURING the session interval ('what was happening around this conversation'). When Sinex is absent, Polylogue degrades silently to standalone (its independence is non-negotiable).

Sinex side (this bead): a session-interval context query shaped for Polylogue's consumption — given (host, time interval, optional cwd/repo hints), return family-grouped activity cards with evidence refs and honest gaps. This is the recall/context substrate re-exposed with an interval parameter, NOT a new engine.

## Acceptance Criteria
A gateway/MCP query: interval + host (+ hints) -> family-grouped context with ViewEnvelope caveats; latency inside the interactive budget on a week-old interval (1wm SLO applies); Polylogue-side consumption tracked in the polylogue repo (cross-repo bead, filed at execution start); demo: a real polylogue session rendered with its machine context alongside. VERIFY: query on live dev store for a known session interval.
design: Reuses: events time-window listing (ordered by occurrence, #2271), family-aware context pack (dogfood recipe memory), interval-lift outputs for focus/workspace spans. The interval-parameterized shape is ALSO what moment-search windows need (4j2.2 step 3) — build once. Auth: Polylogue uses a readonly token; no new auth surface. Cross-repo coordination: Polylogue owns rendering + config + degradation; Sinex owns the query contract stability (zi6 producer-protocol discipline applies to reader contracts too).
acceptance_criteria: A gateway/MCP query: interval + host (+ hints) -> family-grouped context with ViewEnvelope caveats; latency inside the interactive budget on a week-old interval (1wm SLO applies); Polylogue-side consumption tracked in the polylogue repo (cross-repo bead, filed at execution start); demo: a real polylogue session rendered with its machine context alongside. VERIFY: query on live dev store for a known session interval.

title: Analysis: source registry chain — count the hops from contract to running binding
description:
Streamlining audit 2026-07-06: a source travels register_source_contract! -> register_source_runtime_binding! -> catalog_export.rs -> nixos/modules/source-catalog.generated.json (6,877 lines, checked in) -> lib/source-catalog.nix -> sources.nix glue functions -> generated bindings manifest JSON -> SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH -> sinexd bindings.rs. That is ~8 hops with 2 generated artifacts and 3 hand-maintained mapping layers. Each hop exists for a reason (Rust->Nix boundary, deployment config, dev-vs-prod manifests) — the audit asks which hops still pay rent post-#1727 typed contracts: can the generated JSON drive the Nix side directly without per-family glue functions (mkTerminalGlue/mkDesktopGlue/... each reimplement binding->unit mapping); can dev (.agent/dev/dev-source-bindings.json) and prod manifests share one generator?

## Acceptance Criteria
A hop map with per-hop justification or collapse plan; at least one concrete simplification implemented (e.g. one generic glue replacing N family functions, or dev/prod manifest unification) OR a recorded operator decision that the current chain is the intended shape. VERIFY: xtask docs check + a Sinnix eval proving the collapsed path produces identical units.
design: The staticImportGlue (sources.nix:1791) already proves generic glue is viable — mkStaticImportGlue handles arbitrary static sources with zero per-source Nix. The question is whether terminal/desktop/browser/system families can converge on the same pattern with a per-family overlay for genuinely special bits (devices, sockets, timers). Relates: stm (deploy dark sources — benefits directly), 42d (xtask binding-name resolution reads the same manifest).
acceptance_criteria: A hop map with per-hop justification or collapse plan; at least one concrete simplification implemented OR a recorded operator decision that the chain is the intended shape. ADDITIONS (2026-07-07): the hop map distinguishes the Rust-generated source-catalog chain, the prod source-bindings manifest chain, and the separate services.sinex.sources.bindings/exportedJson surface. Nix serializes SourceBinding.extra_env into the SINEX_SOURCE_BINDINGS_PATH manifest, with a regression proving staticImports.git-commit-history carries GIT_CONFIG_COUNT/KEY_0/VALUE_0 through Nix -> manifest -> SourceBinding.extra_env -> EnvGuard. services.sinex.sources.bindings/exportedJson is removed/deprecated or wired into the actual hosted manifest (eval test: enabling a binding there changes what sinexd consumes). runtimeOverlay.bindReadOnlyPaths is applied to the sinexd systemd unit or removed as a nonfunctional field (terminal/browser/desktop no-ACL fallback covered by eval or VM smoke test). The inert services.sinex.core.event_engine.batch options either emit SINEX_EVENT_ENGINE_CONSUMER_FETCH_MAX_MESSAGES/_TIMEOUT_MS or are removed. VERIFY: xtask docs check + a Sinnix eval proving the collapsed path produces identical units.
notes:
2026-07-07 drift audit: three silent-noop surfaces verified — exportedJson is a parallel package sinexd never reads (source-bindings.nix:119-185); mkStaticImportGlue builds extraEnv then activeManifestBindings drops it (sources.nix:1791-1817,1901-1928) while Rust fully supports extra_env (bindings.rs:57-99,232-266); bindReadOnlyPaths is collected (sources.nix:1886-1894) but never applied (sources.nix:650-662). Batch options inert: default.nix:743-747 vs sources.nix:568-630 env block vs config.rs:842-873.
2026-07-07: promoted wave:9->wave:2 and P3->P2 — the audit converted this from hygiene analysis into carrier of three verified prod-visible fixes (extra_env dropped from manifest, exportedJson silent-noop, bindReadOnlyPaths ignored). 2bk's git proof depends on the extra_env fix.

title: Lie-audit standing suite: the doctrine falsifiers as CI tests
description:
Blueprint lens L-1 ('how does this lie to me?') — the operationalization of the truth-substrate thesis. Every doctrine names its falsifier (rl4); this bead turns the falsifiers into a standing test suite that catches the system lying about itself. Catalog of lie-vectors to cover: silent zeros (empty read as nothing-happened), stale CAs/projections read as current, dark derivations presenting as knowledge, confidence-without-provenance, coverage gaps rendered as absence, ts_coided read as ts_orig, replayed interpretation read as original, negative-space regressions (content-hashed ids, id-idempotency, auto-pruning creeping back — doctrine 9 / lens L-7).

## Acceptance Criteria
A named test family (xtask-runnable, e.g. -E 'test(lie_audit)') with at least one test per doctrine falsifier; each test FAILS if the falsifier's observation is produced; negative-space greps (ON CONFLICT(id) occurrence-dedup, UUIDv7-from-ts_orig) enforced in forbidden-pattern lint; suite wired into the broad local gate. VERIFY: xtask test run of the family + one deliberately-broken fixture proving a test catches its lie.
design: Depends on rl4 (the doctrine page defines the falsifiers this suite implements — do rl4 first). Split enforcement by level (type-enforcement hierarchy): greppable lies -> xtask check --forbidden patterns; behavioral lies -> sinex_test scenarios; surface lies (bare counts) -> the 42n envelope tests already partially cover. Do not duplicate 42n coverage — extend it.
acceptance_criteria: A named test family (xtask-runnable, e.g. -E 'test(lie_audit)') with at least one test per doctrine falsifier; each test FAILS if the falsifier's observation is produced; negative-space greps (ON CONFLICT(id) occurrence-dedup, UUIDv7-from-ts_orig) enforced in forbidden-pattern lint; suite wired into the broad local gate. VERIFY: xtask test run of the family + one deliberately-broken fixture proving a test catches its lie.

title: Agent write posture: capable-but-audited (attributability tuple + effect ledger)
description:
Blueprint 018 §2 agent spine + doctrine 8: agents get the FULL MCP mutation surface (annotate, tag, propose, adjudicate, record) — audited, not restricted. Every mutating op carries a stable who-did-this tuple (actor, session, tool, intent) plus operation_id, so the effect ledger is queryable ('show me everything agent session X changed'). Attributability over prohibition.

Current state (2026-07-06): MCP server is read-only by design (crate/sinexctl/docs/mcp_readonly_server.md); mutation exists only via CLI/RPC with token-suffix RBAC; operation_id is honesty-convention (Level 6). The write surface + audit trail is greenfield.

## Acceptance Criteria
MCP exposes the mutation tool set gated per-session (write-scope config); every mutation records the attributability tuple; a query surface answers 'what did session X change' from the ledger; CLAUDE/docs update the agent-posture doctrine; no capability the operator has via CLI is withheld from agents without a recorded attributability reason. VERIFY: MCP write call round-trip on dev runtime + ledger query showing the tuple.
design: DECISION (blueprint, operator-confirmed frame): capable-but-audited, never restricted-by-default. Attributability tuple rides the existing operation_id plumbing (events carry created_by_operation_id; core.operations_log exists) — extend the operations row with actor/session/tool/intent rather than inventing a parallel ledger. MCP write tools should call the SAME RPC handlers as sinexctl (no MCP-only mutation paths). Session identity comes from the MCP transport context. Interacts: 7z0.4 judgment seat (adjudication is a write), k4c.1 recorded-analysis lane (effect ledger generalization), fcb session-bead cross-links.
acceptance_criteria: MCP exposes the mutation tool set gated per-session (write-scope config); every mutation records the attributability tuple; a query surface answers 'what did session X change' from the ledger; CLAUDE/docs update the agent-posture doctrine; no capability the operator has via CLI is withheld from agents without a recorded attributability reason. VERIFY: MCP write call round-trip on dev runtime + ledger query showing the tuple.

title: Docs drift sync: automata census, reflection lane, retired confirmation-buffer references
description:
Verified doc-vs-code drift (grok 2026-07-06, .agent/scratch/020-grok-2026-07-06-delta.md):
1. .agent/includes/architecture/data-flow.md lists 6 'current automata'; automata/registry.rs has 16 (attention-stream, interval-lift, entity-extractor/resolver/enricher, relation-extractor, tag-applier, embedding-producer, document-parser, instruction-reconciler missing). Same file still says entity/relation activation is 'tracked by #1087/#1346' — they are default-registered.
2. .agent/includes/architecture/schema.md does not mention reflection.events / the reflection lane at all (landed #2361-#2365: separate hypertable, SourceRole classification, per-lane consumers, CA migration).
3. .agent/includes/architecture/event-lifecycle.md key-thresholds row 'Confirmation buffer | 10K events' refers to machinery deleted by #2289.
4. Glossary/docs say ts_persisted is set by a DB trigger; it is a column DEFAULT (015 audit correction, never propagated).
5. provenance.md 'enforced at three levels' undersells: serde wire-format XOR rejection (builder.rs ProvenanceWire deserialize) is a fourth level.

## Acceptance Criteria
Each drift item fixed in the owning include; automata list either enumerates all 16 or points at registry.rs as source of truth (prefer pointer over census to avoid re-drift); xtask docs sync + xtask docs check pass; AGENTS.md regenerated.
design: Prefer replacing hand-maintained censuses with pointers to code (registry.rs, TELEMETRY_* constants in apply.rs) per the same move #952 docs made. One PR, docs-only, SINEX_SKIP_DRIFT_GUARD not needed (docs paths do not trip changed-strict).
close_reason: All five drift items fixed in PR #2394 (merged): data-flow.md automata census replaced with registry pointer + families; schema.md reflection.events row added (values verified against apply.rs:1195-1216); event-lifecycle.md confirmation-buffer row replaced with automaton ack-pending bound; provenance.md 4 enforcement levels + ts_persisted column DEFAULT; glossary ts_persisted corrected. command-reference.md regenerated (picked up #2388 flag). VERIFY: xtask docs check (0 stale paths); git show $(git log --grep 'sync architecture includes' --format=%H -1) --stat

title: xtask run module should resolve source-binding names
description:
During ActivityWatch replay proof for sinex-60r, `xtask run module desktop.activitywatch --bg` failed with Unknown binary 'desktop.activitywatch' even though `desktop.activitywatch` is a registered source binding. The working command was `xtask run all-sources --service-name source-driver-desktop.activitywatch-1 --bg`. Improve xtask so `run module <source-binding-id>` either resolves source bindings directly or prints the exact all-sources/service-name command for that binding. This is devloop velocity/reliability work: agents should not rediscover binding runner semantics during live source proof.

## Steps to Reproduce
xtask run module desktop.activitywatch --bg -> Unknown binary 'desktop.activitywatch', while xtask run all-sources --service-name source-driver-desktop.activitywatch-1 --bg works (observed 2026-07-05 during sinex-60r replay proof).
design: TARGETS (2026-07-06): xtask/src/commands/run.rs — RuntimeModule subcommand arm :690/:874, binary resolution failure sites :1115 ('Unknown binary … xtask run list') and :1459-:1526 ('Unknown binary: {name}'). The all-sources path already loads the dev bindings manifest (DevSourceBinding; default_source_binding_service_name :80 derives 'source-driver-<id>-1' names; --service-name filter :711-:713,:1213-:1233). Fix shape: in the RuntimeModule resolution path, when the name is not a binary/automaton, consult the same bindings manifest; on a match either run it directly (preferred — reuse the all-sources single-service path) or error with the exact working command. Note #2328 already narrowed dev runtime component selection and #2325 narrowed automaton targets — follow that code, not older assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: xtask run module <source-binding-id> resolves registered source bindings from the manifest (or fails with a message printing the exact working all-sources --service-name command); repro command from the description now succeeds; a resolution unit test covers binding-id vs binary-name lookup. VERIFY: xtask run module desktop.activitywatch --dry-run.

title: xtask jobs lacks a stop/cancel surface for background runtime jobs
description:
During interval-lift systemd proof on 2026-07-04, xtask run module interval-lift --bg created job 2001574, but xtask jobs stop 2001574 is not a supported command. That forced PID-level cleanup for a normal devloop lifecycle operation. Add a first-class xtask jobs cancel/stop command that terminates the job process tree, records exit/cancel state in history, and is safe for long-running runtime jobs.

## Steps to Reproduce
xtask run module interval-lift --bg (job 2001574, 2026-07-04); then xtask jobs stop 2001574 -> unsupported command; cleanup required manual PID kill.
design: RECONCILED 2026-07-06: the capability ALREADY EXISTS — JobManager::cancel (xtask/src/jobs/mod.rs:760; SIGTERM to the process GROUP with SIGKILL escalation after CANCEL_SIGTERM_GRACE, :758+:23) wired as 'xtask jobs cancel <id>' (commands/jobs.rs:67,118,634) since Jan 2026 (#136/#140). The 2026-07-04 incident used the verb 'stop', got no such command, and concluded the surface was missing. Fix shape = discoverability: visible aliases stop/kill on the Cancel subcommand (implemented 2026-07-06, this session) + verify cancelled state lands in history. Remaining AC beyond the alias: confirm 'cancelled' status + exit code are recorded in job history for a runtime job (xtask run module ... --bg) and that no orphan children survive group-kill.
acceptance_criteria: xtask jobs stop <id> and xtask jobs kill <id> work as aliases of cancel (clap visible_aliases — shows in --help); cancelling a live xtask run module job kills the whole process tree (no orphan sinexd/cargo PIDs) and records cancelled state in jobs history. VERIFY: xtask run module <x> --bg; xtask jobs stop <id>; xtask jobs status <id>; ps for orphans.
close_reason: Reconciled + fixed: JobManager::cancel existed since #136 with full tree-kill semantics (SIGTERM process group -> SIGKILL escalation, jobs/mod.rs:758-794) and cancelled-state recording (finish_invocation_cancelled + finish_background_job Killed). The missing piece was verb discoverability — PR #2395 (merged) adds visible aliases stop/kill on the Cancel subcommand with termination semantics in the help line. VERIFY: xtask jobs stop --help resolves to Cancel help; xtask test -p xtask -E 'test(jobs)' job 2001831 exit 0; xtask check -p xtask job 2001830 exit 0.

title: xtask proof reuse accepted stale interval-lift result after edit
description:
During sinex-8y8 AFK interval-lift work on 2026-07-04, job 2001564 passed before a one-line clamp hardening edit. A subsequent plain xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(interval_lift)' returned 'Fresh result from invocation 2005414' even though the working tree had changed; I had to force job 2001565 with --no-reuse. Investigate freshness fingerprints for focused test invocations, especially unstaged source changes made shortly after a run completes, and make stale reuse impossible or loudly explain why the tree fingerprint is still valid.

## Steps to Reproduce
Run a focused test (job passes); make a one-line source edit without staging; rerun plain xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(interval_lift)' -> 'Fresh result from invocation NNN' reused despite the dirty tree (observed 2026-07-04); --no-reuse forces a real run.
design: TARGETS + LIKELY MECHANISM (2026-07-06 code read): the base fingerprint DOES cover dirty content — tree_fingerprint_in (xtask/src/coordinator.rs:887-904) refreshes the git index (rapid same-second edits are the documented motivation) and calls hash_dirty_content. The suspect is the SCOPED path: scoped_tree_fingerprint_in (:1050-:1058) narrows hashing to a package closure via scoped_fingerprint_packages_in when -p is present — if the edited file falls outside the computed closure paths (or the closure/path mapping :910+ misses it), the fingerprint stays stable and reuse false-hits. Repro used '-p sinexd --lib -E test(interval_lift)'. Investigate: does the closure include the edited file's dir? Does the freshness key include the -E filter? Instrument with 'xtask freshness explain test -- -p sinexd --lib -E test(interval_lift)' before+after a 1-line edit; the explanation shows scope inputs (explain_freshness :1116).
acceptance_criteria: Root cause identified and fixed: freshness fingerprint for focused test invocations covers unstaged working-tree edits (dirty-hunk hash or mtime-content probe); regression test proves an edit between run and re-query invalidates reuse; xtask freshness explain shows the invalidating input. Until fixed, --no-reuse workaround documented in command guide.
notes: 2026-07-07: proof-substrate-seal bead extends this — the stale-reuse class is broader than the interval-lift incident (live DB/NATS/env/tool versions all outside the fingerprint; proof consumption precedes preflight).

title: Interval-lift live bridge skips later fresh focus rows
description: Live proof for sinex-8y8 on 2026-07-04 showed rebuilt interval-lift consumed the first fresh wm.hyprland/window.focused transition after startup and emitted derived.interval-lift/state.interval, but later fresh focus rows in the same induced focus sequence were present in core.events and not used as interval parents before artifact capture. Investigate confirmed-event live delivery, ordering, checkpoint, or durable consumer behavior for narrow event-type automata so the lifter can continuously close every subject-change interval, not only startup/historical catch-up rows. Evidence artifact: .agent/demos/sinex/interval-lift-live-20260704T0700Z/summary.json.
notes: 2026-07-04 diagnosis: live bridge did NOT skip later focus rows. Controlled focus probe delivered/acked 18 fresh wm.hyprland/window.focused confirmations through the interval-lift confirmed-event consumer, but only one interval persisted. Root cause is interval_id/equivalence_key using a restored local sequence; fresh kitty interval collided with existing interval:desktop.focus:<subject>:<sequence> rows and was suppressed by event-engine equivalence_key admission. Fix in progress: key interval identity by state_kind + subject + start_parent_event_id + end_parent_event_id, matching the SourceContract occurrence_identity.
close_reason: Fixed. Interval identity now uses state_kind + subject + start/end parent event ids instead of a restored local sequence counter. Targeted interval-lift tests passed (job 2001546); sinexd package check passed (job 2001547/fresh reuse); fixed runtime job 2001548 live-probed WeeChat -> Sinex kitty -> WeeChat and persisted the previously suppressed kitty interval with parent-id equivalence key.

title: Keep xtask run automaton targets in sync with sinexd registry
description: xtask run all-automatons and xtask run list use a hard-coded AUTOMATON_TARGETS list in xtask/src/commands/run.rs. After adding attention-stream, sinexd's runtime registry can run it via SINEX_AUTOMATA_ENABLED=all, but xtask run list/all-automatons still omit it. Fix the tooling so dev runtime proof does not require an env workaround and add a regression test that registered hosted automata are visible in the xtask run surface.
close_reason: Fixed by adding attention-stream to xtask run's automaton target list and updating run/completions tests. Verification: job 2001520 passed focused xtask run target tests; job 2001521 passed xtask check -p xtask; xtask run list --json now exposes attention-stream.

title: Make pre-push xtask rebuild path observable and cheap
description: Why this exists: pushing PR #2305 rebuilt checkout-local xtask through the pre-push changed-strict hook because this branch touched xtask/src and the hook rejected stale/direnv wrapper candidates. The behavior was correct but opaque and expensive enough to look like a reliability failure. What needs to be done: make the pre-push path explain why each candidate is rejected, expose whether branch changes actually require rebuilding xtask, and prefer a warm checkout-local binary path when safe without bypassing changed-strict. Acceptance: a branch that does not touch xtask build inputs uses an existing current xtask binary without nix develop fallback; a branch that does touch xtask build inputs emits a compact reason and rebuilds once through the intended checkout-local path; tests or shell-level smoke cover both decisions.
close_reason: Completed: pre-push now reports whether a branch changed xtask build inputs, uses a warm checkout-local/PATH/cache xtask for non-xtask branches without rebuild, requires fresh binaries for xtask-changing branches with per-candidate rejection reasons, and exposes SINEX_PRE_PUSH_DRY_RUN=1 for shell-level selector smokes. Verification: bash -n .githooks/pre-push; SINEX_PRE_PUSH_DRY_RUN=1 .githooks/pre-push; BASE_REF=HEAD^ SINEX_PRE_PUSH_DRY_RUN=1 .githooks/pre-push; git diff --check.

title: Audit and prune stale agent worktrees
description: Old agent worktrees remain registered under .claude/worktrees and /realm/tmp/worktrees after the local-master reconciliation. Several branches are 94 commits behind master and contain dirty files or commits whose substance appears to have landed via later PRs (#2289-#2301). Audit each worktree by patch/content, preserve any still-valuable diff as a PR or Beads note, then remove worktrees/branches that contain no unique value. Current inventory from 2026-07-03: agent-a2c7e0005d2a7f3ae is 94 behind/0 ahead but dirty in api mod/service_container; agent-a4fb3e6b355fa0f30 replay/schema branch was audited and sinex-afd closed after proving content already landed; agent-a75e66d3841233019, agent-ab8420f7db6f66786, agent-abb78a3c53a8a5f1d, agent-ac74836b5d8274e95, and /realm/tmp/worktrees/sinex-pr2207-fix still need classification.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory all agent worktrees with branch, dirtiness, last activity, linked bead/session if any, and proof artifacts; classify each keep/prune/freeze; delete only worktrees with clean or archived evidence and no unique uncommitted changes; produce before/after disk/path list and a rollback note; leave any ambiguous tree filed as follow-up instead of deleting it.
notes: 2026-07-04 stale-worktree audit pass: current master is synced with origin (rev-list origin/master...HEAD = 0 0 at c1bd59ddf). Worktree classifications: agent-a2c7 branch is merged into master; its dirty api/mod.rs and service_container.rs removal of ConfirmationBuffer health is already represented by #2289 on master (current api/mod.rs exports only ServiceContainer and service_container.rs has no ConfirmationBufferHealth/probe_confirmation_buffer_pressure). agent-a4fb replay/schema commits were superseded by #2301/#2300; its dirty lifecycle_restore_test anchor-byte accessor is stale because current master uses provenance().anchor_byte(). agent-a75/abb/ac748/ab842 contain old confirmation/raw-filter/schema/replay/material commits whose scope landed or was superseded by #2207/#2208/#2220-#2231/#2262/#2288/#2289/#2296/#2299/#2300/#2301; ac748 dirty deletion of confirmation_handler/provisional is not applicable because current master intentionally keeps reduced trait/checkpoint-loader modules. /realm/tmp/worktrees/sinex-pr2207-fix has its committed helper patch patch-equivalent to master; dirty automaton consumer-name suffix is superseded by current master's provenance/event_type_filters consumer-name construction, and confirmed filter subject shape was corrected by #2296. Deletion candidates after explicit confirmation: worktrees agent-a2c7, agent-a4fb, agent-a75, agent-ab842, agent-abb78, agent-ac748, /realm/tmp/worktrees/sinex-pr2207-fix; corresponding local branches worktree-agent-* and pr/sinex-devloop/01-add-confirmed-event-type-filter-subject-helper. Also audit broader stale pr/sinex-devloop/* branch garden separately; many are squash-reconciled by #2262 but not safe to mass-delete without a branch ledger.

title: Make infra smoke --run-core non-disruptive when dev runtime is live
description: Why this exists: sinex-egs closure wants xtask infra smoke --run-core evidence, but the command currently fails with INFRA_SMOKE_NOT_STOPPED when the current checkout dogfood infra is already running unless the operator permits --reset-first or a stop. During an active devloop that makes the closure proof disruptive. What needs to be done: add an explicit non-disruptive smoke mode or teach --run-core to use an isolated temporary namespace/job when checkout infra is already live, preserving the current runtime while still proving managed core startup/observe/cancel behavior. Acceptance: xtask infra smoke --run-core can produce closure-grade evidence without stopping the existing dogfood runtime; the old reset-first behavior remains available for destructive lifecycle tests.
close_reason: Completed: added xtask infra smoke --allow-running so run-core proof can observe an already-running dogfood stack without stopping Postgres, NATS, or sinexd. Live verification 2026-07-03T22:44:47Z: xtask infra smoke --allow-running --run-core passed; baseline and final state both running; observed existing sinexd pid 2230434.

title: Avoid cold schema bootstrap rebuilds for narrow proofs
description: Observed while working sinex-9u4 on 2026-07-03: focused xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(raw_stream_reconciliation)' was earlyoom-killed twice at default test-profile codegen-units=256 (rustc RSS 8.2-8.7 GiB; earlyoom threshold 5% RAM). Retrying with CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS=16 succeeded, but it forced a cold schema-apply bootstrap derivation and then a broad dependency compile: 410s compile overhead for 0.03s of test body. xtask should provide a first-class low-memory/narrow-proof path that does not route through a cold bootstrap rebuild when the checkout-local schema is already prepared, or otherwise explains and caches that bootstrap work predictably.
design: TARGETS (2026-07-06): the cold-bootstrap trigger is the checkout-local SQLx schema bootstrap invoked from the devshell/xtask wrapper when compiling SQLx-backed code; the incident shape is profile-knob changes (CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS / CARGO_BUILD_JOBS) forcing both a schema-apply rerun and a broad dependency recompile for a 0.03s test. Note #2286 already cached the schema bootstrap LOOKUP and #2375 hardened checkout postgres bootstrap — re-measure the 410s repro on current master BEFORE designing further; the remaining cost may now be dominated by the cargo profile recompile (not bootstrap), which would re-scope this bead to profile-stable test invocation defaults (e.g. pin the earlyoom-safe knobs in nextest profile instead of ad-hoc env).
acceptance_criteria: A narrow focused test after a profile/codegen env change no longer pays a cold schema-apply bootstrap: bootstrap artifact cached per checkout independent of cargo profile knobs (or reused across them). Measure: the 410s-overhead repro from the description drops to seconds on second run. VERIFY: time two consecutive focused runs with different CARGO_PROFILE_TEST_CODEGEN_UNITS.

title: Structural-join demo tier: capture.coincidence + provenance_pack (zero new capture)
description: The cheapest real L3 composites, available TODAY from provenance columns while interval-lift is being built: capture.coincidence (containment-join over one source_material's co-events - 'these observations came from one capture moment') and derivation.provenance_pack (provenance-join: any derived event to its material roots). They demo the join algebra with today's data and today's columns.
acceptance_criteria: capture.coincidence and derivation.provenance_pack ship as query-time composites (query units or recall cards — NOT new automata; structural joins are free from provenance columns): given an event ref, return co-events of its source material (containment) and its material-root pack (provenance walk). Each renders in recall/context with evidence refs; documented in derivation_catalog as the structural-join tier; live dev query returns real rows. VERIFY: sinexctl query / MCP call on dev runtime.
notes: 2026-07-06 scope extension (017 §7 structural tier, same zero-new-capture class): beyond capture.coincidence + provenance_pack, the tier also includes task.lifecycle (time-join over task.* events — the self-hosting task-history interval) and document.internal_structure (containment-join over one document's chunks/parses/embeddings — within-doc navigation, live inputs today) and the udev-dbus device-connect dedup (proves object-level occurrence dedup without touching the PK). Same rule as the first two: query-time composites/read models, not new automata, unless state is genuinely needed.

title: Close the failure loop: verify postmortem to next session's context seed
description: xtask test failures and their postmortems should seed the NEXT session's recall so agents do not rediscover the same failure. Today failures live in xtask-history.db and are not surfaced at session start. Polylogue close-the-failure-loop analog.
acceptance_criteria: A failed xtask test/check invocation produces a durable failure record that the NEXT session surfaces: sessionstart recall (or bd prime context) shows unresolved recent failures with the failing node + postmortem pointer; resolving/rerunning green clears it. VERIFY: seed a failing test, start fresh session, failure appears; fix, rerun, next session clean.

title: Projection/read-model registry with freshness, dependencies, and readiness caveats
description: recall, context_pack, projections, and CAs are built ad-hoc with no unifying abstraction (018 meta-gap). nbi.3 migrates documents off the DB trigger toward a projection mechanism - that mechanism SHOULD BE the read-model primitive, so the convergence-contract has one place to read materialization state from.
acceptance_criteria: derivation.projection_registry stores projection_kind, scope_key, semantics_version, input_fingerprint, coverage_window, status, freshness class, source counts, stale reason, last error, built_at, and verification_command. derivation.projection_dependencies supports replay/archive, redaction, and semantics-version staleness propagation. At least two read surfaces use ProjectionReadinessView caveats from the registry instead of ad-hoc readiness checks. Replay/archive, redaction policy changes, and semantics-version bumps mark dependent projections stale or partial before reads claim readiness.
notes:
2026-07-06 fold (blueprint lens L-iii): the read-model primitive should carry a FRESHNESS CLASS per read model (acceptable staleness: health=seconds, window-focus=seconds, git=minutes, sleep-import=days) so readiness caveats and the SLO tier report staleness RELATIVE to class — a 2-min-stale health surface is degraded, a 2-min-stale daily rollup is fine. Deliberately folded here instead of a separate freshness-registry bead: the class is a field of the read-model abstraction, not its own system.
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §7): projection lifecycle registry shape: {projection_kind, scope_key, semantics_version, input_fingerprint, coverage_window, status: absent|building|ready|stale|failed|partial, built_at, source_counts, stale_reason, last_error, verification_command}. Every read model powering context/recall registers here before being treated as ready. First slice: ProjectionReadiness DTO in sinexctl context output with hardcoded readiness for current read-time projections; then migrate one projection.
2026-07-07 blueprint decision: projection readiness is part of the derivation control plane. First slice may hardcode the readiness DTO, then migrate the document projection and one recall/current-device surface.

title: change.episode: the self-hosting cold-start demo
description: agent-session (from devloop-dogfood) time-joined and entity-joined with git commits, xtask history, and CI to render 'this change: intent, toil, verification, outcome'. The one derivation guaranteed dense, real, and present on ANY deployment - the deployment's own construction feeds it - which makes it the cold-start answer for horizontal expansion.
acceptance_criteria: change.episode derived events exist on the dev deployment from its own construction data (agent-session x git x xtask history): for a chosen real change, sinexctl renders intent/toil/verification/outcome with evidence refs to the underlying events; works on a fresh deployment with zero operator-life sources enabled (the cold-start claim). Depends on devloop/beads ingestion (hlv) and time/entity join availability.

title: Derivation DSL: query expressions as composable, agent-buildable derivations
description: SinexQuery already parses typed queries over events/entities/relations/materials (parse_sinex_query in crate/sinex-primitives/src/query_units.rs) and ORDER BY is landing (sinex-882). Extend into DERIVATION expressions: derivation := input_query (join input_query)* to aggregation to output_kind@lane. A new composite becomes a lane record (DATA), compiled to an AutomatonSpec - not hand-written Rust. This operationalizes algebraic composability and makes composites agent-buildable: an agent writes a derivation expression, the control plane runs it as a shadow lane, the diff evaluates it, a judgment promotes it.
acceptance_criteria: A derivation expression (input query + join + aggregation + output kind@lane) parses to a typed plan and compiles to a runnable lane/AutomatonSpec WITHOUT new Rust per composite; one existing composite (e.g. a4w.3.2 interruption.event) re-expressed in the DSL runs in a shadow lane and matches the hand-coded result; grammar documented in derivation_catalog. Gated on pq2/dcs algebra adoption + 0vx control plane.

title: Formalize the 4-operator join algebra in the derivation catalog
description: On adopting dec-join, record the formal core in crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md: every composite is (time | entity | provenance | containment)+ aggregation + interpretation, where time/entity are semantic (need lifting/resolution first) and provenance/containment are structural (free from provenance columns today). Reclassify screen.grounding as a containment-join (exact) rather than a time-join (fuzzy).
design:
DESIGN (2026-07-08, pq2 ADOPTED and closed — this bead is now pure encoding work, no open semantics):

TARGET: crate/sinexd/docs/automata/derivation_catalog.md, new "Formal core" section. Every composite is expressed as (time | entity | provenance | containment)+ then aggregation then interpretation.

OPERATOR TABLE to encode verbatim from the pq2 ruling:
- time (SEMANTIC, requires interval lifting): key columns = ts_orig ranges + watermark state. CLOSURE RULE: only closed-and-settled intervals are joinable (closed-before-watermark per 5s6 two-mode watermark). A join over the live edge is a category error, not a caveat — document it as a refusal state, not a caveat row.
- entity (SEMANTIC, requires resolution): key = resolved entity_id. Never raw strings; a string-match urge = a missing entity kind (rule 7).
- provenance (STRUCTURAL, free today): key = source_event_ids DAG reachability. Exact.
- containment (STRUCTURAL, free today): key = (source_material_id, anchor_byte, anchor_len) overlap. Exact.

RECLASSIFICATIONS required by the ruling: screen.grounding = containment-join (exact), NOT a fuzzy time-join; provenance_pack = provenance-join. Also classify attention.stream, work.episode, capture.coincidence.

REVISION CLOSURE (encode as a doctrine paragraph): when an input interval is superseded (y8v supersede-by-occurrence, RATIFIED), the join output is a derived descendant of the superseded row and re-derives through the SAME archive-cascade + scope-invalidation path replay uses. There is no join-specific staleness machinery; projections get staleness via 68c.4. This is what makes the algebra closed over its own inputs.

PITFALL: do not restate provenance.md or the y8v/pq2 rulings in prose — cite the closed beads and the doctrine page (rl4). The catalog states the algebra and classifies composites; the semantics live in their owning beads.
acceptance_criteria: derivation_catalog.md formal core section: every composite expressed as (time|entity|provenance|containment)+ aggregation + interpretation; the two structural operators documented as available-today (provenance columns), the two semantic ones as requiring lifting/resolution; existing composites (attention.stream, work.episode, capture.coincidence) each classified; xtask docs check passes. Blocked conceptually by pq2 decision.
notes: 2026-07-08: unblocked — pq2 ratified and closed. Design is now execution-grade; this bead gates k6s, 2xy, a4w.3.2, a4w.3.3, a4w.3.12.

title: Beads-native work loop: session-bead cross-links and archive-rendered work history
description: Agent sessions and beads should cross-link so work history is queryable from the substrate rather than reconstructed. Polylogue beads-native-work-loop analog; polylogue-lio already tracks a cross-repo devloop-on-beads contract for Sinex parity.
acceptance_criteria: Bead ids appear in session/work evidence and vice versa: (a) agent sessions record the bead(s) they worked (commit trailers or bd notes carry session refs); (b) a query surface (bd or sinex once hlv lands) answers which sessions/commits/PRs touched bead X; (c) the convention is documented in .agent/CONVENTIONS.md and survives one real session round-trip. Coordinate with hlv (beads as Sinex source) — this bead owns the cross-LINK convention, hlv owns ingestion.
notes: 2026-07-08 cross-repo parity: the polylogue side of the lio devloop-on-beads contract is RATIFIED (polylogue planning session 2026-07-08). Sinex-side execution begun this session: the session-bead cross-link convention is being added to .agent/CONVENTIONS.md (bead ids in commit trailers/PR bodies via the existing Ref pattern; bd notes carry session evidence per work slice — bd update <id> --append-notes with date + session ref). AC (b) query surface still waits on hlv ingestion; AC (c) round-trip proof = this session itself once the PR lands with bead-id trailers.

title: Light the evidence graph: entities and relations as a query target
description: entities and relations are ALREADY QueryUnitId query units (query_units.rs) but the entity/relation lane is write-only. Promote it to a queryable knowledge graph - the cheap CONSUME half of dec-entity. Tier-gated so heuristic relations return tier:heuristic, honest by construction.
design:
DESIGN (2026-07-08, REFRAMED by the ratified pq5-B ruling — read this before claiming):

CONSEQUENCE OF pq5-B: the 4-stage entity chain is now DEFAULT-OFF (sinex-ijz6 applies it). This bead can no longer assume entities/relations are being produced. Its role inverts: k6s is the NAMED CONSUMER whose existence is one of the two conditions for re-entry (the other is the 0vx shadow lane + a4w.1 extractor evaluation). Build the query surface against the EXISTING rows (the chain ran for months; the substrate is populated) and against a dev-enabled chain — do NOT re-enable it by default as part of this bead.

SHAPE: entities and relations are already QueryUnitId query units (query_units.rs). Light them:
1. Query surface: sinex query / MCP sinex_query list entities and relations with ViewEnvelope caveats.
2. HONESTY GATE (8cr): every returned relation carries its ClaimSupport vector, not a bare 0.5 float. Under pq5-B these rows are semantic_candidate DerivedProductClass — candidates awaiting judgment, never presented as knowledge. Rows produced BEFORE the ClaimSupport rollout have no support vector: render them with an explicit unknown-support caveat rather than defaulting them to anything (cmy: the 1.0/0.5 defaults with empty source refs are fabricated authority — do not launder them through the read surface).
3. Composite query (the AC): entity -> relations -> supporting events via lineage (provenance-join per the pq2 algebra — this is a STRUCTURAL join, exact, available today).
4. Recall/context can cite an entity card, carrying the same caveat.

WHY IT STILL MATTERS UNDER RETIREMENT: the pq5 re-entry gate needs a real consumer to argue against. k6s + a4w.1 (extractor P/R measurement) together are the evidence package that decides whether the chain comes back as a 0vx shadow lane. If the composite query proves unusable on real rows, that is a legitimate outcome and the chain stays retired — record that verdict on pq5s follow-up rather than forcing consumption.

VERIFY: sinexctl query entities + MCP call on live dev runtime with the chain dev-enabled; envelope shows the candidate/support caveat.
acceptance_criteria: sinex query / MCP sinex_query can list entities and relations as query units with ViewEnvelope caveats carrying tier:heuristic on co-occurrence relations; at least one documented composite query (entity -> relations -> supporting events via lineage) returns real rows on dev data; recall/context can cite an entity card. VERIFY: sinexctl query 'entities ...' + MCP call on live dev runtime; envelope shows tier caveat.

Blocked by sinex-cmy (verified: relation defaults confidence 1.0 with empty source refs; canonical_name globally unique across kinds — graph corruption risk if lit as-is). First slice: edges as a function of context_pack_build_id with debug JSON artifact, before any DB table.

title: Legibility campaign: Nix-for-personal-data framing, skim ladder, one-command demo
description: The positioning frame that makes the architecture legible in one sentence: declarative convergence + provenance + replay = the Nix analogy for personal data (declarative schema apply not migrations; replay = reproducibility; CAS material + provenance = the content-addressed store). Plus a README artifact-first skim ladder (L0-L3 distillation) and a one-command public demo (30s to first result). Polylogue legibility-campaign import.
acceptance_criteria: Three artifacts: (1) README skim ladder (L0 one-liner to L3 architecture) with the Nix-for-personal-data frame; (2) concepts page mapping Nix concepts to Sinex concepts (derivation/store/replay/convergence); (3) one-command demo (30s, no infra knowledge) that a fresh reader can run. Each reviewed against the honest-gaps rule — no capability claims beyond verified state. Pairs with ej9 (external artifacts) — this bead is the in-repo half.

title: Decision: adopt the 4-operator join algebra (time, entity, provenance, containment)
description: derivation_catalog.md states two join keys (time-join on ts_orig intervals, entity-join on resolved ids) and 'a third ad-hoc key = a missing entity kind'. That is right for SEMANTIC joins but omits two STRUCTURAL joins intrinsic to the provenance model: provenance-join along the source_event_ids DAG (already the trace/replay substrate) and containment-join on shared source_material_id + overlapping anchor ranges. Both are available TODAY from provenance columns and are the cheapest honest L3 - and several catalog composites filed under time-join (screen.grounding) are actually containment-joins done exactly instead of fuzzily.
design:
PROPOSED RULING (2026-07-08, encoded for operator ratification — doctrine-level, one-way):

ADOPT the 4-operator join algebra. The catalog recognizes exactly four join operators; a fifth ad-hoc key is by definition a missing entity kind or a missing containment declaration.

1. TIME-JOIN (semantic): overlap/containment on ts_orig intervals. CLOSURE RULE (adversarial input 1 answered): time-joins consume only CLOSED-AND-SETTLED intervals — closed-before-watermark per the 5s6 two-mode watermark; the live edge is never joinable. A join product over an open interval is a category error, not a caveat.
2. ENTITY-JOIN (semantic): equality on resolved entity ids (never raw strings; a string match urge = missing entity kind).
3. PROVENANCE-JOIN (structural): reachability along source_event_ids — the trace/replay substrate reused as a query operator. Exact, always available.
4. CONTAINMENT-JOIN (structural): shared source_material_id + anchor-range overlap. Exact; screen.grounding and provenance_pack reclassify here (they were filed as fuzzy time-joins but are exact containment).

REVISION PROPAGATION (adversarial input 2 answered): when an input interval is superseded (y8v supersede-by-occurrence), the join output is a derived descendant of the superseded row — the SAME archive-cascade + scope-invalidation path replay uses re-derives it. No join-specific staleness machinery; projections get staleness via 68c.4. This makes the algebra closed over revision: every operator declares what its outputs do on input supersession = re-derive, uniformly.

KEY COLUMNS TABLE (for dcs to encode in derivation_catalog.md): time -> ts_orig ranges + watermark state; entity -> resolved entity_id; provenance -> source_event_ids DAG; containment -> (source_material_id, anchor_byte, anchor_len).

ALTERNATIVE CONSIDERED (rejected): keep 2-operator catalog + treat provenance/containment as implementation details — rejected because both are already load-bearing in replay/trace code and hiding them produces exactly the screen.grounding misclassification observed.
acceptance_criteria: Recorded decision adopting (or amending) the 4-operator algebra; derivation_catalog.md updated by dcs accordingly; each operator names its key columns and its semantic/structural class; screen.grounding and provenance_pack reclassified under the adopted operators. Operator sign-off (doctrine-level).
notes:
2026-07-07 sequencing round: promoted wave:4->3. This is a pure operator decision with zero hard blockers that now gates five beads (k6s, 2xy, a4w.3.2, a4w.3.3, a4w.3.12) after the dcs->pq2 gate swap; landing it before wave:4 work starts unblocks the consumption lane. One-way reversibility noted — take the deliberation, but do not let the decision idle behind implementation waves it gates.
2026-07-07 adversarial hardening — two decision inputs the join-algebra ruling must cover or explicitly defer: (1) interval-end watermark semantics: every interval end in the current stack means 'the next event arrived', not 'the state ended' (closure-by-next-arrival, see 5s6) — a time-join must define which intervals are joinable (closed-before-watermark) or joins over the live edge are nondeterministic; (2) revision propagation: intervals have no revision mechanism today (y8v) — the algebra must say what happens to a join output when an input interval is later superseded/extended (re-derive via scope invalidation? projection-only staleness via 68c.4?). Without both, the algebra is not closed over its actual inputs.
RATIFIED 2026-07-08 (operator, decision-brief batch): 4-operator join algebra ADOPTED as specified in design (closure rule: closed-and-settled inputs only; revision propagation: supersession re-derives via the replay invalidation path). Catalog encoding is owned by dcs (open). Closing this decision bead.
close_reason: Decision FINAL and operator-ratified 2026-07-08: 4-operator join algebra ADOPTED (time/entity/provenance/containment) with the closure rule (joins consume closed-and-settled intervals only) and revision propagation (input supersession re-derives outputs via the replay archive-cascade/invalidation path — no join-specific staleness machinery). Key-columns table in design. Catalog encoding owned by sinex-dcs (open, now unblocked); screen.grounding/provenance_pack reclassify under containment there. Verify: bd show sinex-pq2 (design ruling + ratification note); bd show sinex-dcs (catalog carrier open).

title: Dogfood the conductor devloop state into the Sinex substrate
description: The .agent/conductor-devloop state (OPERATING-LOG.md, HANDOFF-LATEST.md, EVENTS.jsonl) is markdown/JSONL OUTSIDE Sinex - the silo the devloop is fighting. The self-observation lens says Sinex's own construction is its richest always-present derivation testbed. Depends on staticImport/producer plumbing; pairs with hlv + 8ve.

title: Decision: bound the wildcard fan-in (five automata reading *)
description: Five automata read the wildcard event stream (session, analytics, entity_extractor, tag_applier, and the summarizers via activity). This is the automaton-side of the telemetry-lane lesson: x9i separated self-observation for the ENGINE, not for the automaton fan-out. Every new *-reader is a linear cost on the busiest stream.
design:
DECISION FRAME: the *-readers are session-detector (by design: needs everything), analytics, tag-applier (+ summarizers via broad activity types); entity-extractor was narrowed to MaterialOnly earlier (8a589c0be). Reflection-lane separation (x9i) already removes self-observation volume from the activity stream.

PROPOSED RULING (2026-07-08, encoded for operator ratification): per-automaton verdicts, effective with pq5-B: (1) session-detector: KEEP WILDCARD — its contract is everything by design. (2) entity-extractor/resolver/enricher/relation-extractor: MOOT under pq5 ruling (B) — default-disabled removes them from the fan-in entirely; on shadow-lane re-entry they return with explicit filter_subjects allowlists, never wildcard. (3) analytics + tag-applier: DEMAND-GATE — default-disabled until a named consumer exists (same construct-validity logic as pq5; recall p1 consuming analytics would be that evidence). (4) summarizers: NARROW-FILTER — push their broad activity-type sets down into filter_subjects via automaton_consumer_config (mechanism already exists). (5) MEASUREMENT REGARDLESS: per-automaton events/sec gauge + budget alert lands as part of n23.3 capacity model so future *-reader proposals argue against a measured baseline, not vibes. Post-x9i re-measure remains the trigger for tuning, not for the structural verdicts above — the structural calls (gate zero-consumer automata, allowlist where the type set is known) are correct at any volume. Options (c) shared pre-filtered sub-stream: REJECTED — adds a stream tier before measurement justifies it; revisit only if the gauge shows filter evaluation itself dominating.
acceptance_criteria: Recorded decision with per-automaton verdict (keep-wildcard / narrow-filter / demand-gate) + measured events/sec basis post-x9i; implementation beads created and dep-linked for any narrowing; the confirmed-stream consumer inventory (sinexctl runtime automata) matches the decision. Operator sign-off.
notes: RATIFIED 2026-07-08 (operator, decision-brief batch): per-automaton wildcard verdicts FINAL as specified in design. Narrowing/gating implementation folded into the portfolio-application bead; events/sec gauge requirement transferred to n23.3. Closing this decision bead.
close_reason: Decision FINAL and operator-ratified 2026-07-08: session-detector keeps wildcard (contract requires everything); entity chain moot under pq5-B; analytics + tag-applier demand-gated default-off; summarizers narrowed via filter_subjects pushdown; per-automaton events/sec gauge lands in n23.3; pre-filtered sub-stream REJECTED pending measurement. The former x9i blocker removed: the ruling makes post-x9i re-measurement a TUNING input (n23.3 gauge), not a decision gate — structural verdicts are volume-independent. Implementation carrier = sinex-ijz6. Verify: bd show sinex-vfy (ruling + ratification); bd show sinex-ijz6.

title: Adopt an area:* label namespace across the bead graph

Namespace as designed plus area:architecture added for the nbi family (17 -> 18 areas). Legacy single-word labels retained (non-destructive; existing bd list -l queries keep working). Verify: bd list --json -n 0 | check area: labels — 151/151 exactly-one.

title: Producer protocol as a public contract: any tool becomes a source, no Rust adapter
description: polylogue already publishes EventIntent to NATS with no sinexd consumer process (the external-producer pattern; see crate/sinexd/src/sources/source_contracts/polylogue.rs). Promote it to a DOCUMENTED public wire contract plus self-serve schema registration, so scripts/agents/other tools/phone apps become sources trivially. This is how Sinex scales in source VARIETY horizontally at zero core cost - the app-store surface.
acceptance_criteria: A documented public wire contract (docs page + schema): EventIntent envelope, subjects, auth, material registration, and self-serve schema registration flow; one NON-polylogue external producer (a shell script counts) publishes events end-to-end following only the doc; contract covered by a compatibility test so sinexd changes cannot silently break external producers.

title: Refresh fresh-agent MCP proof after caveat fixes
description: After PRs #2279/#2280/#2281, rerun the fresh Codex Sinex MCP proof against current master. The previous proof exposed two caveats: single-quoted RFC3339 query literals and raw runtime-status gateway request errors. Produce an inspectable artifact showing whether a fresh agent now uses sinex_orient/sinex_query/runtime-status tools cleanly, with resolvable refs and explicit caveats only for real evidence gaps.

title: xtask preflight/proof substrate seal (original pre-queue DDL race fixed on master)
description:
Concurrent xtask invocations can run checkout-local SQLx/Postgres schema bootstrap before the coordinated job slot is assigned. During recall verification on 2026-07-03, launching test/check commands in parallel emitted duplicate pg_class_relname_nsp_index errors for ix_archived_events_archived_at / ix_archived_events_ts_orig, even though xtask later queued jobs behind the active slot. This is confusing observability and a real reliability smell: the queue boundary should cover schema bootstrap, or schema apply must be idempotent under concurrent bootstrap.

## Steps to Reproduce
On a fresh checkout, launch two xtask test/check invocations simultaneously -> duplicate pg_class_relname_nsp_index errors for ix_archived_events_archived_at / ix_archived_events_ts_orig during concurrent schema bootstrap (observed 2026-07-03).
design: Move checkout-local SQLx schema bootstrap behind the same coordinated slot used by xtask jobs, or add a per-checkout bootstrap lock before apply. Do not paper over this by telling agents never to use --bg; xtask should make queued parallel verification safe and quiet. Evidence jobs: 2001381-2001383, with foreground wrapper output showing duplicate pg_class_relname_nsp_index while jobs were queued/running.
acceptance_criteria: Two xtask test/check invocations launched simultaneously on a fresh checkout produce zero duplicate pg_class/index-exists errors: bootstrap runs exactly once behind the coordinated slot or a per-checkout lock, the second waits. VERIFY: parallel launch repro from description; check journald/xtask output for ix_archived_events_* duplicate errors.
notes: 2026-07-07 audit (new-gpt-pro 03): the originally-described failure (two frontends both run DDL before queueing on a fresh checkout) is FIXED in current source — wrapper SQLx bootstrap serialized under flock (flake.nix:1884-1897), launcher-only --bg skips pre-exec bootstrap (flake.nix:1457-1464), Rust schema apply waits on exclusive lock (preflight.rs:977-1011). Remaining live risk is proof reuse bypassing/under-sealing preflight — now owned by the proof-substrate-seal bead. Keep this bead only for the parallel-smoke regression (two xtask check --bg on a schema-pending checkout, second queues without duplicate pg_class/index errors); close after that test lands.

title: Moment search / evidence windows: seed hits become intervals, intervals gather heterogeneous evidence, output states support and overreach
description: The flagship consumption capability of the substrate claim, and a genuinely new query shape: 'commands I ran while discussing X' is not an event search — it is a MOMENT search. Pipeline: (1) SEED: full-text/semantic hits for X in agent messages (or any content family) produce candidate anchor points; (2) WINDOW: anchors lift to candidate time intervals (session-bounded, gap-merged — interval logic shared with attention.stream, not reinvented); (3) GATHER: within each window, pull heterogeneous evidence across families — shell commands, window focus, browser visits, fs activity, git operations, machine context — via the normal ts_orig window queries; (4) REPORT: for each moment, state SUPPORT (evidence consistent with the seed topic), CAVEATS (families with no coverage in the window — from source-continuity data), CONTRADICTIONS (evidence inconsistent with the claim), and TEMPORAL OVERREACH (the seed discussed X at 14:02-14:15; commands at 14:40 are adjacent, not during — the boundary is stated, not smoothed). This is where Sinex-as-backend pays: heterogeneous evidence without forcing everything into Polylogue's chat schema. Polylogue consumes it for 'what was happening around this session'; the operator consumes it for any 'while' question; agents consume it via MCP.
