design: DECISIONS: (1) build as a composition over existing primitives — seed search (docs/semantic search + payload text match), interval lifting (shared L2 helper), window evidence pull (the recall/context substrate) — the new code is the orchestration + the four-verdict report shape, NOT a parallel query engine (algebra rule). (2) Surface: sinexctl moments <query> [--families ...] [--range ...] + an events/context RPC + MCP tool; output = moment cards, each with interval, seed refs, per-family evidence refs, and the four verdict sections; every claim in the report is a resolvable ref. (3) Support/contradiction classification starts MECHANICAL (family presence, term co-occurrence, count thresholds — each a measured quantity per standing rule 1); LLM-graded relevance is a later recorded-effect enrichment (k4c.1 lane), never a hardcoded score. (4) Coverage caveats reuse the source-continuity machinery from recall verbatim. (5) Latency: moment search is explicitly a bounded-work operation (N windows cap, per-family limits) — it inherits the g3k read-model outcome; do not promise interactive full-archive moments before that lands. PITFALL: seeds from agent messages need the observation vocabulary sibling landed first for message-grain anchors; the fallback seed source (documents/browser content) works today — build the pipeline against what exists, add the agent family when the lane flows.
acceptance_criteria: The motivating query works end-to-end: given a topic discussed in a known session, sinexctl moments returns the discussion interval(s) with the shell commands actually run during them, families absent from the window listed as caveats, and adjacent-but-outside evidence marked as overreach rather than included silently; all refs resolve; JSON stable for MCP; a falsifier run (topic never discussed) returns zero moments, not fabricated ones.

title: Polylogue observation vocabulary: the full normalized domain as anchored Sinex events
description: Layer 2->3 of the epic: replace the metadata-thin session_indexed lane with the complete normalized-observation vocabulary. Kinds (each a stable-semantics observation, per the epic's admission boundary): agent.session.{started,resumed,compacted,ended} (Point/transition; compaction is a first-class observation — it explains context loss in any later forensic question); agent.message.emitted (Point; role, model, block summary, content BY ANCHOR into session JSONL material + content hash — never embedded text); agent.tool_call.{started,returned} (Point pair; tool name, args digest, outcome class, duration — the span the work-packet derivation lifts into intervals); agent.subagent.{started,stopped}; agent.attachment.referenced (ref to the attachment material); polylogue.export.imported (provider export landed — ts_orig = export's content time span, NOT import time); polylogue.annotation.{marked,read,analyzed} (operator/agent actions on sessions — intentional-role events); polylogue.usage.observed (provider usage/accounting rows — the cost plane). Producer stays the polylogued daemon (external-producer mode, EventIntent to NATS — no sinexd-side consumer process). Materials: per-session JSONL registered with growing-file re-registration (content-hash + size watermark); attachments as CAS blobs; provider export archives as import materials.
design: DECISIONS: (1) every kind declares (catalog extension rules) temporal character, occurrence key (session_id + JSONL line identity for message/tool kinds; provider row identity for usage), ts_orig quality (IntrinsicContent — provider/JSONL timestamps) and consumer derivation (work.episode/change.episode for session+tool kinds; consumption/cost views for usage; moment search for messages). (2) Volume class declared up front: messages ~low-millions total, comparable to page.visited — admissible at full grain; tool calls similar order; usage tiny. Backfill of the historical archive runs PACED (sinex-2n9 mechanism) — do not repeat the journald incident with chat history. (3) Occurrence identity must survive Polylogue re-parses: JSONL line identity = (material, anchor_byte) with equivalence key from (session_id, message/provider id) so a re-normalized session does not duplicate observations. (4) No polylogue.source_material.observed — epic correction 2. (5) Schema evolution: kinds versioned via semantics_version; Polylogue-side normalization changes replay through the normal pipeline (archive + re-derive). CONSTRUCT-VALIDITY WIRING (what consumes this, named now): moment/evidence-window search (sibling child), change.episode/work.episode composites (a4w.3.9/.4), cost/accounting query units, recall timeline family 'agent'.
acceptance_criteria: All kinds flowing from live polylogued against real sessions; a sampled agent.message.emitted resolves via show -> material anchor to the exact JSONL line and the provider timestamp equals ts_orig; compaction events present for a session known to have compacted; historical backfill of the archive completes paced with counts recorded; re-running Polylogue normalization over an already-ingested session creates zero duplicate occurrences; recall --window over a coding hour shows the agent family interleaved with terminal/git.
notes: 2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §8): mirror STRUCTURED metadata, not transcripts: polylogue.{session,work_event,tool_call,provider_usage,context_snapshot}.observed with stable ids, archive refs, time bounds, project hints, tool names, cwd, command hash/summary, file refs, usage/cost provenance, content hashes. Polylogue stays transcript authority. Join-power test: 'AI sessions/work-events around this commit/command' must distinguish same-day from same-work (needs tool-call cwd/file refs, not just session bounds).

title: Agent arrival: MCP profile registration + ambient injection + self-teaching tool surface
description: The place agents ARRIVE. The MCP server already exposes the right tools (sinex.context_pack/query/search_events + 40 more in crate/sinexctl/src/mcp.rs) but no agent profile loads it — sinnix's mcp-registry (flake/data/mcp-registry.nix) registers Polylogue/Lynchpin/Serena but not Sinex, so the widest surface in the codebase has zero agent consumers. Three legs: (1) register sinex-mcp-server in the sinnix MCP registry (appropriate profiles; read-only role default); (2) ambient injection — the SessionStart hook feeding agents recent machine context (sinex-cfr executes this leg; child-linked); (3) self-teaching surface: tool descriptions + a sinex.orient tool (or MCP instructions block) that teaches a cold agent the data model — refs, materials-vs-derived, query expression shape, honest-gap semantics — so first-contact agents use it correctly without reading repo docs. Agent-tangibility test: a fresh Claude session with no Sinex knowledge answers 'what was I doing yesterday 14:00-15:00' correctly via MCP, citing resolvable refs.
design: DECISION: registration follows the existing mcp-registry.nix pattern (sinnix-side change; coordinate like other cross-repo wiring); default profile gets read-only token via runtime-target descriptor; the orient/instructions content is generated from the same source as the concepts-page ladder (sinex-ej9) so internal and external explanations never fork; cfr remains the hook leg and is not duplicated here.
acceptance_criteria: claude/codex default profiles list sinex tools; the cold-agent test passes (fresh session, correct answer, resolvable refs); orientation content generated not hand-forked; read-only by default with explicit opt-up.
notes: 2026-07-03 progress: implemented sinex.orient in crate/sinexctl/src/mcp.rs backed by shared docs crate/sinexctl/docs/agent_orientation.md; updated MCP docs/tests. Sinnix side registers sinex in the recall MCP tier for Codex/Claude/Gemini and installs mcp-sinex without forcing Sinex compilation during Home Manager activation. Verification so far: xtask test job 2001393 passed focused MCP catalog/orient/doc tests; stdio protocol proof listed 66 tools and called sinex.orient; registry eval shows sinex in lean/evidence/browser/full profile selections; Home Manager activationPackage build produced /nix/store/d1d9xxv65rkhhrplx8ia3gb6gzfkapk9-home-manager-generation. Proof packet: .agent/demos/sinex/agent-arrival-mcp-registration-20260703T144026Z. Residual: switch/commit/PR flow in progress; package fmt check job 2001394 exposed pre-existing broader sinexctl formatting drift outside this slice.
close_reason: Completed implementation substrate: PR #2277 merged as 4cb3f5d299da471f41e6106c682576263e021566 adds sinex.orient, shared orientation docs, MCP docs, and tests; Sinnix commit 57e2f0f registers/installs mcp-sinex in agent profiles and was switched live. Focused MCP tests passed (job 2001393), pre-push changed-strict passed, and stdio proof listed/called sinex.orient. Deferred behavioral fresh-agent natural-language proof tracked by sinex-4bn.

title: The adjudication seat: a judgment queue an operator can actually clear
description: The place you JUDGE — the missing workflow for the doctrine's only path to truth. Today proposals (shadow-lane entities/relations/curation diffs) accumulate with no queue, no triage order, no fast accept/reject loop; when the semantic plane activates (sinex-5v6, sinex-2nd), an unworkable judgment UX means L4 silently stops mattering. Build: a proposal queue surface (TUI pane + CLI) ordered by evidence strength x staleness, single-keystroke judge (accept/reject/edit-then-accept/defer), batch judgment over a selection with one recorded judgment covering N proposals (the judgment event carries all proposal parents), and session summaries ('you judged 40 proposals, 3 deferred'). Delegated policy comes AFTER the manual loop proves its shape: a recorded standing judgment ('auto-accept entity-merge proposals with >=N corroborating sources from lane L') is itself an adjudication object with provenance — but do not build delegation before the manual queue has real usage evidence.
design: DECISION: judgments stay typed and recorded exactly per doctrine (§adjudication) — the UX changes throughput, never the authority model; batch = one judgment event, N proposal parents (legal: NonEmptyVec); queue ordering factors are each measured quantities surfaced in the row (standing rule 1); defer is a first-class verdict so the queue can reach zero honestly; queue age/depth are telemetry so the epic's 'judged within days' acceptance is measurable. Surfaces: sinexctl semantic judge (interactive TUI mode + scriptable flags) over the existing curation/proposal read models — check sinexctl semantic + curation_proposals MCP tool for the current read surface before adding any.
acceptance_criteria: Operator clears a real 50-proposal backlog in one sitting without leaving the TUI; every canonical write traces to a judgment event with proposal parents; queue depth/age queryable; deferred items resurface; no auto-promotion path exists.

title: Operator daily surface: today-view TUI pane + morning brief as the default entry points
description: The place you LOOK. Two entry points sharing one substrate: (1) sinexctl tui 'today' view — the attention timeline (a4w.3.1 when it lands; sessions+summaries before then), source health strip (stall states from the continuity data), pending-judgment count, latest brief — the pane an operator keeps open or opens by keybind; (2) the morning brief — the citation-required daily brief (sinex-a4w.2) delivered ambiently: written to a stable path + desktop notification + oracle-digest inclusion, so consumption requires zero initiation. The habit loop closes when checking Sinex is cheaper than not checking it.
design: DECISION: build on the existing sinexctl tui workbench — extend, never fork a new binary; every widget = an existing query unit/card rendered (no TUI-only queries); brief delivery = systemd user timer invoking sinexctl report daily --output <stable-path> + notify-send, and the sinnix oracle script includes the latest brief file (one-line change there); keybind/alias wiring in sinnix dots. Instrument invocations (consumption telemetry) so nbi.2 can measure whether the habit loop actually closed — the epic's acceptance depends on this being measurable.
acceptance_criteria: Today-view renders timeline + health + judgment count from live data with honest gaps; brief lands at a stable path every morning with a notification; both are used-in-anger for a week and the telemetry shows it; no new query paths that bypass query units. COHERENCE ADDITIONS (2026-07-07): bare sinexctl renders Today by default when a runtime target is configured (command-center catalog moves to an explicit path); sinexctl tui opens a Today tab by default with Resume / Away-delta / Judgment / Health panes; CLI, TUI, and MCP Today surfaces render from ONE intermediate view model (resume_summary, away_delta, judgment_summary, health_summary, caveats, actions, query_echo); Away delta uses an explicit since_last_seen checkpoint with override — never a hard-coded one hour; Judgment pane shows count + oldest age + up to three cards, with zero red badges/streaks/nag copy; every empty pane renders the first relevant caveat + a suggested readiness probe matching JSON/YAML; brief generation extends report/metrics substrate (report daily --output or metrics report today --output), no bypass query path.
notes: 2026-07-07 coherence review (new-gpt-pro sessions/10): current bare sinexctl is a command catalog (main.rs:224-228,350-413); TUI has no Today/Judgment tab (tui.rs:72-84) and hard-codes last-hour events (tui.rs:396-402). Full daily-loop design + unified vocabulary table (since/until, around/window, source vs source_identifier vs source_family, limit/budget_tokens) in the session final answer — treat that table as the flag-naming contract for all new operator surfaces.

title: Polylogue on Sinex: evidential substrate, not ontology replacement
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03, deepened from the recovered plan): take Sinex-as-Polylogue-substrate FAR — but the plan was never 'merge Polylogue into Sinex'. Polylogue stays the conversation/agent-work PRODUCT with its own semantics: it knows sessions, messages, blocks, tool calls/results, attachments, providers, agents/subagents, context compactions, exports, marks, read states, analysis notes, usage/accounting. Sinex is the generalized evidence/event substrate underneath. Full collapse is rejected — it would dissolve the product boundary into generic event-browser sludge. THE FIVE LAYERS (each must be real, see construct-validity gate in design): (1) raw material — provider exports, session JSONL/Markdown, attachments, blobs; (2) normalized Polylogue domain — the ontology above, authority stays in Polylogue; (3) Sinex event/material substrate — normalized observations with provenance, material refs, timestamps, lineage; (4) derived/correlation layer — session-commit-PR-check RELATIONS (derived, never intrinsic), moment candidates, evidence windows, Agent Work Packets, context packs, coverage caveats; (5) Polylogue UX/read models — timelines, session views, agent-work views, search, daily summaries. BACKEND TRAJECTORY (staged, both keep Polylogue standalone-functional): Phase A = deep export/sync (external-producer publishing, today's shape deepened to the full observation vocabulary + materials); Phase B = Sinex-primary evidence — Polylogue's SQLite demotes from source-of-truth to REBUILDABLE read-model/index over Sinex-held materials and events; the rebuild test (drop SQLite, reconstruct from Sinex) is the proof the substrate claim is real. A generic storage-trait 'alternative backend' middle option is skipped: what moves is EVIDENCE, not Polylogue's read models — those stay Polylogue's forever. Children carry the capability work; cross-links: hlv (bd: refs), a4w.3.9 (Agent Work Packet derivation), cem.10, 266 (@@-DSL).
design: SETTLED CORRECTIONS (operator-established; DECISION lines, do not re-litigate): (1) ADMISSION BOUNDARY: not 'selected/important events' — anything that is a normalized observation with stable semantics is admissible: session occurred/resumed/ended, message emitted, tool call started / result returned, subagent started/stopped, context compacted, attachment/material referenced, provider export imported, mark/read/analyze performed, provider usage observed. This supersedes the earlier 'no per-message events' line in this epic: messages ARE domain observations; volume is handled by anchoring (content-bearing events carry (material_id, anchor_byte) into session JSONL + metadata/hashes, never embedded bulk — catalog rule 2) and by declared volume class (catalog rule 6), not by exclusion. (2) MATERIAL IS NOT AN EVENT: no polylogue.source_material.observed or any '*.material.observed' kind — raw exports/logs/attachments are substrate (layer 1) registered in the material registry; domain observations (layer 2->3) are what become events. Violating this recurses implementation detail into the domain. (3) ts_orig IS SOURCE-DOMAIN TIME: message -> provider message timestamp; session -> actual session occurrence/start; export-import -> when the exported thing happened, not when imported. Ingestion time is ts_coided/provenance/audit — a Polylogue-on-Sinex that degrades ts_orig to 'when my daemon noticed' is broken (this is the existing temporal-ledger doctrine applied strictly). (4) NO session_commit OBJECT: sessions (Polylogue), commits/branches/PRs (git), commands/spans/checks (telemetry) are separate domains; their connection is a DERIVED relation computed in layer 4 (Agent Work Packet / evidence window), never an intrinsic event type pretending a session is a commit. (5) CONSTRUCT-VALIDITY GATE: every named layer/kind/derived object must be wired into a real read model or user-facing operation (context packs, evidence windows, work packets, timelines, accounting views, coverage caveats, audit trails) — a backend that hoards evidence without answering questions now is the named failure mode; no child of this epic closes on schema+ingestion alone. (6) ONTOLOGY AUTHORITY: Polylogue's domain model defines what a conversation/message/tool-call/compaction/agent/work-packet MEANS; Sinex stores and serves the evidence. Sinex is Polylogue's evidential substrate, not its ontology replacement.
acceptance_criteria: Phase A: the full observation vocabulary flows (child 1) with byte-anchored content events and source-domain ts_orig spot-checked against provider timestamps; Polylogue standalone suite stays green with the Sinex flag off. Phase B: the rebuild test passes — Polylogue reconstructs its SQLite read models from Sinex materials+events with a recorded parity diff; at least two user-facing operations run THROUGH the substrate (moment search child; work-packet view via a4w.3.9); no '*.material.observed' kind exists (rg proves); no intrinsic cross-domain fusion kinds exist. Every child cites which layer-4/5 surface consumes it (construct-validity gate).
notes: 2026-07-06 rawlog additions to the integration surface (06-28 21:55/22:48-22:53): the annotation DSL extends beyond annotations to lightweight REQUESTS in prose ('language overlay... batched micro-requests, interpreted by polylogue or sinex, injected into context') and agents dynamically tuning their own context injection ('adjust whether they want a list of recent sessions at session start... figured empirically over time, based on their annotations'). Both stay implementable: request forms are more grammar entries parsed by the same extractor; context-tuning is config-as-data read by the sessionstart hooks. Sequence AFTER the 4-form annotation DSL proves volume-of-use (the operator's own gate: 'extend only after volume of use proves need').

title: Daily-grain rollup lane + parity: per-domain (domain, local-day) derived events replace Lynchpin daily products
description: Lynchpin's layer-3 (derived daily products: daily_signals.ndjson, spotify/daily.ndjson, web_category_daily, operator_daily) and its DuckDB substrate tables exist to make cross-domain daily correlation cheap. Sinex replacement: a general per-domain daily-rollup lane — windowed/scope-reconciler automata keyed (domain, local-day) emitting typed daily summary events (activity, terminal, web, media, physiological once rkv.6 lands, machine telemetry) — plus query units over them (sinex-lv8 direction) so 'join domains on day' is one query expression, not an NDJSON copy step. The existing hourly/daily summarizers are the seed; this generalizes them from activity-only to per-domain and makes local-day (timezone-honest, cf. sinex-nbi.5) the scope key. Parity target: every field Lynchpin's daily products carried either exists in a Sinex daily event, is derivable by query, or is recorded as deliberately dropped in the ledger.
design: DECISION: one parameterized daily-rollup automaton family (domain -> input filter + reducer), not N bespoke automata; scope = (domain, local_day, tz) with DST-correct day boundaries; emitted kind activity.summary.daily.<domain> or a single kind with domain field (prefer single kind + domain field — parsimony rule); replayable (semantics_version); backfill over history via normal replay, paced (sinex-2n9). Query side: day-grain query units so 'sleep_hours x commits_count by day' is expressible in one expression — this is the acceptance query.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl query can answer a two-domain day-grain join (e.g. sleep duration vs commit count per day over a month) in one expression against derived daily events; parity table for daily_signals fields recorded in the subsumption ledger; timezone/DST day-boundary test passes.
notes:
2026-07-06 parity source enumeration (from census): the proving domains are lynchpin health_daily + sleep_productivity + personal_signals substrate tables — their DuckDB schemas ARE the parity column lists. Substrate table defs: /realm/project/sinity-lynchpin/lynchpin/substrate/{personal,readers_health}.py.
2026-07-08: this lane is the canonical example of the generality rule — one parameterized family, domains as data. Physiological (rkv.6), web (rkv.16), media (spotify), machine (rkv.17) all land as domain parameters here; any PR adding a per-domain rollup automaton instead is wrong by doctrine.

title: Recorded-analysis lane: generalize the effect ledger from model calls to arbitrary computations
description: The abstraction improvement the subsumption forces. Lynchpin's real advantage over Sinex was never data — it was that a one-shot cross-source investigation (weather_correlations, substance_kinetics, mood_correlations) cost one Python file over DuckDB. Sinex's only sanctioned derivation shape is a Rust automaton with deployment ceremony, so ad-hoc analysis either doesn't happen or happens as untracked scratch SQL — the exact provenance hole the system exists to close. Fix: generalize the L3 doctrine ('every model call is a recorded effect') to 'every ANALYSIS is a recorded effect'. A recorded analysis = a computation over the store whose inputs (query-unit specs + as-of watermark + input event-id set digest), code identity (script/notebook hash + semantics tag), and outputs (artifact and/or derived events) are registered — making one-shot investigations replayable, auditable, and citable without automaton ceremony. This is the ModelEffectRecord pattern with 'model call' replaced by 'computation', and it is how ~30 of Lynchpin's ~40 analysis modules translate (the standing ~10 become real L3 automata).
design: DECISION SKETCH (settle details in implementation, doctrine is fixed): (1) New registry surface analysis_records: id, title, question, code_hash, code_ref (repo path or CAS blob), input_spec (query-unit expressions + as-of watermark), input_digest, started/finished, artifact refs, optional emitted derived-event ids. (2) Execution shape: sinexctl ops analysis run -- <script> (or an xtask lane for dev): wrapper snapshots the input spec, executes, registers the record, stores the artifact in CAS, and — when the analysis emits typed conclusions — publishes derived events whose parents are the input event set (bounded: for large input sets, parent = the analysis-record ref via a synthetic material or a designated provenance mode; DECIDE: derived-from-analysis provenance representation, this is the one open design question — options: (a) cap parent lists + record overflow in the analysis record, (b) analysis record as source material and conclusions as material-provenance events anchored into the record — (b) is cleaner and matches 'the record IS the evidence'). (3) Replay semantics: re-run = new record, new interpretation (never mutate); as-of watermark makes reruns comparable. (4) Language: Python/SQL scripts are fine — the LANE is Rust-registered, the analysis is not; agents author analyses as easily as Lynchpin modules. (5) Standing promotion path: an analysis that proves recurring value gets promoted to an L3 automaton (the record's question/spec is the automaton's spec). (6) The demo/report shelf artifacts and cem falsifier packets can also register through this lane — one evidence genre.
acceptance_criteria: An agent can run a cross-source one-shot analysis (e.g. reimplement mood_correlations over Sinex data) in one command; the record is queryable (sinexctl show analysis:<id>) with input digest, code hash, artifact ref; re-running with unchanged inputs+code is detectable as identical (digest match); conclusions, when emitted, carry provenance per the decided representation; one real Lynchpin analysis reproduced through the lane end-to-end as the proof.
notes: 2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §12): effect-key v1 must include provider, model_id, model_revision_or_pricing_epoch, operation_kind, prompt_template_hash, input_material_hashes, input_event_refs, schema_version, params (temperature/seed/top_p), privacy_policy_version, redaction_scope_hash, output_schema_hash. Replay policy explicit per effect: exact_reuse_allowed | revalidate_required | reuse_forbidden_after_redaction | nondeterministic_sample_only. Redaction MUST invalidate cached effects whose inputs were redacted. Dry-run explainer: why would/wouldn't two candidate calls reuse one effect.

title: Lynchpin subsumption: absorb the higher-order layers properly, retire the stopgap
description: Operator directive (2026-07-03): ~total subsumption of Lynchpin into Sinex — not just sources. Lynchpin is the dormant stopgap; Sinex is the real system. Its four layers map onto the tower, and the higher layers are the hard, valuable part. Layer map: (1) lynchpin.sources.* typed iterators -> Sinex source contracts over RAW provider data (never Lynchpin's processed intermediates); (2) lynchpin.substrate.* DuckDB read models (commit_fact, ai_work_event, machine_metric_sample) -> Sinex query units / consumer-maintained projections; (3) /realm/data/derived/lynchpin daily products (daily_signals, spotify/daily) -> L2 windowed daily-rollup automata, typed + versioned + replayable; (4) lynchpin.analysis.* (~40 modules: burnout_warning, mood_correlations, lifestyle_correlations, sleep_architecture_productivity, substance_kinetics, productivity_predictors, life_phase, operator_rhythm, weather_correlations...) -> split into standing derivations (L3 automata: baselines, anomaly, episode composites) and one-shot investigations (the recorded-analysis lane — the new abstraction this epic forces, see child bead); (5) graph/context packs -> recall/context (already Sinex's). EXCLUDED for now (operator): codebase analytics (dms/, code_index, code_quality, code_history_claims, tool_rules), thoughtspace (dead end). Existing beads that become this epic's limbs: sinex-44q (source promotion ledger), sinex-rkv.6 (health raw-fidelity), sinex-stm (import glue), sinex-a4w.3.6 (routine.baseline/anomaly), sinex-a4w.3 composites generally.
design: DOCTRINE FOR TRANSLATION (settled): Lynchpin things are not ported line-for-line — each is re-derived from what question it answered. Rule per module: name the question -> classify {standing derivation | one-shot investigation | read model | dead} -> map to {L3 automaton | recorded analysis | query unit/projection | retire}. Raw-data rule: every subsumed source re-lands on ORIGINAL provider data as materials; Lynchpin processed intermediates are never inputs (they are evidence for parity checks only). Parity-then-retire rule: a Lynchpin module turns off only when a named Sinex surface answers its question with an evidence artifact proving parity or superiority; the ledger records question -> lynchpin module -> sinex surface -> parity artifact. Daily-grain rule: cross-domain daily correlation (sleep x productivity, weather x mood) requires the (domain, local-day) rollup lane as query units — the composite plane's daily grain, not ad-hoc NDJSON.
acceptance_criteria: Every non-excluded Lynchpin module has a ledger verdict with evidence; the 'port' rows have child/linked beads; the recorded-analysis abstraction is decided and implemented (child); at least the health+sleep and daily-signals families run natively in Sinex with parity artifacts; Lynchpin runs nothing on a schedule that Sinex now answers.
notes:
2026-07-06: subsumption is now decision-complete — census at .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-06-lynchpin-subsumption-census.md (per-family verdicts + S1-S7 phase order: S1 recorded-analysis lane, S2 daily rollups w/ health-sleep parity, S3 staged-export ports, S4 polylogue family dies via 4j2, S5 devloop analytics via a4w.3.9/k4c.1, S6 evidence-graph parity, S7 MCP-tool parity + repo archive). Operator ruling honored: entire subsumption, no keep-forever verdicts. Cross-epic deps: S3→stm, S4→4j2.1+backfill, S5→a4w.3.9, S7 gates on everything.
2026-07-06 rawlog mining (2026-06-29 00:49 entry, operator's own words): 'might want to port lynchpin via implementing its sources in sinex and hooking these up through lynchpin, delaying the architecturally delicate questions about its higher levels. porting lynchpin sources should be ~trivial... no significant degrees of freedom.' This CONFIRMS the census S3-first order AND adds a transition mechanism the census should carry: an optional S3b shim where lynchpin's substrate materializes FROM SINEX QUERIES instead of raw exports (lynchpin higher layers keep working unmodified while sources move — DuckDB materializers point at sinexctl query/gateway instead of files). Cheap, reversible, kills the big-bang risk.
2026-07-08 completeness audit (operator-requested): the subsumption surface is now FULLY beaded at execution grade — every lynchpin source module maps to a covered-raw contract, an owning bead (rkv.6/.15/.16/.17/.18, rkv.4, rkv.5, k4c.1/.2), or an operator-excluded row. One doctrine violation found and scheduled for deletion (sleep-merged-summary). The raw-data rule holds everywhere else: reddit/spotify/wykop/messenger contracts already read original GDPR/provider exports. Epic exit = k4c.3 ledger complete + the named beads landed + lynchpin schedules nothing sinex now answers.
2026-07-08 operator directive sharpened: subsumption completeness is PURPOSE-coverage, not module-coverage — every purpose a lynchpin structure served must have a sinex-native equivalent that matches or exceeds it (deliberate downgrades allowed with rationale, e.g. narratives). k4c.3 ledger now enforces this via the mandatory purpose column and the zero-gap-rows retirement gate. New general mechanism minted from the sweep: sinex-0eam cross-provider observation fusion (the real purpose behind sleep-merged — witness fusion with per-field provenance, agreement-aware ClaimSupport, and late-witness supersession). The purpose sweep found all other higher-order purposes already owned; the epic's exit criteria now hinge on the purpose-graded ledger.
2026-07-08 GENERALITY RULE (operator, standing): subsumption purposes are answered by GENERAL abstractions that specific event kinds then USE — a bespoke mechanism inside one source's scope is a doctrine violation when a generic tool could carry it. The subsumption toolkit, by purpose: ingest shape -> stm static-import glue + producer protocol (zi6); occurrence identity & re-import -> 908 dedup regimes (never per-source dedup code); interval/session/episode shapes -> the declarative state-evidence engine (4b86: evidence adapters + closure policies + fence table) — sleep sessions, listening sessions, browse sessions are ADAPTERS + POLICY ROWS there, never new interval logic; multi-witness composites -> 0eam fusion declarations; day-grain aggregation -> the k4c.2 parameterized rollup family (never a per-domain rollup automaton); one-shot investigations -> k4c.1 recorded-analysis lane; read models -> 68c projection registry; operator/agent surfaces -> x5mg pack pipeline. Review gate for every subsumption PR: name the generic tool each mechanism instantiates, or justify why none can (a genuinely provider-specific PARSER is fine; a provider-specific MECHANISM is not).

title: Capture-stall alerting: sources must not silently stop capturing (push, not caveat)
description: Today a stalled source surfaces only as a pull-side caveat: the recall-v2 terminal packet itself shipped with browser.history in runtime_binding_stalled, and nobody was notified — the stall was discovered because a demo happened to render caveats. The recall caveat plumbing (source-status gap details, runtime_binding_stalled classification) already computes the right facts; what is missing is the push leg: when a source with an expected cadence stops producing output beyond its family-appropriate threshold, the operator gets an actionable desktop notification (and the fact lands as a reflexive event). This is the capability behind demo sinex-cem.2 (fault injection proves the detector); the demo must not be the only consumer. Distinct from sinex-r6d.3 (honest health = truthful check results) — this is delivery of those results to the operator without being asked.
design: DECISION: reuse existing source-continuity/source-status classification as the single detector (no parallel staleness logic). A small watcher (sinexd-internal tick or systemd timer running sinexctl sources status --format json) evaluates per-source expected-cadence thresholds from the source contract/binding (continuous sources: minutes-hours; scheduled imports: their period + slack); emits notify-send on transition into stalled and on recovery, plus a reflexive event for history. Threshold config lives with the binding, not hardcoded per source. Anti-flap: alert on state transition only.
acceptance_criteria: Killing the browser runtime binding (or the cem.2 fault injection) produces an operator notification within one evaluation period and a queryable stall event; recovery notifies once; no notification storms from a flapping source; thresholds visible in sources status output.

title: Backup/restore drill + CAS integrity scrub: make 'L0 retained forever' a verified claim
description: The doctrine says L0 (source materials + CAS blobs) is retained forever and events are a cache over materials — but nothing verifies that claim. The 2026-07 production incident proved restore had never been executed until forced; it succeeded (sinex-mhk), but as a one-off manual operation. Absent capabilities: (1) automated backup verification — prod pg backup exists per crate/sinex-db/docs/backup_restore.md but no scheduled restore-drill proves backups are restorable; (2) CAS integrity scrub — blobs are BLAKE3 content-addressed, so a scrub walking <stateRoot>/blobs/sinex-cas verifying digest-vs-content and cross-checking against raw.source_material_registry is cheap and detects bitrot/truncation/orphans in both directions; (3) beads/Dolt database inclusion in the backup story. Without this, every replay/eviction/retention design (sinex-qky, sinex-lpz) rests on an unverified premise.
design: DECISION: scrub is a sinexctl ops subcommand (registry-vs-CAS bidirectional check: missing blob, digest mismatch, orphan blob), runnable bounded (--sample N) or full; results are events (reflexive role) so honest-health can consume them. Restore drill is an operator-invoked documented procedure first (script + runbook), scheduled later; drill target is a throwaway DB, never prod. Fold into ops verify as a correctness-class probe per sinex-r6d.3.
acceptance_criteria: sinexctl ops verify (or ops scrub) detects a deliberately corrupted CAS blob and a deleted-but-registered blob in a test store; restore drill runbook executed once end-to-end with row-count proof; backup coverage documented to include the beads Dolt db.

title: Stop devshell xtask launchers rebuilding schema-apply bootstrap repeatedly
description: During repeated xtask test/check/build launches on 2026-07-03, the devshell wrapper rebuilt schema-apply-bootstrap before queueing jobs, even for quick reruns. This adds ~30-60s of serial latency before useful background work starts. Investigate the flake/devshell wrapper path and make schema bootstrap reuse/cache/queue behavior explicit without bypassing SQLx correctness. Evidence jobs around 2001369-2001373 repeatedly printed 'this derivation will be built: ... schema-apply-bootstrap-0.4.2.drv' before job start.
design: DIAGNOSE FIRST (the fix depends on which layer rebuilds): evidence shows 'this derivation will be built: schema-apply-bootstrap-0.4.2.drv' repeatedly — determine whether (a) the drv inputs genuinely change per invocation (e.g. a timestamp/env/store-path leak into the derivation), (b) the wrapper calls nix build without --no-link/reuse so the result is GC'd or not cached, or (c) the wrapper unconditionally rebuilds instead of checking an existing result symlink/profile. FIX SHAPE by branch: (a) pin the leaking input; (b/c) cache the built bootstrap at a stable per-checkout path (.sinex/ or the /var/cache/sinex tree) keyed by the drv hash, and have the wrapper exec the cached result when the hash matches — rebuild only on real input change. HARD CONSTRAINT: never skip SQLx schema correctness — the cache key must cover schema defs; a stale bootstrap running against changed defs is worse than the latency.
acceptance_criteria: Back-to-back xtask check launches show zero 'will be built' schema-bootstrap lines on the second run (cold->warm proven with timestamps); a schema-defs edit DOES trigger rebuild (cache-key correctness); serial pre-job latency for warm launches drops by the measured 30-60s; root cause written in the closing comment (which branch it was).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #2286: devshell cargo/xtask wrappers now cache the schema-apply-bootstrap executable path under SINEX_DEV_STATE_DIR, keyed by a source fingerprint over Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, flake.nix, crate/sinex-schema, and crate/sinex-primitives. Warm in-devshell SQLx-needed xtask launch verified at 1.657s with no schema-apply-bootstrap build line; temporary schema definition edit triggered the rebuild path.

title: Replace recall diversity top-up with bounded shared query
description: The sinex-0tj hardening slice made recall finite and observable, but live proof still shows diversity_top_up consuming its full 8s budget and returning recall.diversity_top_up_timeout. Replace the sequential per-source events.cards loop in crate/sinexctl/src/commands/context.rs with either a bounded concurrent implementation or, preferably, a server-side source-diverse event-card query in the shared events/context substrate. Preserve algebraic query composition; do not add a recall-only silo.
design: DECISION: server-side source-diverse event-card query in the shared events/context substrate (option 2 of the 0tj packet — the bounded-concurrent CLI loop is acceptable only as an interim commit if the server query stalls). Shape: one RPC extending events.cards semantics — given a base filter + a diversity spec (source/family list), return base latest cards PLUS latest card per requested source in one disclosure pass; implemented in the composable-query layer (crate/sinex-db/src/repositories/events/composable_query.rs) as a lateral/union-per-source plan bounded by the existing indexes (ix_events_source_ts_orig), NOT a recall-only handler. CLI top_up_context_diversity (context.rs:456) collapses to one call. Keep stage timings from the 0tj instrumentation; keep the diversity budget caveat for the degraded path. PITFALL: per-source LIMIT 1 over ts_orig on the hypertable is the exact shape g3k is diagnosing — run EXPLAIN on the generated plan before declaring the latency win; if chunk-exclusion kills it, this bead's RPC consolidation still lands (1 RPC vs N) and the plan evidence hands off to g3k.
acceptance_criteria: Recall issues a bounded number of RPCs independent of missing-source count (target: 1 cards RPC + optional source-status); diversity_top_up stage time drops from 8s-budget-exhaustion to sub-second on the dev store with the EXPLAIN plan recorded; composable-query tests cover the diversity spec; no recall-only query silo (the RPC is usable by any events consumer).

title: Emit bounded multi-entity outputs from entity extractor
description: Dead-lane sweep follow-up from sinex-y7l and fixture prerequisite for sinex-a4w.1. entity_extractor currently returns only find_first_entity from a single-output transducer, hiding co-occurring URL/email/file/tool entities in one input event. Move to bounded multi-output extraction or equivalent shared runtime support.
design: DECISION: extend the extraction to bounded multi-output while keeping the Transducer runtime shape — emit ONE entity.extracted event whose payload carries a bounded entities[] array (deterministic order: by kind priority then first-occurrence offset; cap configurable, default ~8 with an overflow count field), rather than N events per input. Rationale: one input -> one interpretation event preserves 1:1 occurrence mapping and avoids multiplying the already-noisy lane (the 2h demo window showed 503 extractor events — one per input; N-per-input would make it the top volume source); consumers and the golden set (a4w.1) score the array. The single-output DerivedNodeImpl constraint (one event_type per node) is untouched — same type, richer payload. Schema: bump the payload schema + semantics_version so replay re-derives history under the new semantics when invoked. PITFALL: keep find_first_entity's kind-priority logic as the ORDER, not the FILTER.
acceptance_criteria: An input containing multiple supported entity kinds emits all bounded matches with deterministic ordering and volume limits. Existing priority/first-match behavior is replaced by tests that assert URL/email/file/tool co-occurrence. Entity golden-set scoring can evaluate all predicted entities. One entity.extracted event per input (bounded entities[] payload), never N events per input; overflow count present when the cap truncates.

title: Fix entity enricher active-hours timezone semantics
description:
Dead-lane sweep follow-up from sinex-y7l. entity_enricher active_hours currently buckets by raw UTC hour, which is a misleading behavioral surface for operator-local activity. Decide and implement either operator-local timezone with explicit metadata or delete the field.

## Steps to Reproduce
Ingest activity spanning a local-vs-UTC hour boundary; entity_enricher active_hours buckets by raw UTC hour, so evening local activity lands in the wrong day/hours for any operator east of UTC.
design: DECISION (settled now per design-now doctrine): operator-local timezone with explicit metadata — not deletion. Rationale: active-hours is exactly the shape routine.baseline (a4w.3.6) consumes, so the field has a named consumer derivation; raw-UTC bucketing is the bug, not the field. Implementation: bucket by the operator's local tz (from config, one authority — the same local-day source k4c.2 standardizes; do NOT invent a second tz-resolution path), stamp the payload with the tz used (tz_id field) so the value is self-describing, bump semantics_version, and replay re-derives history when invoked. While in the file: grep the automata for other raw-UTC-hour bucketing (this is the template instance of the timezone-class sweep noted on the nbi epic).
acceptance_criteria: active_hours histogram computed in operator-local timezone (config/env-provided, not host guess) with explicit tz metadata on the payload; DST-boundary test; entity_enricher docs/derivation_catalog name routine.baseline (a4w.3.6) as the consumer. VERIFY: xtask test -p sinexd -E 'test(enricher)' incl. a fixed-offset + DST case.

title: Classify source contracts by replayability and loss authority
description: Checkpoint-loss matrix follow-up from sinex-r6d.1. The loss-window matrix needs source rows grouped by whether the source is a live observation, mutable snapshot, append stream, staged export, or external producer, and what authority can replay/catch up after a crash.
design: Research packet integrated: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-4.md#sinex-r6d8---source-recovery-policy. Add explicit SourceRecoveryPolicy to SourceRuntimeBinding, not SourceContract, because binding owns deployment/runtime recovery shape: runner_pack, checkpoint_family, runtime_shape, material_lifecycle, and transport_semantics. Make the policy typestate-required in SourceRuntimeBindingBuilder, add a builder method, extend SourceMeta/SourceDefinition attrs, export to source catalog/package completeness/source-status/replay views, and make replay planner/executor reject archive-then-scan replay for non-replayable bindings unless an explicit operator override records degraded recovery. Taxonomy: RetainedMaterial, AppendStream, MutableSnapshot, JournalCursor, ExternalProducer, LiveObservation, DerivedInternal.
acceptance_criteria: Every registered SourceRuntimeBinding has explicit SourceRecoveryPolicy. SourceMeta/SourceDefinition can declare it and manual builders must do so. Package completeness and source-status expose replayability_class, catch_up_authority, and accepted_loss_policy. Non-replayable/live bindings either prove zero silent-loss windows or link fix Beads/accepted-loss records. Replay archive/execution refuses non-replayable source replay before archiving live rows unless explicit operator override records degraded/non-replayable recovery. Existing TransportSemantics.replayable is kept consistent or becomes derived/display context. Docs updated for source package template, evidence lanes, package completeness, and replay control.
notes:
Research packet integrated 2026-07-03: see .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-beads-research-packets-2.md#sinex-r6d8-source-replayability-and-loss-authority. Implementation shape: add required SourceRecoveryPolicy per SourceRuntimeBinding; wire through SourceMeta/builder/package completeness/startup validation; reject non-replayable sources before replay archive; keep declared recovery policy separate from observed continuity scorecards.
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit + systematic-v2 packet 9): replayable:bool cannot carry recovery semantics (TransportSemantics, source_contracts/lifecycle.rs). SourceRecoveryPolicy shape: {replay_mode, replay_horizon, authoritative_raw_store, cursor_commit_contract, loss_authority, negative_absence_authority, accepted_gap_policy, material_required_before_cursor}. Map the immediate actives first (window-manager, activitywatch, atuin, git static, raindrop, self_observation); coverage view uses the policy to decide whether absence may render as covered-zero; the r6d.1 matrix keys crash cases on it.

title: GitHub/CI activity source (API sync): the outcome leg of every work episode
design: Highest leverage-per-effort missing source (S, API-sync shape). Issues/PRs/reviews/CI runs as events — for a development-centric life this is the OUTCOME leg of every work episode; xtask history covers local verification, nothing covers remote. Shape: scheduled API sync (the email provider-sync substrate is the pattern: cursor = updated-since + per-resource ids, rate-limit/backoff via provider runtime state); occurrence identity = (repo, resource kind, resource id, updated_at); ts_orig = the GitHub event timestamp. Start with the operator's own repos; auth via the same secret-deployment boundary as email. Feeds: work.episode, change.episode composites.
acceptance_criteria: Issues/PRs/reviews/CI-run events flowing for the active repos with correct ts_orig; cursor survives restart without duplicates (equivalence keys); rate-limit produces visible provider state, never silent skip; change.episode can join a commit to its PR and CI outcome.

title: work.episode: the unit of resumption (session x attention x git x agent-sessions)
design: RECIPE: session ⊕ attention.stream ⊕ git commits ⊕ agent sessions -> work.episode intervals answering 'what was I doing and did it land'. THE unit the resumption demos score against, and the composite that beads-ingestion enriches with intent labels (bead claim->close intervals). Prereqs: attention.stream (parent composite), git-commit-history flowing in deployment (verify via the static-imports bead), agent-session events (integration.polylogue lane). Episode boundaries = session detector output, refined by attention continuity; outcome leg = commits/PRs in the window.
acceptance_criteria: work.episode events with resolvable parents across >=3 source families; a known real work session reconstructs as one episode with its commits attached; recall can render episodes; the resumption demo scores against these instead of ad-hoc window assembly.
notes:
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §2): episodes from typed segments first (terminal_session, attention_focus, polylogue_work, git_change, browser_research, rawlog_intent), then a windowed merger grouping by project/repo/branch/ref/close-time that PRESERVES splits on conflicting evidence. Fields: {episode_id, interval, title_candidate, project_candidate, intent_basis explicit|inferred|absent, support_segments, verification_refs, unresolved_next_actions, caveats, split_reasons}. Titles are inferred labels, not operator intent, unless backed by rawlog/beads/instruction text. Split test: two repos in one hour must yield two episodes. First slice: context-pack-only candidates for a requested window; no global materialization.
2026-07-07 adversarial hardening: 'episode boundaries = session detector output' inherits every session defect — counter-based equivalence keys (ecy), closure-by-next-arrival (5s6), and no open-episode representation (599). The CURRENT episode (the resumption target) cannot exist as an event until it is over; a first slice over closed sessions is shippable but must state that limitation in its AC honesty matrix, and the resumption construct is only fully valid once 599 lands. Boundary revision behavior binds to the y8v interval-revision ruling.
2026-07-08 subsumption purpose stamp: lynchpin's deep-work blocks purpose lands here as typed episode-quality classification — a declared-constant threshold rule (min uninterrupted attention duration, max interruption tolerance) labeling episodes, never a narrative; the label carries the measured values it derives from.

title: project.attribution: honest time-per-project via entity-join
design: RECIPE: command cwd ⋈ git repo paths ⋈ fs paths ⋈ window titles ⋈ browser context -> per-interval project attribution. The project entity is derivable MECHANICALLY (path prefixes + repo roots) — no LLM needed; this is the first real entity-join composite and will surface missing entity kinds (rule 7: a needed third key = a missing entity). Emits project.attribution interval events over attention.stream output. Ambiguous intervals attribute to nothing with an explicit unattributed share — never force a label.
acceptance_criteria: Per-day project attribution queryable; unattributed share reported honestly; attribution of a known worked-on-repo day sanity-checked against git/terminal ground truth; parents resolve.
notes:
2026-07-07 audit integration (audit §1): project attribution ships as a CANDIDATE projection first, never canonical: {window, candidate_project, support_families [cwd,git_repo,editor_path,bead_ref,polylogue_work_event,browser_title,rawlog_hint], support_refs, counter_refs, coverage_basis, temporal_quality, score_terms (not just total), semantics_version, status candidate|accepted|rejected|stale|insufficient_evidence}. Promotion rule v0: strong = ≥2 independent hard families incl. one of path/git/bead/polylogue-work; ambiguous = conflicting hard families; only-soft-titles = unsupported. Failure mode to test: browser tab 'Sinex' + terminal cwd polylogue → must NOT emit project=sinex. First slice: sinexctl context project-attribution --around <ts> --window 30m, no background automaton.
2026-07-08 (post-ratification design note): pq5-B default-disables the 4-stage entity chain — this bead is UNAFFECTED and should be read as confirmation of its own design. The project entity is derivable MECHANICALLY (path prefixes + repo roots): project.attribution never needed entity-extractor/resolver/relation output. Under the ratified pq2 algebra this composite is an entity-join whose entity kind (project) is produced by a deterministic rule, i.e. DerivedProductClass = canonical_derived_event with ClaimSupport support_level = mechanical, NOT semantic_candidate. Two riders: (1) the time-join half (attribution over attention.stream intervals) must consume only closed-and-settled intervals per the pq2 closure rule — attributing the live open interval is the category error the ruling forbids; (2) when an input interval is later superseded (y8v), attribution outputs re-derive via scope invalidation like any derived descendant — no bespoke recompute. The unattributed share stays an explicit reported quantity (never force a label).

title: interruption.event: notification x focus-change correlation (cheap side-demo)
design: RECIPE: notification event ⊕ focus-change within Δt -> interruption event carrying the source (which app interrupted) and the attention cost (time to return to prior focus, when measurable). Both inputs LIVE. Δt and the return-detection window are declared constants with provenance notes, not magic numbers. Transducer-with-small-window shape.
acceptance_criteria: interruption events flow on the dev store with real notifications; each carries notification + focus-change parents; a no-focus-change notification produces nothing (control); the derived kind is registered per catalog rule 1 (names consumer: attention analytics + recall annotations).

title: Honest health: replace default-true checks and presence-only verify probes
design: PROBLEM (construct-validity inventory): the system's self-assessment conflates liveness with correctness. Health checks default to true (Level 6 convention — a check that measures nothing); ops verify probes assert presence ('automaton emitted something'), not correctness ('output is right'). Both name constructs they do not measure. DESIGN: (1) health checks must compute their verdict from evidence or report unknown — a default-true path becomes a typed Unknown{reason} state rendered distinctly from Healthy (no more free greens); (2) ops verify gains correctness-class probes alongside presence: for each derived family, a cheap invariant check (e.g. session boundaries non-overlapping, daily summary parent-set spans the day, canonical commands parse back) declared next to the automaton; (3) the health aggregator distinguishes liveness / correctness / unknown per component in its report events, so current_health stops laundering unknown into healthy. RULE this operationalizes (doctrine rule 1): every verdict carries evidence-provenance or is absent.
acceptance_criteria: No health path returns a default-true verdict (rg proves; defaults become Unknown); ops verify reports presence and correctness columns per family with at least one real invariant probe per running automaton; current_health view exposes the three-state distinction; a deliberately-broken automaton output flips correctness while liveness stays green (test).
notes:
2026-07-07 audit integration: verified on master — HealthReporter constructs with last_status=Healthy, liveness_ok=true (health_reporter.rs new_with_clock); missing reporter counts as healthy via is_none_or (adapter/mod.rs:494 health_check, :630 exploration view). Fix shape: HealthStatus::Unknown (or HealthVerdict{observed,status}) until first successful probe; missing reporter renders Unknown/Degraded, never green; split liveness/correctness/freshness/probe-ran into separate fields; render Unknown distinctly in TUI/API/CLI.
Accelerant pointer (2026-07-08): execution packet = .agent/scratch/new/sinex_urgent_correctness_systematic_v2.md section 7 (default-healthy -> Unknown/Unproven). Line anchors in the packet are from a drifted snapshot — re-verify against master on claim (021 protocol).
close_reason: Merged sinex/#2418: HealthReporter::calculate_status() reports Unknown until real evidence exists (has_evidence() gate on events_processed>0 or a completed liveness probe), last_status inits to Unknown, and the two is_none_or(missing-reporter-is-healthy) sites in adapter/mod.rs now use is_some_and. Verified: automata/health.rs's aggregator was already correct (excludes never-reported components, computes Unknown overall status) — this fix feeds it sound leaf data rather than requiring aggregator changes. Deliberately did NOT touch heartbeat.rs (investigated the identical-looking pattern, found 3 existing tests encode zero-errors-across-N-ticks=healthy as intentional; a heartbeat's existence is itself liveness evidence unlike HealthReporter's zero-effort error_rate()). Residual/deferred: ops-verify correctness-class probes per automaton family (a separate, larger feature — not started). AC honesty: 'no default-true health path' and 'three-state distinction exposed' are satisfied; 'ops verify gains correctness-class probes... at least one real invariant probe per running automaton' is NOT done. Verify: xtask test -p sinexd --lib -E 'test(health_reporter) | test(heartbeat)' -> 16 passed.

title: Demo: reconstruct my Tuesday — full-day timeline with provenance to bytes and honest gaps
design: CLAIM: a full-day timeline with provenance to bytes and honest absence. PROTOCOL: interleaved sessions/commands/pages/docs for one real day where every row resolves via show <ref> down to source-material bytes, and capture outages render as EXPLICIT gaps from source-continuity data — displayed, not footnoted. INSTRUMENT: random-sample K rows (K>=10, sampled by script not by hand) and trace each to raw bytes; the gap rendering is the anti-cherry-picking control. FALSIFIER: any sampled row fails byte-trace, or a known outage window renders smooth. PREREQ: recall truthfulness (sinex-vhm) + continuity caveats (landed). Distinct from the campaign (sinex-9j9): the campaign proves a bounded work-window with a baseline arm; this proves breadth (full day, all families) and drilldown depth. CAPABILITY LANDING: the random-sample byte-trace script becomes a standing recall-integrity check.
acceptance_criteria: One full real day rendered; K script-sampled rows each traced to source bytes in the artifact; at least one genuine outage window shown as a gap; one-command regeneration; complete summary.json.

title: Citation-required daily brief: sinexctl report daily with machine-checkable refs
design: DESIGN: sinexctl report daily <date> — a narrative brief over activity.summary.daily + session boundaries + git commits + docs/pages, where EVERY bullet embeds event refs that resolve via show <ref> (the oracle digest pattern, upgraded with machine-checkable citations). Structure: sections per family, each bullet = statement + [ref, ref]; a --verify mode re-resolves every embedded ref and fails the brief on any dangling citation (the construct-validity gate built into the feature). Honest gaps: source-continuity caveats render as explicit absent-source lines. Reuse: the recall/context card + caveat substrate (sinex-vhm) and query units — this is a composition, not a new query silo; if it needs a primitive recall lacks, add the primitive there. LLM-narrated prose is optional and comes later (would be an L3 effect with a ModelEffectRecord); the first version is template-rendered — no model dependency.
acceptance_criteria: report daily produces a brief where every bullet's refs resolve (—verify green); disabling a source renders an explicit gap line; refs sample-traced to source bytes; JSON output stable for MCP; zero raw SQL outside the shared query substrate. Small enough to demo the same week recall truthfulness lands.
notes: 2026-07-08: 'today' in the daily brief is a civil-time construct — boundaries per the civil-time contract (2ged), not UTC floors (current hourly/daily automata are UTC-bucketed by declaration).

title: Projection mechanism unification: migrate documents off the DB trigger (operator-decided)
design: DECISION (operator, 2026-07-03): documents MIGRATE off the trigger path. The standing rule this establishes: consumer-maintained projections (event-engine post-persist hooks or dedicated consumers on the confirmed stream) are THE read-model mechanism for product surfaces; TimescaleDB CAs remain for telemetry rollups only (and move to the reflection table per the telemetry-lane design); DB triggers are retired as a projection mechanism. TARGET: core.fn_document_projection() + trg_document_projection on core.events (crate/sinex-schema/src/defs/documents.rs:292-345) — an AFTER-INSERT trigger folding document.ingested/document.chunked events into core.documents/core.document_chunks. MIGRATION: (1) implement a document-projection consumer on the confirmed stream (same site/pattern as the recall.timeline hook design in the latency bead — build them on one hook mechanism, that is the point); (2) declare it as a DerivationSpec with rebuild + invalidation (replay/redaction), which the trigger version cannot express; (3) backfill/verify parity against the trigger-maintained tables; (4) DROP the trigger + function and remove their schema-apply emission (also shrinks the gh#2182 boot-lock surface — one fewer unconditional CREATE FUNCTION/TRIGGER at boot). WHY the trigger is the odd one out: it is invisible to the derivation registry (no DerivationSpec, no rebuild operation, no invalidation semantics), runs inside the insert transaction (couples ingest latency to projection cost), and is exactly the class of unconditional DDL the boot-race fix has to guard around.
acceptance_criteria: Document projection maintained by a confirmed-stream consumer with DerivationSpec + rebuild op; parity proven against trigger output on the same input set before cutover; trigger + function dropped from defs and from live DBs via convergence; doc search behavior unchanged (existing document tests green); one projection mechanism documented as the standing rule in the schema design docs.
notes: 2026-07-07 overlap reconciliation: sinex-68c.2 (wave:4) registers the document projection in derivation.projection_registry with readiness caveats; THIS bead (wave:9) changes the maintenance mechanism (DB trigger -> confirmed-stream consumer). Sequencing: 68c.2 may register the trigger-maintained projection as-is (status/fingerprint honest about the mechanism); when nbi.3 lands, the registry row's semantics_version/verification_command update. The operator decision recorded here (consumer-maintained projections are THE read-model mechanism; CAs only for telemetry) is standing doctrine for 68c/0vx.4 schema work — do not design the registry around trigger-maintained projections as the long-term shape. Also note: document_chunks ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING staleness (privacy audit) is another reason the trigger path retires.

title: Golden recall/context fixture with stable occurrence labels (supersedes entity-only golden set)
description: Versioned recall-context fixture: ~40 resumption questions over bounded ts_orig windows producing ~200 gold items with relevance grades (0-3), roles, stable occurrence identities, gap expectations, negative assertions, and salience judgments. The measurement substrate for cem.5 (pgvector decision), cem.7 (resumption duel), uxd (salience), and pq5 (dark-entity verdict). Full protocol + JSON Schema (sinex.recall_golden_set/v1) in .agent/scratch/new-gpt-pro/sessions/06-recall-quality-eval. Reframed 2026-07-07 from the entity-only golden set; the entity-extraction baseline survives as a sub-fixture.
design: QUESTION: what is the measured P/R of the current 4-regex entity baseline — and what fixture will every future lane comparison score against? INSTRUMENT: hand-label ~200 events (entities + relations) sampled across sources, LABELED BEFORE any lane runs (pre-registration); score the current extractor against it. ARTIFACT: the committed golden set + the baseline P/R number. PURPOSE: the prerequisite instrument for the semantic-lane duel demo and all LLM-lane work — without it the A/B harness has nothing valid to measure against. Also becomes the standing monthly drift-check epoch fixture (U9 class 4).
acceptance_criteria: Committed recall_golden_v1 fixture: >=40 resumption questions producing >=200 labeled gold items across strata — 50% high-signal work windows, 25% gap/edge windows, 15% sparse/single-source, 10% negative controls; source families terminal/browser/filesystem/git/desktop/activitywatch + derived attention/interval/session. Fixture uses JSON Schema sinex.recall_golden_set/v1 (full schema in new-gpt-pro sessions/06) recording store epoch, repo commit, source-coverage snapshot, label protocol version, and generator exclusions. Labels committed BEFORE any retrieval/pack/salience/duel runs (git history proves precedence). Every grade-2/3 item has stable identity: source-material occurrence identity when available, else derived equivalence_key + parent refs, else explicit manual key with rationale — never bare event ids (replay mints new ids). Gap expectations labeled against the caveat vocabulary (source.absent, window.partial, coverage.unmeasurable, derivation.lane_not_promoted, recall.session_rows_absent, recall.attention_lineage_timeout). Annotation protocol: 8-12-item or 20-minute batches, first-class defer reasons, 10-15% hidden duplicates, sentinel windows, adjudication flow; consistency report emits weighted kappa, Jaccard, salience-rank correlation, defer rate. The entity-extraction baseline this bead originally described is preserved as a separate sub-fixture (still unblocks lane duels / pq5) and is explicitly NOT sufficient evidence for recall/context quality.
notes:
2026-07-03 subagent packet integrated: full sampling protocol, label schema, fixture paths, and baseline scorer shape live at .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-subagent-beads-packets.md#sinex-a4w1---entity-golden-set. The issue is executable now: export deterministic stratified corpus from core.events, sanitize to input_text, commit golden_v1 before lane runs, add scorer, then record baseline_v1.json and P/R.
2026-07-07 reframe (new-gpt-pro sessions/06): recall quality needs query/window/gap/span/salience labels, not only entity labels. Sampling on ts_orig windows (ts_orig answers what happened). Relevance grades 0-3; only 2-3 count for hit/coverage; grade-1 for ranking; grade-0 for distractor metrics.

title: Analysis: checkpoint-loss-window matrix (the data-loss close-out instrument)
design: Executable issue packet written: .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-sinex-r6d-1-checkpoint-loss-window.md. Close by driving five non-zero rows to zero/accepted: source cursor durable handoff (sinex-r6d.4), recovery spool no-discard quarantine (sinex-r6d.5), confirmed-event retention-gap catch-up or non-discarding retention (sinex-r6d.6), invalidation no ack-before-recompute/checkpoint or remove legacy scan path (sinex-r6d.7), and source replayability/loss authority policy (sinex-r6d.8). Verification carrier: crash-window harness sinex-r6d.9 plus packet-listed xtask commands.
acceptance_criteria: Matrix covers all sources + all 14 automata + the transport spool; every non-zero row links a fix bead or an explicit acceptance; the epic's close cites this artifact.
notes:
2026-07-03 subagent packet integrated: full matrix design and code-reference map lives at .agent/scratch/research/2026-07-03-subagent-beads-packets.md#sinex-r6d1---checkpoint-loss-window-matrix. Executable follow-ups are being created under sinex-r6d for every likely non-zero loss row: source durable-before-checkpoint, spool overflow, confirmed-event catch-up, invalidation ack ordering, replayability classification, and crash-window harness.
2026-07-07 audit integration: seed matrices exist at .agent/scratch/new/sinex_loss_window_matrix_v2.csv and _prefill.csv (12 rows: area, window, refs, risk, required invariant, owning bead). Line numbers are from a stale archive — re-cite against master via .agent/scratch/021-grok-2026-07-07-audit-verification.md. The emission-barrier row maps to the new sinex-r6d.11 bead.
2026-07-07 GPT-Pro batch adds to the matrix: NEW non-zero row — event-engine multi-event-intent per-child settlement (sinex-r6d.12; consumer-side loss, distinct from producer rows). The 18-scenario killpoint CSV in .agent/scratch/new-gpt-pro/01-batch-a-receipt-red-team.report.md section C is a ready row-source (each row = kill location file:line + expected post-restart state). AUDITED-CLEAN surfaces (02-event-engine audit, do not re-audit without cause): single-event confirm->ack ordering (confirmation.rs:128-162, persist.rs:135-231); raw-event DLQ publish-before-ACK (dlq.rs:213-238); pull-loop settlement delegation (run_loop.rs:286-313); RecentIdCache restart (prefilter only, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING backstop); COPY-vs-QueryBuilder routing (except the wv7 chunk-boundary defect); material assembler END-WAL durability, finalization TX boundaries, begin-registration reconciliation, slice ordering.
2026-07-07 new matrix row: counter-based equivalence-key collision (sinex-ecy) — replay/checkpoint-reset restarts window/session counters, colliding keys with unrelated live rows; admission dedup then silently suppresses fresh derived events. This is a SUPPRESSION loss class (not crash-window): no crash needed, fail-open dedup does the losing.

title: Demo: hybrid retrieval hit@k — justify or kill pgvector with one number
description: Wave 3 — cheap once the worker exists; second of the LLM-plane pair.
design: CLAIM: embeddings add measurable recall over FTS on real personal history. PROTOCOL: PRE-REGISTER 30 'find the moment where I ...' queries with known target events from the operator's own history (pre-registration = the validity discipline; targets fixed before any retrieval runs); report hit@5 for FTS-only vs hybrid on the same store. INSTRUMENT: hit@k table, both arms. FALSIFIER: built into the design — hybrid <= FTS kills the pgvector investment honestly, and that outcome is as publishable internally as a win. PREREQ: sinex-5v6. CAPABILITY LANDING: the pre-registered query set is committed as a reusable retrieval-regression fixture; the two-arm runner becomes a docs-search eval command.
acceptance_criteria: Experiment descriptor cem5_pgvector_hybrid_retrieval committed before runs: fixtures, arms (fts_only vs hybrid_pgvector; vector_only diagnostic-only), frozen embedding model, store epoch, stopping rule. >=30 preregistered retrieval queries labeled before execution; sequential looks every 10 up to 80. Primary metric paired delta hit@5; secondary hit@1/hit@10/MRR/nDCG@10/first-hit-rank/latency/citation-resolvability. Paired bootstrap (10k resamples, 95% CI over query_id) + discordance table. KEEP pgvector only if delta hit@5 >= +10pp absolute AND lower 95% CI >= +5pp AND no citation-resolvability regression. KILL/de-scope if upper 95% CI <= 0 after n>=30 or max-n finishes below the practical threshold.
notes: 2026-07-07: schema/repos already carry pgvector embeddings + HNSW + RRF hybrid search while the embedding producer emits receipts, not vectors — the decision experiment is the gate for keeping that surface. Runs through cem.12 substrate.

title: Demo: run-twice-costs-once (deterministic LLM effect ledger)
description: Wave 3 — the demo that separates Sinex from every pipe-your-life-into-GPT project. First of the LLM-plane pair.
design: CLAIM: LLM derivation is replayable provenance, not an oracle call. PROTOCOL: run an LLM lane (embedding worker) over a corpus; rerun the identical lane -> 100% effect-cache hits, $0 marginal cost, byte-identical derived outputs; bump the prompt/model version -> ONLY affected effects recompute and the budget report shows the exact delta. INSTRUMENT: ModelEffectRecord ledger + llm budget-report (non-zero for the first time). FALSIFIER: cache misses on the identical rerun, or nondeterministic outputs (same key, different output = the U9 class-3 alarm). PREREQ: sinex-5v6 (embedding worker). CAPABILITY LANDING: the rerun-vs-delta comparison becomes an llm effects diff command.
acceptance_criteria: Ledger shows 100% hits on rerun; version bump recomputes exactly the affected subset with the delta in budget-report; outputs byte-identical on hit; one-command rerun; complete summary.json.

title: Demo: retroactive bug-fix over months of history (replay as the product)
description: Wave 2 — the deepest single differentiator; no comparable system can show this. Doubles as acceptance evidence for the replay crash-window fix.
design: CLAIM: interpretations are disposable, evidence is permanent. PROTOCOL: fix a REAL parser defect (live candidates: journald salience, browser title parsing), replay that source across months of material through the normal pipeline. INSTRUMENT: machine-checked invariants, all asserted by script: ts_orig stable across replay; new id + new ts_coided on every replaced interpretation; archive count == replaced count; zero orphaned derived events; occurrence identity (source_material_id, anchor_byte) unchanged. FALSIFIER: any invariant fails — which is exactly what the open replay-crash bug predicts, so this demo IS that fix's acceptance test. PREREQ: sinex-afd closed (hard block). ENHANCEMENT once as-of lands (sinex-a11): show recall over the same window as-of pre-fix vs post-fix — the belief diff as the presentation layer. CAPABILITY LANDING: the invariant-checker script becomes a replay verification command (ops replay verify), not a demo-local assertion pile.
acceptance_criteria: Real defect fixed (not synthetic); replay across >=3 months of material; all five invariants asserted green by a committed script; before/after interpretation shown for at least one concrete record; one-command rerun against the dev store; complete summary.json.

title: Recall salience scoring: every term measured, breakdown in the DTO
description: Recall (sinex-vhm) needs ranking beyond family round-robin, and doctrine forbids unexplained numbers. This implements the honest scoring design from handoff U4. Sequence after the truthfulness slice (sinex-vhm) so scores rank real derived-plane rows, not the degenerate heuristic.
design: DESIGN (settled, from handoff U4): score(card) = w_r*recency + w_x*rarity + w_e*effort + w_a*anchor, with family-diversity as a re-rank pass (not a score term). Every term is a measured quantity: recency = exp-decay on ts_orig with half-life = the MEASURED session-gap median (not a magic number); rarity = -log(frequency of the canonical form in a trailing 30d window) computable from command_frequency_hourly CA + per-family equivalents; effort = dwell/duration evidence (session length share, fs edit churn); anchor = boundary bonus (session start/end, first-seen entity). Weights: fixed documented constants initially; the DTO carries per-term contributions (salience: {recency: .41, rarity: .32, ...}) so the number has provenance — doctrine rule 1. Calibration loop later via the predictions domain (operator marks rows that did/didn't matter).
acceptance_criteria: Recall rows carry a salience breakdown whose terms are each traceable to a measured quantity; removing a term's evidence (e.g. no session data) drops the term, never fabricates it; ranking is stable and explainable in the JSON output; a doc comment names each constant and its provenance. VERIFY: unit tests on the scorer with synthetic distributions; live recall output inspection.
notes: 2026-07-07 eval harness defines the salience acceptance experiment (uxd_salience_ranking_v1): reverse-chronological baseline vs measured-salience arm; primary nDCG@10 with lower 95% CI >= +0.05 AND salience_term_nonfabrication == 1.0 (any fabricated term kills); every displayed salience contribution must trace to measured evidence and removing the evidence must remove/reduce the term.

title: Devloop and meta plane
description: The devloop-and-meta plane: the loop that builds Sinex must itself be honest, fast, and observable. Owns: task-substrate integrity (beads as the shared source of truth), verification trustworthiness (impact planner correctness — a false skip is a lie the loop tells itself), dev-infra friction (anything adding serial seconds to every iteration), and loop observability (the evidence a future agent needs to resume cold).
design: LIMBS by function — VERIFICATION TRUST: 1p2.2 (impact planner staged-hunk blindness + evidence staleness — fix before relying on narrowed test runs), 1p2.1 (dead-lane reachability lint). FRICTION: guw (schema-bootstrap rebuild tax on every launch), 1ul (5432 collision blocks concurrent lanes), jvs (2.5min/7GiB single-test compile tax). OBSERVABILITY: 8zy (JetStream state into xtask analytics store — the SQL-blind-spot lesson), pke (connect disconnected verification machinery). SUBSTRATE: hlv (beads+devloop as sources; ownership boundaries settled), 06d (schema-bundle drift in pre-push). INTEGRATION HYGIENE: keep the reconcile lesson — the ahead-train cost compounds per devloop pass; PR flow is restored (uzl closed), keep it. SEQUENCING: 1p2.2 and guw first — they tax or corrupt every single loop iteration; everything else is per-occasion.
acceptance_criteria: Exit criteria: impact planner produces no false skips on the known vectors (staged changes, stale evidence) with regression tests; a second concurrent checkout/agent lane works without infra collision; a cold agent can reconstruct loop state from bd + committed artifacts alone; JetStream reality visible in xtask analytics.

title: Capture breadth and quality
description: Finish and deploy the capture surface: dark finished sources into deployment, attention-source data quality, the media and email runtime tiers, and the new predictions domain. Cheap breadth first (static-import glue), quality where it blocks cross-source value (attention sources).
design: DOCTRINE: breadth via the generic lane, quality gated before joining composites, portfolio pruned by decision not drift. LIMBS: stm (the scheduled static-import glue — cheapest breadth, unlocks the 21 dark contracts); 2bk (trust the deployment path first); 60r (quality gate: attention sources currently ingest garbage — blocks attention.stream); new sources rkv.1 (GitHub/CI — outcome leg), rkv.2 (nix generations), rkv.3 (browser content — opens topics), rkv.4 (calendar — the only planned-time capture), rkv.5 (mobile export bridges), rkv.6 (physiological — RAW provider data only), rkv.7 (satellite hosts — capture topology expansion); di7 (email runtime — opens people) and xv1 (media/OCR) are sibling epics feeding the same role table. GOVERNANCE: 5j8 (capture-domain portfolio decision) prunes; u3n (stall alerting) keeps deployed sources honest; the derivation catalog role table is the coverage scoreboard — a new source must name the derivation it feeds (extension rule 1).
